puzzleDrag2 — Icon & Tile Audit + Fix Status

910 items reviewed; 337 flagged for fixes. Fixing worst-first (broken → major → minor), player-facing before unused. Each fix is rendered and adversarially verified. Before = audit render; After = post-fix render (animations play in-page).

—
 weather_fog
Vector icon (still) unusedweather
 broken pending This is a vertical stack of fuzzy grey horizontal streaks with no recognizable form; it does not read as fog so much as a render glitch or motion blur. It is pushed hard to the right, sits below center, and the bars clip the right magenta bound while leaving the left half empty. Replace with a centered, clearly readable fog motif (e.g. soft overlapping horizontal cloud/mist bands with defined edges), centered in the box and not clipping the bounds. —
ore_gold_nugget
Vector icon (animation) player-facingores
 broken verified VERIFIED BUG (instrumented): animGoldNugget has 3 ctx.save() but only 2 ctx.restore() — the outer save (with translate(0,-5)) is never restored. On a reused canvas (the strip here AND the live Dev Panel ticker, which does not reset the transform per tick) the +1 save leaks the translate, so each frame shifts down ~5px and the icon walks off its cell. On a fresh canvas it renders ~3400 opaque px every frame, so the art itself is fine. Balance the stack: add the missing ctx.restore() at the end of animGoldNugget (or drop the unneeded outer ctx.save()). netSaveDelta should be 0. fixed unbalanced save/restore (was BROKEN: leaked transform drifted nugget off-cell after frame 1) — all 14 frames now render in-cell
—
 ach_ability_artisan
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 major pending The central emblem is muddy and ambiguous: two crossed tan shapes (calipers/legs?) tipped by a small purple sphere read as a stick-figure or scissors rather than 'artisan'. The gold rim, glossy gradient and starred ribbon are clean and match the family, but the subject does not convey craftsmanship. Replace the central glyph with a clearer artisan symbol (e.g. a hammer-and-chisel or a glowing crafted gem) with a confident silhouette so it reads instantly at small size. —
—
 ach_first_steps
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 major pending The central emblem is ambiguous: two grey curling shapes that could be footprints, links, or a figure-8, and they read as muddy grey scribbles rather than 'first steps'. The bronze rim, gloss and one-star ribbon are clean and tier-consistent, but the subject fails the instant-read test. Swap the glyph for an unmistakable pair of footprints (clear sole + toe shapes) with stronger contrast against the disc so it reads as steps at a glance. —
—
 ach_herder
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 major pending The emblem is a confusing cluster of overlapping dark navy rings/loops with a small brown animal head poking out on the right; it reads as an abstract knot rather than a herd of animals. Low internal contrast (dark navy on grey) makes it muddy and hard to parse at small size. The silver rim and ribbon are fine, but the subject fails readability. Replace with a clear small group of animal silhouettes (e.g. a few sheep heads) or a shepherd's crook so 'herder' reads at a glance, and raise contrast against the disc. —
—
 ach_powerful_keep
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 major pending The emblem is a sharp navy-and-cream four-pointed compass/north star, which does not read as a 'keep' (fortress/castle) at all — subject accuracy is off. Craft of the star and medal is clean and well-centered, so the failure is purely conceptual. It also risks confusion with navigation/compass-type icons elsewhere. Replace the compass star with a castle keep / tower / battlement silhouette so the icon matches 'Powerful Keep'. —
—
 ach_supply_chain
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 major pending The emblem reads as a wooden barrel or crate with a small gold disc, not as a 'supply chain' (linked chain or logistics network). Subject accuracy is poor and the brown barrel is also easily confused with other storage/cargo icons. The medal craft itself is fine. Use linked chain-links or a stack-of-crates-with-arrow motif so the icon reads as a supply chain rather than a single barrel. —
—
 ach_trusted_friend
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 major pending The bronze emblem is a striped almond/eye shape over a darker band that reads ambiguously as an eye, a mouth, or muddy clasped hands — it does not clearly say 'friend'. Bronze-emblem-on-bronze-medal gives weak contrast so the shape is hard to parse, and the medal surface looks slightly muddy. It also duplicates the 'Village Voice' emblem. Swap to a clear two-figures / handshake / heart motif and raise emblem-vs-medal contrast so it reads at small size. —
—
 arcane_pentacle
Vector icon (animation) player-facingarcane
 major pending This is a one-shot build-on reveal: it starts as an almost empty dark disc, the ring and five-point star progressively assemble, ending fully drawn and brightly glowing. As a flipbook it is animated and the final symbol looks good, but it is a draw-in intro, not a loop — the bright completed last frame will hard-cut back to the empty first frame. Early frames are nearly blank so a frozen first frame would show almost nothing. Convert to a looping idle (e.g. the completed pentacle slowly pulses/rotates) or have it draw in then fade back out so first and last frames match. —
—
 astral_shooting_star
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 major pending Frames 3-5 show a strong fiery comet streak and a blown-out warm flash, but it happens once early and then the rest of the strip is just a static four-point star sitting still for ~9 frames. That makes it read as a one-shot burst, not a loop, and the bright flash at frames 3-4 is a near-blown-out glow artifact. The end state (plain star) is far from the start, so it pops hard when looping. Make the streak recur periodically across the loop (or have the star travel and re-enter) and tame the white flash; ensure first and last frames match so it loops without the dead static tail. —
—
 axe
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 major pending The head is a solid faceted hexagonal grey block with no cutting edge or bit, so it reads as a maul/hammer rather than an axe. Wooden handle and shading are clean and the framing is good, but subject accuracy is wrong for the label. Reshape the head into a clear single-bit axe blade with a visible curved cutting edge and a slimmer poll, instead of a symmetric block. —
—
 bond_8_arc
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 major pending A glossy pink heart overlaid with a gold key and a small four-point sparkle top-right — a sensible 'bond unlocked' motif — but it is badly off-centre, parked in the lower-left quadrant with more than half the design box empty above and to the right. The sparkle floating far top-right makes the composition feel scattered rather than balanced. The heart and key art themselves are clean, but framing is broken. Re-centre the heart+key as one group on the crosshair and pull the sparkle in close to the top-right of the heart so the icon fills the box. —
—
 boon_branch_drive_out
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 major pending Three hollow dark triangles mounted on a brown forked stake over a blue glow read as a trident/candelabra, not as a 'drive-out' branch choice; the subject is ambiguous and disconnected from its sibling coexist tree. The hollow-triangle motif lacks fill and looks unfinished against the family's solid glossy style. The blue radial glow is also inconsistent with other boon icons. Redesign to clearly convey 'drive out' (e.g. an arrow/boot pushing a creature away) and adopt the family's solid glossy fills instead of hollow outlines. —
—
 bug_dragonfly
Vector icon (still) unusedcritters
 major pending This is the weakest of the family: a teal head with two eyes is legible, but the body and wings collapse into a dark muddy horizontal blob with very low internal contrast, so it does not instantly read as a dragonfly at small size. The long thin shape almost reads like a sewing needle or feather rather than the classic crossed-wings dragonfly silhouette. On the dark background it nearly disappears. Re-pose with clearly spread, lighter translucent wings crossing the body and add contrast/lighten the abdomen so the dragonfly silhouette reads at a glance. —
—
 bug_firefly
Vector icon (still) unusedcritters
 major pending The firefly concept is there (dark beetle head with red nose/eye, antennae, glowing abdomen), but the enormous radial green glow dominates the icon, clips well past the magenta design box, and pushes the actual bug off-center to the left. The glow reads more as a generic light orb than a firefly, and on the light background the soft glow is weak/washed. Shrink and tighten the glow so it stays inside the box, re-center the bug, and make the glow read as the lit tail of the abdomen rather than a dominant background orb. —
—
 bug_ladybug
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcritters
 major pending The first roughly five frames show the elytra split open revealing the face/underwings, then it snaps to a solid red spotted dome that stays essentially frozen for the entire back half. It plays as a one-shot reveal, not a loop, so the second half is static and the wrap-around is a hard jump from closed dome back to open shell. The underlying drawing is clean but the animation does not cycle. Make it a continuous gentle open-close cycle (ease the shell open and back) so the frozen dome half disappears and the loop seam is smooth. —
—
 choice_coexist_drive
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 major pending The split-disc (green vine half vs a white spade/trowel half) is cryptic and does not clearly communicate 'coexist vs drive-out'; the right-side white object reads more like a trowel or knife than an eviction action. The two halves are stylistically mismatched and the icon feels conceptually muddy. It is a removal-candidate, which fits its weak read. If kept, redesign with two clearly opposed legible symbols (e.g. a sheltered creature vs a creature being pushed out); otherwise proceed with removal. —
—
 crop_watermelon
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcrops
 major pending Frame 1 shows a cut red-flesh wedge beside the melon, but every subsequent frame drops the wedge to a plain whole green-striped melon that then sits essentially static for the rest of the strip. That makes frame 1 a hard pop/teleport against the rest, and frames 2-14 are effectively unanimated. The melon art itself is nice, but the loop has a jarring jump and there is no real motion. Remove or keep the cut wedge consistently across all frames, and add an actual idle (gentle bob or sheen sweep) so it animates instead of holding still after a popping first frame. —
—
 cur_heirloom
Vector icon (still) unusedcurrencies
 major pending A round gold pendant bearing a dark heart, suspended from a thin bent gold rod/branch — the heart reads but the bent-stick hanger is awkward and ambiguous (looks like a broken twig or coat-hanger). The hanger arm clips the top magenta bound and the whole piece hangs well below center, leaving the box poorly filled. The dark heart on dark-gold has weak internal contrast. Drop or redesign the hanger into a clear chain/loop, re-center the pendant in the box, and lighten the heart or add a rim so it reads at small size. —
—
 dish_sandwich
Vector icon (still) unuseddishes
 major pending The art is clean and well-crafted (sesame bun, lettuce, tomato, cheese, patty with good light and outline), but it unmistakably depicts a hamburger, not the labelled 'Sandwich'. This is a subject-accuracy mismatch: a sandwich should read as bread slices with fillings, not a round sesame bun. Craft and framing are fine; the problem is what it depicts. Either redraw as an actual sandwich (square/triangular bread slices with visible fillings) to match the label, or rename the resource to 'Burger'/'Hamburger' so art and label agree. —
—
 drink_milk
Vector icon (animation) player-facingdrinks
 major pending Across all 14 frames the bottle, red cap, and milk level look identical; the only thing changing is the alternating light/dark split background, so it reads as effectively frozen. There is no surface ripple, level bob, or shine to sell motion. The bottle drawing itself is clean with a tidy crimped cap. Add an actual animated element such as a milk-surface ripple, a gentle level sway, or a moving glass highlight so the icon is genuinely animated. —
—
 expedition_pack
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 major pending The olive-green satchel with a brown top flap and a small gold latch is recognisable as a pack, but it is rendered so dark and low-contrast that on the dark half it nearly disappears into the background. Subject accuracy is weak too: it reads more like a flat briefcase than rugged 'pack provisions', and there is a muddy pillow-shaded look across the body. Lighten and warm the palette, add a clear highlight and a brighter rim/outline so it survives on dark backgrounds, and add straps/buckles to sell it as a provisions pack rather than a briefcase. —
—
 festive_holly
Vector icon (still) unusedfestive
 major pending Badly off-centre: the sprig sits high and left, leaving the entire bottom-right of the design box empty, and its drop shadow is detached far below the art. The leaves are rendered as faceted angular crystals rather than holly foliage, so it reads more as a green gemstone than holly; only the three red berries hint at the subject. It is also much smaller than its festive siblings, breaking family scale consistency. Recentre the sprig on the box and enlarge it to match sibling scale, reattach the shadow beneath it, and redraw the leaves as recognisable serrated holly leaves with veining instead of crystalline facets. —
—
 fish_oil_bottled
Vector icon (still) player-facingrecipes
 major pending The bottle itself reads fine (amber/yellow oil, dark cork stopper, a wired label tag), but the whole subject is badly off-centre — it sits in the lower-right quadrant well below the crosshair, leaving the top-left of the design box empty. The fish identity is also not conveyed; without the label it's just a generic oil bottle. Recentre the bottle on the box centre and scale it up to fill the frame; add a small fish silhouette on the label or a tiny fish motif so it reads as fish oil specifically. —
—
 furn_bed
Vector icon (still) unusedfurniture
 major pending The bed barely reads — a single tall brown headboard post sits beside a green quilted mound and a separate lilac pillow that looks like a sleeping creature, not bedding. There is no clear mattress/frame silhouette, so at glance it is an ambiguous cluster rather than a bed. It also clips the left magenta bound and is the weakest, least cohesive icon in the family. Redraw as a clear side-on bed: long horizontal mattress + pillow at one end + a low headboard/footboard, drop the floating green mound and recolour the pillow to a soft neutral so it doesn't read as a face. —
—
 gold_pick
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsMine
 major pending The head is a flat horizontal hexagonal bar with no points or curved spike, so on a diagonal wooden handle it reads as a gold mallet/hammer rather than a pick. It is also nearly identical in silhouette to the silver 'pickaxe' sibling, differing only by colour, which fails the distinctness test. Craft itself (gold gradient, handle shading) is clean and well framed. Give the head an actual pick profile (one pointed spike, or a curved point plus flat poll) so it reads as a pickaxe and is distinct from the hammer-headed siblings. —
—
 harpoon
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 major pending The wide flat metal blade head at an angle on a roped wooden haft reads far more as an axe/hatchet than a barbed harpoon spear. A harpoon should be a long straight shaft ending in a narrow barbed point, not a broad chopping head. The craft (metallic highlight, rope binding) is fine but the subject is wrong. Redraw as a long straight shaft tipped with a narrow barbed/forked spearhead so it reads as a harpoon, not an axe. —
—
 hound
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 major pending It reads as a dog (floppy ear, pale belly, curled tail with white tip), but a bright red circle with a yellow dot sits on the shoulder/chest like a stray fill or misplaced collar tag and is very distracting. The body is also lumpy and slightly overlong for a clean hound silhouette. Remove or rework the red/yellow chest blob (make it a proper collar/tongue or delete it) and tighten the body proportions. —
—
 instr_horn
Vector icon (still) unusedinstruments
 major pending The gold curved horn with a flared brown bell reads fine on its own, but a disconnected dark maroon C-curve floats below/right of it with no visible connection, reading as a broken or stray element rather than part of the instrument. This hurts readability and looks like a rendering artifact. Otherwise the gold body has nice highlights and a clean outline. Either connect the maroon arc to the horn as a clearly-read coil/strap or remove it; tighten the silhouette into one continuous horn shape. —
—
 iron_ration
Vector icon (still) player-facingmissingItems
 major pending It reads as a filled bun or sandwich (tan top bun, dark brown filling with seed dots, on a wooden crate/board) — the blue 'I' badge is the only thing tying it to 'iron'. As a 'ration' the food read is plausible but generic, and the floating serif-I medallion looks like a UI sticker pasted on rather than part of the object. Craft itself (gradients, outline) is fine. Redesign to read as preserved travel rations (e.g. wrapped hardtack/jerky bundle) and drop the floating 'I' badge, or commit to a clear tin/can if 'iron ration' means a canned ration. —
—
 keeper_tidesinger
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 major pending A skin-toned figure in a blue robe with a green collar holding a beige triangular horn to its mouth reads as a singer/herald, but the pose is awkward: the legs splay as thin dark sticks and the horn shape is ambiguous (reads as a paper cone, not clearly a conch/instrument). It is noticeably pushed up and right of centre, leaving dead space at the bottom-left and clipping near the top crosshair. Lowest-craft of the keeper trio. Re-centre the figure in the box, redraw the legs with real volume instead of sticks, and make the horn read as a conch/instrument; raise contrast so it survives on dark UI. —
—
 lobster_trap
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 major pending The vertical-slatted brown dome with a curled rope handle reads as a generic basket/barrel, and the small red comma shape with a dark hole inside does not read as a lobster at all. It is also nearly indistinguishable from crab_pot's silhouette, hurting family distinctness. The 'lobster' element is too abstract to identify. Give it a clearly readable red lobster (segmented body, claws, tail) inside the trap and differentiate the trap shape from crab_pot. —
—
 map_forge
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 major pending The anvil-plus-hammer base reads as a forge, but a large semi-transparent peach/orange disc overlays the centre with floating four-point sparkles, muddying the silhouette and obscuring the anvil so the subject is unclear at a glance. The disc looks like an accidental glow/selection-state bleed rather than intentional art, and its hard circular edge fights the clean vector style of the family. Contrast and centring are otherwise okay but the overlay is a real defect. Remove or greatly reduce the peach glow disc and the floating sparkles so the anvil+hammer silhouette reads cleanly; if a forge-glow is wanted, restrict it to a small ember at the anvil top. —
—
 ore_copper
Vector icon (still) unusedores
 major pending It reads as a generic veined rock-ball, nearly identical to ore_iron (same dark sphere + orange chevron) so the two are not distinct siblings. The scattered teal and orange dots look like noise/stray fills rather than copper, and copper's signature pink-orange metallic tone is muddied to brown. Outline and shading are competent but subject accuracy and family distinctness fail. Push the band to a brighter copper pink-orange, remove the random teal dots, and change the silhouette or vein pattern so it is clearly distinct from iron (e.g. nuggety copper chunks rather than the shared zigzag ball). —
—
 ore_iron
Vector icon (still) unusedores
 major pending Competent dark faceted sphere with rusty-orange zigzag veins and ore flecks, but it is almost indistinguishable from ore_copper (same sphere, same orange band) which is a real look-alike problem. The rusty orange also reads more like copper/rust than grey iron. Craft (outline, shading, sparkle accents) is fine in isolation. Differentiate from copper: make iron's veins a duller grey-brown or steely tone and give copper the warmer orange, or change one of the two silhouettes so the pair is no longer the same ball. —
—
 pet_chick
Vector icon (still) unusedpets
 major pending The body is drawn as a scalloped petal/gear-edged disc rather than a soft fluffy chick, so the silhouette reads as a flower or cog more than a baby bird. Face (dot eyes, orange triangle beak, pink cheeks, sprout tuft) is cute and on-style, but the rogue body outline and an odd large pale oval on the right flank hurt subject accuracy. Tiny legs are fine. Redraw the body as a smooth round/oval fluffy mass with at most a few soft down bumps; remove the hard scalloped perimeter and the stray pale oval, or resolve it into a clearly-shaped wing. —
—
 pet_goat_kid
Vector icon (still) unusedpets
 major pending The head (tan face, dark horns, floppy ears, pale beard) reads as a goat, but the large near-white body is almost flat and nearly disappears against the light half, killing readability and looking like an undefined blob. Proportions are off — a huge low-detail oval on tiny stub legs reads more sheep/cow than nimble kid. A pale stray sliver near the rear also looks like an artifact. Add a darker rim/outline and subtle shading to the white body so it separates from the light background, shrink the body relative to the head, lengthen the legs, and clean up the stray pale sliver at the tail. —
—
 pickaxe
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 major pending Same problem as gold_pick: the head is a flat horizontal hexagonal bar with no spike or curved point, so it reads as a steel mallet/sledge rather than a pickaxe. It is essentially a recolour of the gold_pick silhouette, so the two are not visually distinct. Metal gradient and wood handle are cleanly drawn and framing is fine. Redraw the head as a true pick (pointed pick end and/or chisel end on a curved bar) to match the label and differentiate it from gold_pick. —
—
 plough
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 major pending A wooden handle with a brown grip knob and a pointed metal scoop blade reads as a spade or trowel, not a plough (which should have a curved mouldboard and frame). There is also a brown smear overlapping the drop shadow at the base that looks like a stray fill. Redesign as a recognizable plough (angled mouldboard blade on a frame) or relabel; also remove the stray brown blob under the shadow. —
—
 potion
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsPortal
 major pending A round-bottomed green flask with a brown cork and a glossy highlight reads cleanly as a potion. The problem is the large cream label carrying horizontal lines that look like placeholder/lorem text rather than a sigil or symbol — it reads as a 'document' label and muddies the magic-potion intent. Otherwise centred, well-shaded, and distinct from siblings. Replace the text-line label with a single bold symbol (rune, star, skull) or drop the label and add a liquid surface + bubbles so it reads unmistakably as a magic potion. —
—
 rope_knot
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 major pending The tan twisted loops with diagonal hatch marks read overwhelmingly as a pretzel, complete with the classic pretzel silhouette and the dashed marks looking like salt/scoring. It does not read as a nautical rope knot at all. The craft is clean but the form choice defeats the subject. Redraw as a recognizable nautical knot (e.g. a figure-eight or bowline) with visible rope-fiber texture and free working ends, avoiding the symmetric pretzel loop. —
—
 shuffle
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 major pending The subject is muddled: at a glance it reads as a bulbous brown mitt or pouch gripping a gold ingot rather than a 'Reshuffle Horn', and only the yellow arc sound-waves on the right hint at a horn. The blue circular-arrow (reshuffle) and the horn body fight for the same space, and the gold bar across the body is ambiguous. Outline and shading are competent but the silhouette does not communicate a horn. Redraw as a recognisable curved bugle/horn silhouette (clear bell + mouthpiece) in profile, keep the sound-wave arcs, and overlay a small blue reshuffle arrow as a corner badge rather than across the body. —
—
 silver_bar
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 major pending A grey trapezoidal form with a glossy top gradient and a single ring/O stamp on the front; it reads more like a small crate, bin, or coin-slot box than a silver BAR/ingot. The circular stamp is ambiguous and the trapezoid silhouette undercuts the 'bar' identity. Craft itself is clean, but subject accuracy is weak. Reshape to a clearly stacked metal ingot/bar (angled brick form, maybe a small stack) and drop or replace the ring stamp so it reads unmistakably as a silver bar. —
—
 tile_fruit_blackberry
Vector icon (still) player-facingfruits
 major pending The body is a smooth egg-shaped purple form with bumps laid out in a flat, evenly-spaced grid, so it reads as a plum or grape rather than a clustered aggregate (drupelet) berry. The cluster structure that defines a blackberry is absent, and the bumps look like a surface texture rather than individual fused druplets. Rebuild the body as a cluster of overlapping rounded druplets (lumpy silhouette, varied bump sizes catching light individually) rather than a smooth ovoid with a dot grid, so it reads as a blackberry not a plum. —
—
 tile_veg_squash
Vector icon (still) player-facingvegetables
 major pending A tan-orange teardrop/pear body with vertical rib stripes and a small dark stem nub; it reads as a pear or generic gourd rather than a recognizable squash. The bottom-heavy bulb with a narrow neck is ambiguous and the muted tan palette lacks squash character. Craft itself (gradient, ribs, outline) is competent but subject accuracy fails. Reshape to a recognizable butternut or crookneck squash silhouette (clear bulbous end + distinct neck) and warm the palette toward a richer butternut tan-orange for unambiguous reading. —
—
 tile_veg_turnip
Vector icon (still) player-facingvegetables
 major pending The white-to-magenta gradient bulb with a purple shoulder and three green leaf sprigs does read as a turnip, and the two-tone body is the correct cue. However it is far too similar to the beet sibling: same round silhouette, same three-sprig green top, and overlapping magenta palette make them easy to confuse on the board. The soft pillowy white highlight in the center is also a bit muddy/blown-out. Strengthen the white lower half vs purple top contrast and tighten the central highlight to reduce the pillow-shaded look, so it reads distinctly from the beet. —
—
 ui_build
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 major pending The dark grey head with a glossy oval highlight reads more like a mallet block or even a cleaver than a clear 'build' tool, and the thin brown handle is dwarfed by it. It is pushed to the lower-left and the handle tip clips at the box edge, and the all-dark head nearly vanishes on the dark background. Redraw as an unambiguous claw/framing hammer or trowel with a metal-grey head plus a distinct claw silhouette, center it in the box, and give the handle more visual weight. —
—
 ui_inventory
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 major pending Almost entirely muddy olive-green on a dark interior, so contrast is poor and it nearly disappears on the dark background; the form reads as a generic backpack rather than clearly 'inventory'. The straps and buckles at the top are cluttered and low-contrast, and the two pale dots read as random highlights. Lift the value range (lighter midtones, brighter buckles/clasps), simplify the strap detail, and add a clearer identifying element so it reads as a pack/bag instantly on dark UI. —
—
 ui_scale
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 major pending It reads as a balance scale but is drawn tilted with one pan raised and one lowered, an odd off-balance default for a UI 'Scale' icon. The two pans are rendered as dark muddy ellipses that blend into the wood beam and don't clearly read as dishes, hurting legibility at small size. Level the beam to a balanced/symmetric pose and brighten/define the pans (lighter rim, clearer cup shape) so they read distinctly from the dark beam. —
—
 villager_walking
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 major pending The conical straw hat and blue tunic read as a villager, but the legs and arms are awkward dark navy/brown blobs that don't articulate into a clear walking pose, so it looks broken/mid-glitch rather than a confident walk. There is no orb/badge framing like the rest of this set, the proportions are top-heavy, and the limbs nearly merge into a muddy mass against the dark half. Lowest-craft icon in the batch. Redraw with clear limb separation and a readable stride (one leg forward, arms swinging), lighten/recolour the limbs for contrast, and either centre it in the design box or add the family's badge framing. —
—
 weapon_dagger
Vector icon (still) unusedweapons
 major pending It is a well-rendered short blade with a steel gradient, gold cross-guard and a red-jewel pommel, but it is nearly the SAME asset as weapon_sword and reads as a small sword rather than a distinct dagger. The blade proportion is too long/sword-like for a dagger, undermining subject accuracy and family distinctness. Craft itself is clean (good outline, consistent light), and framing is centered. Differentiate from the sword: shorten and broaden the blade to a stubby dagger profile and simplify the guard, so the two icons are not look-alikes. —
—
 weather_comet
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweather
 major pending The comet head holds roughly the same position while the tail behind it lengthens and shortens across frames, so it reads as a pulsing/breathing tail rather than a comet actually streaking across the sky, which is the wrong motion for a comet. There is no travel arc and the head barely moves, making it feel like an in-place effect; the first and last frames differ enough in tail length that the loop has a soft hitch. The glowing core and gradient tail are drawn attractively. Animate the comet translating diagonally across the cell with a trailing tail that follows the head, or at minimum bob the head along an arc, so it reads as motion through space rather than a tail that grows and shrinks in place. —
—
 weather_crescent_moon
Vector icon (still) unusedweather
 major pending The crescent, craters and two sparkles are nicely drawn, but a thick full-circle golden ring/halo wraps the entire moon, so it reads as a ring or eclipse rather than a clean crescent. That outer band fights the silhouette and is the dominant shape at small size. Remove the full outer ring; keep only the solid crescent so the silhouette is unambiguous, retaining the craters and the two sparkles. —
—
 wtool_chisel
Vector icon (still) unusedworkshopTools
 major pending The brown bullet-shaped tip in a chrome ferrule over a tapered grey handle reads far more like a lipstick or felt marker than a wood chisel; there is no flat bevelled cutting edge anywhere. Craft is clean (bold outline, soft metallic gradient) but the silhouette fails the subject. It is also easy to confuse with the paintbrush, which shares the ferrule+handle layout. Redesign as a recognizable chisel: a long flat steel blade ending in a clear angled bevel edge, joined to a wooden handle via a ferrule, ideally pointing down-left so the cutting edge is the focal point. —
wtool_wrench
Vector icon (animation) player-facingworkshopTools
 major verified VERIFIED BUG (instrumented): animWrench calls ctx.restore() one more time than ctx.save() (netSaveDelta -1, stack goes negative). The single save() at the top precedes BOTH the -0.62 framing rotation and the osc handle rotation, so restore() (commented ‘back to the rotated-but-unturned frame’) actually unwinds to identity — the fixed hex socket then draws in the wrong frame — and the extra restore pops a state the caller saved, corrupting the caller’s transform (drifts/garbles on a reused context). The ratchet motion itself is nicely eased. Add a ctx.save() right after the framing rotate(-0.62) (establishing the rotated-but-unturned frame) so the existing restore() returns there and the hex draws rotated; this also balances the stack to netSaveDelta 0. Then verify the hex socket sits on the bolt pivot. added save() after framing rotate(-0.62): hex bolt now stays fixed & frame is balanced (was -1 restore underflow)
—
 ability_trigger
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A clean cottage (terracotta roof, tan walls, glowing yellow window) with a radiant gold sunburst/spark hovering top-right reads as 'activate/trigger', which is reasonable. Outline and shading are confident and the light direction is consistent. The two elements feel slightly disconnected and the burst is a little small relative to the house, weakening the 'trigger' read at a glance. Enlarge the gold burst slightly and overlap it onto the roof so house+spark read as one 'activation' unit. —
—
 ach_dairyman
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending The cow reads acceptably (body, spots, head with horns/ears on the right) on a pale oval inset, distinct from the other animal badges. However the silhouette is a bit blobby and the legs/spots merge into a brown smear at small size, so it leans more 'spotted blob' than unmistakable cow. Silver rim and blue ribbon are clean and tier-appropriate. Tighten the cow silhouette — separate the legs and clarify the head/horn shape — so it reads as a cow rather than a spotted lump at icon size. —
—
 ach_deep_digger
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending A pickaxe (curved silver head + orange handle) reads clearly enough for 'digger', with clean rim and crisp two-star ribbon. The main weakness is family-level: this is the same pickaxe emblem as ach_first_strike (bronze) and ach_mine_master (gold), so it relies entirely on rim colour and star count to differentiate. Craft is solid; centering is good. Consider differentiating the mining-tier emblems (e.g. pickaxe vs pickaxe-in-rock vs paired tools) so they are distinct by shape, not just rim colour. —
—
 ach_first_strike
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending Reads as a pickaxe/mining first-milestone with clean bronze rim and a single star — craft and centering are fine. The issue is it is the same pickaxe emblem as Deep Digger (silver) and Mine Master (gold), so differentiation rests only on rim colour and stars. Subject is readable but not distinctive within the family. Differentiate the mining-tier glyphs by shape rather than relying on rim colour alone (see Deep Digger note). —
—
 ach_mine_master
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending Reads as mining mastery with a gold rim and a three-star pedestal ribbon signalling top tier — craft and framing are clean. As with the other two mining badges, it reuses the identical pickaxe emblem, so it is distinguished only by rim colour and star count rather than a distinct shape. Acceptable but not distinctive. Give the master-tier mining badge a more elaborate glyph (e.g. crossed pickaxes or pick-in-ore) so it stands apart from the lower mining tiers. —
—
 ach_naturalist
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending A single veined green leaf on the bronze disc reads as nature/botany and is reasonably clean, with a one-star ribbon. The concern is overlap with Forester (also a green-plant emblem) — leaf vs tree is a fine distinction at small size, so the two can be confused as siblings. Centering and contrast are good. Push the leaf design to be more distinctly 'naturalist' (e.g. add a small flower or insect, or a pressed-specimen look) so it doesn't read as a near-duplicate of the Forester tree badge. —
—
 ach_patient_hands
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending A dark grey interlocking figure-8 / infinity loop sits on the silver medallion with a 2-star ribbon plaque. The symbol does not read as 'hands' at all and is identical in shape to the gold 'Tireless' icon, so the two are look-alikes distinguished only by metal tier and star count. Craft itself is clean. Consider a clasped-hands or hourglass motif so 'Patient Hands' reads on-subject and is distinct from Tireless beyond the tier color. —
—
 ach_stable_hand
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending A chunky brown horse-head silhouette sits on the silver medallion, which does read as horse/stable, but the form is angular and slightly awkward (the muzzle and mane edges are blocky rather than confidently curved). Contrast against the silver is decent. Acceptable but the silhouette could be cleaner. Smooth and refine the horse-head silhouette (cleaner muzzle/mane curve) so it reads more confidently as a horse at small size. —
—
 ach_tireless
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending A figure-8 / infinity loop on a gold medallion with a 3-star plaque; the infinity symbol fits 'Tireless' reasonably well. However it is the same emblem as silver 'Patient Hands', so the pair are look-alikes separated only by tier and star count. Gold craft and contrast are good. Keep infinity for Tireless but differentiate Patient Hands' emblem so the two are not confused. —
—
 ach_village_voice
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 minor pending A striped almond/eye shape over a brown band sits on the gold medallion with a 2-star plaque; it could be read as a speaking mouth or an eye, so the 'voice' meaning is ambiguous though plausible. Gold contrast is better than the bronze twin (Trusted Friend) it shares the emblem with. Craft is otherwise clean. Consider a megaphone/speech-bubble motif so 'Voice' reads clearly and is distinct from Trusted Friend. —
—
 arcane_candle
Vector icon (animation) player-facingarcane
 minor pending The flame genuinely changes shape and lean frame-to-frame and the warm halo pulses, so it reads as a real flicker rather than a slide. Drawing is clean (white waxy body, gold holder, soft glow disc). However the final frame blooms into a near white-out that won't loop back to the dimmer frame 1, giving a visible flash on repeat. Tone down the last 1-2 frames' bloom so the brightness returns close to frame 1 for a seamless loop. —
—
 arcane_magic_dust
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 minor pending A brown leather pouch with a gold drawstring and a burst of yellow-white sparkles to the right reads as a 'magic dust/pouch', though the bag body is quite dark and muddy and could be mistaken for a coin purse or a nut without the sparkles. The sparkles do the heavy lifting; the pouch itself has low internal contrast and slightly flat shading. Framing pushes the sparkle cloud to the right edge but stays inside the box. Lighten and warm the pouch leather and add a clearer cinched-neck/fold so it reads as a pouch on its own, and balance the sparkle burst more symmetrically around the bag. —
—
 arcane_magic_dust
Vector icon (animation) player-facingarcane
 minor pending The pouch body is completely static; the only motion is a small star/glint twinkling at the tie-off plus a warm glow that swells on the right side, an overlay rather than the object moving. The bag is decently drawn (segmented brown sack, gold tie) but feels frozen. The glow keeps growing through the strip and does not return to the frame-1 state, so it will pop on loop. Give the pouch a gentle bob/squash or let dust motes lift out of it, and bring the side-glow back down by the last frame so it loops. —
—
 arcane_pentacle
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 minor pending The five-pointed star inside a ringed circle with node dots clearly reads as a pentacle/summoning sigil and the family-consistent purple glow is attractive. However the whole icon is rendered as a soft low-contrast glow with no solid core, so it looks hazy and slightly washed-out, and on the light half it nearly disappears. It is also the same purple as the crystal ball, risking a palette clash within the family. Add a brighter solid (near-white) core to the star and ring lines so the shape holds at small size and reads on the light background, keeping the purple glow as a halo rather than the entire fill. —
—
 arcane_runestone
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 minor pending An upright dark-grey rune tablet with three cyan Norse-style runes carved into it reads as a runestone, and the cool blue glyphs are a nice accent. The problem is contrast: the dark slate body nearly vanishes into the #23262b dark half (the outline merges with the background) so only the floating runes remain, making it look unanchored on dark. Shading on the stone is fairly flat/muddy. Lighten the stone body or add a subtle rim/edge light so the tablet silhouette holds against the dark background, and increase the carved-groove shadow under the runes for more depth. —
—
 arcane_runestone
Vector icon (animation) player-facingarcane
 minor pending There is visible motion but it is a rigid whole-icon pendulum tilt — the entire slab leans left then right while the blue rune glyphs stay frozen, so it reads as the sprite being rotated rather than anything organic. The tablet art itself is fine (bevelled grey stone, glowing carved runes). It does swing back so it loops, but the motion is the crude rigid type. Add a subtle pulse/glow cycle to the carved runes (or a tiny shimmer) instead of relying on a stiff whole-body tilt. —
—
 astral_constellation
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 minor pending The seven four-point stars with thin connector lines and small dim dots read as a constellation, and the Big Dipper-like shape is recognizable. However it leans entirely on soft white bloom: on the light half the stars and especially the connecting lines nearly vanish, hurting readability. The faint grey dots scattered around add noise without clearly reading as stars. Add a subtle darker/colored outline or stroke to the stars and connector lines so the shape holds on light backgrounds; drop or brighten the ambiguous grey filler dots. —
—
 astral_constellation
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 minor pending Several four-point stars connected by faint lines twinkle slightly and the connecting lines/extra dots shift a little across frames, but the change is small and the overall cluster stays put. It reads as a gentle twinkle rather than clear motion, and the busy dark-navy background makes the faint linking lines hard to parse. Loops without a hard jump. Strengthen the twinkle (stagger star brightness pulses) and make the connecting constellation lines draw/brighten in sequence so the motion is clearly legible against the dark ground. —
—
 astral_full_moon
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 minor pending The drawing is nice - pale cratered sphere with a cool-blue rim shadow and a soft surrounding glow - but the motion is very subtle, basically just a gentle halo/glow breathing and barely-perceptible crater shimmer. Adjacent frames look almost identical, so it reads as nearly frozen rather than animated. Loops fine because there is so little change. Add a clearer slow drift or twinkle (e.g. a subtle rotating sheen or one crater highlight cycling) so the motion is actually legible at a glance. —
—
 astral_galaxy
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 minor pending The glowing pale-yellow core surrounded by an ellipse of blue-to-purple dots reads reasonably as a spiral galaxy seen at an angle. The dotted ring is a bit mechanical rather than a flowing spiral arm, and the heavy soft nebula glow makes the whole thing low-contrast and mushy on the light background. Color palette is attractive and distinct from siblings. Give the dot ring a clearer two-arm spiral sweep and tighten contrast so the form survives on light backgrounds rather than dissolving into the bloom. —
—
 astral_north_star
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 minor pending A four-point sparkle star with long horizontal rays and a soft halo reads as a bright lone star. The problem is differentiation and light-background survival: it is almost pure white bloom with no outline, so on the light half it nearly disappears, and as a plain sparkle it risks looking like a generic shine rather than a specific North Star. It is also similar in vocabulary to the shooting-star sibling. Add a faint blue rim/core definition so it holds on light backgrounds, and give it an identifying touch (e.g. a steadier cross or a subtle directional emphasis) to distinguish it from the shooting star. —
—
 auger
Vector icon (still) player-facingmissingItems
 minor pending Reads as a hand drill bit: brown wooden grip, ribbed steel shaft and a blue-grey arrowhead/tip with a white highlight dot. Outline is clean and the metal gradient is convincing, but it reads more as a screwdriver/awl than an 'auger' (a screw-augur should show a helical spiral flute). Framing is good and centered. Add a visible helical/spiral flute down the shaft so it reads as a boring auger rather than a plain ribbed driver. —
—
 banner_hammer
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending Slate-blue pennant with brass rail; the emblem is a pair of crossed pickaxe/mattock heads over a brown handle, which reads more as 'crossed pickaxes' than a single hammer. Craft is consistent with the family and the metal heads have decent shading, but the crossed-tool motif is busier and slightly muddy where the heads overlap. Subject-to-label match is a touch loose (hammer vs pickaxes). Either rename to match the crossed-pick motif or simplify to a single bold hammer silhouette for clearer 'hammer' read. —
—
 banner_wave
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending Teal-blue pennant with brass rail; the white emblem is a rounded cresting wave with two foam dots, but it reads a little as an amorphous white blob/cloud rather than a clean wave at small size. Family finish and framing are good, contrast is strong. The wave's silhouette is the weak point — the crest curl isn't clearly directional. Sharpen the wave into a clearer curling-crest silhouette with a defined trough so it reads as water, not a blob. —
—
 basic
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 minor pending Clean outline and warm wood gradient with a sprout, but it reads as a bellied pot or seed jar more than a flat paper 'Seedpack'. The seed-bumps under the rim are nicely drawn and the sprout is crisp. Framing is well-centred and fills the box. If the label must read as a seed PACKET, flatten the silhouette into a paper envelope shape; if a seed jar is acceptable, leave as-is. —
—
 bee
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending The yellow/black striped abdomen and pale translucent wings read as a bee, helped by the small antenna and stinger. The body is a bit blobby and the legs are barely-there wisps, and the wings slightly overlap into mush, but it is recognizable. Define a couple of small legs and tighten the wing separation so the two wings read distinctly. —
—
 blast_charge
Vector icon (still) player-facingmissingItems
 minor pending Instantly reads as dynamite: two red cylinders bound by a brown rope wrap with cream labels and a yellow spark star on the fuse. Strong subject clarity and good outline. The problem is a soft orange glow halo that bleeds well outside the design box on both light and dark fields, muddying the silhouette. Tighten or remove the orange bloom/glow so it stays inside the silhouette; keep the spark localized to the fuse tip. —
—
 bld_barn
Vector icon (animation) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A dark-red barn with cream cross-braced doors and a small cupola finial at the roof peak that rotates subtly across frames; the barn body itself is static. The weathervane motion is thematically apt but very small and easy to miss at icon scale, so it sits between still and animated. Drawing is solid with no flashing or drift. Make the weathervane/cupola rotation more pronounced, or add a gentle hayloft-window glow pulse, so the motion reads clearly at small size. —
—
 bld_caravan_post
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending Reads as a caravan/covered wagon: arched canvas top with vertical ribs, brown wagon body and two dark spoked wheels, plus a signpost on the left. It is competent but the wagon body and wheels are quite dark and a bit muddy, and the signpost is empty/plain which slightly weakens the 'post' read. Sits low-left in the box. Lighten the wagon body, add rim/spoke highlights for definition, and put a small icon or banner on the signpost board. —
—
 bld_chapel
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending Reads clearly as a chapel/church — tall steeple topped by a yellow cross, arched stained window, and an adjoining gabled wing with a glowing arched doorway. Craft is clean. The only issue: it is byte-identical to bld_church (its declared shared partner), so the two are not visually distinct. None for the art itself; if chapel and church must be distinguishable, give one a different scale, roof tone or a bell to differentiate the siblings. —
—
 bld_church
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending Identical artwork to bld_chapel (they share the same source draw): cream nave, cross-topped steeple, arched windows and a warm-lit arched door. The icon itself is clean and reads well as a church. The sole concern is the lack of visual distinction from its sibling chapel. If both need to coexist on screen, differentiate one sibling (e.g. larger nave or a bell tower) so they are not indistinguishable. —
—
 bld_church
Vector icon (animation) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A nicely drawn grey-and-cream church with a tall dark spire, arched windows and a tiny gold finial glint, but across all 14 frames it is effectively frozen — at most a faint shimmer at the steeple tip. There is no sway, bell motion or light cycle to register as animated, so it reads as a still icon. Craft is good; motion is essentially absent. Add a small looping motion such as a soft warm glow pulsing in the arched windows or a subtle gold twinkle on the spire finial so it reads as animated rather than static. —
—
 bld_housing
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A well-crafted little cottage: tan walls, brown plank-thatch gable roof, arched door with knob, a 4-pane lit yellow window, and a smoking chimney. Craft is clean and the light direction is consistent. The only issue is family-level: it is pixel-identical to bld_housing2 and bld_housing3 (they share art) so the trio is indistinguishable as variants. If the three housing blocks are meant to read as distinct upgrade variants, differentiate them (e.g. wall colour, roof material, second storey, or door/window count); otherwise this single icon is fine. —
—
 bld_housing2
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending Pixel-for-pixel identical to bld_housing — same tan cottage, plank-thatch roof, arched knobbed door, lit 4-pane window, smoking chimney. Craft and readability are good in isolation, but as a separate 'Housing Block' entry it carries zero visual distinction from its siblings. Give this variant a differentiating detail (different wall/roof colour, extra window, or scale) if it is meant to read apart from bld_housing/bld_housing3. —
—
 bld_housing3
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending Again identical to bld_housing and bld_housing2 — the same cottage with brown gable roof, arched door, lit window, and chimney smoke. Individually the art is clean, well-centred and readable; the demerit is purely the lack of variation across the three shared housing icons. Differentiate from the other two housing blocks (colourway, storey count, or roof type) if distinct variants are intended. —
—
 bld_powder_store
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A low brown barn-roofed hut with a vent grille, a metal-banded chest and a wooden powder keg beside it; the keg and chest sell 'store' reasonably. The whole icon is very dark and low-contrast, so at small size the interior details muddy together and it can be hard to distinguish from a generic dark shed. The little gold latch/flame on the right edge is a nice accent. Lift the mid-tone fills slightly and add a touch more rim light so the chest, keg and vent read apart at thumbnail size. —
—
 bld_silo
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A tall cylindrical wood-banded silo with a metal domed cap and a side-mounted ladder reads correctly as a silo. The angled chute/foot jutting out to the lower-left is ambiguous and looks a bit like a broken leg or spout rather than an unloading chute. Otherwise clean shading and a solid silhouette. Clarify or remove the lower-left protruding chute so it doesn't read as a broken/odd appendage. —
—
 bld_tent
Vector icon (animation) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A red-and-white striped conical tent with a dark interior and a gold pennant at the apex; the pennant flutters slightly and the stripe shading shifts a little, but the motion is small and the tent body barely changes, so it reads as nearly still. The drawing is appealing and the loop is clean, but the animation is faint. No glitches or flashing. Strengthen the flag flutter and add a gentle canvas ripple/breathing on the tent fabric so the motion is clearly visible at icon size. —
—
 bld_tower
Vector icon (animation) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A grey stone tower with an arched glowing doorway and a blue pointed roof topped by a gold finial; across the frames the body is essentially frozen with at most a faint shimmer on the finial and door light. It reads as a still icon rather than an animated one. The art is clean and well-shaded but there is no clear motion. Add a visible looping cue — e.g. a warm flickering glow in the arched doorway/window or a small twinkle on the gold finial — to make it read as animated. —
—
 bld_windmill
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending A tapered mill body with a glowing window and four latticed sail arms on a central hub, reading clearly as a windmill and nicely distinct from the watermill. However the upper-left and right sail tips clip right at the magenta design-box bounds, so the blades feel cramped against the frame. Otherwise clean craft and good contrast. Scale the sprite down slightly so the rotating sails sit comfortably inside the 64px box without touching the bounds. —
—
 bld_workshop
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 minor pending An olive-brown gabled hut with a crossed hammer/chisel emblem in the gable and a wide dark workbench-doorway with a stacked-plank stool reads acceptably as a workshop. It is quite dark and muted, and the crossed-tools emblem is small and easy to miss at thumbnail size, so it leans generic. The wood-plank accent on the lower right is a good touch. Make the crossed-tool emblem larger/higher-contrast and lift the mid-tones a little so the workshop identity reads at small size. —
—
 bond_rank_1
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending Clean glossy pink heart with a clear bronze rank badge showing '1'; subject and rank read instantly and the bronze tier matches the family's low-rank colour. The persistent issue is framing — the heart+badge cluster sits high-left of centre, leaving notable empty space in the lower-right of the box. Art craft (gloss highlight, dark rim, badge numeral) is good. Nudge the whole heart+badge group down and right to centre it on the crosshair. —
—
 bond_rank_2
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending Same crisp pink heart with a bronze '2' badge nestled at the lower-right of the heart; numeral and tier colour are legible and distinct from rank-1 only by the number, as intended. Craft is consistent and clean. Off-centre framing (heart sits high-left) is the only knock, identical to the rest of the rank set. Centre the heart+badge group within the design box. —
—
 bond_rank_3
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending Pink heart with a bronze '3' badge; reads cleanly and sits correctly as the top bronze tier before the silver ranks. Numeral contrast and heart gloss are good and consistent with ranks 1-2. Framing remains high-left of centre like its siblings. Centre the heart+badge group within the design box. —
—
 bond_rank_4
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending The badge switches to a silver/white disc with a dark '4', correctly signalling the higher tier and visually distinct from the bronze ranks 1-3. Heart and numeral are clean and the tier-colour change is a good legibility cue. Same off-centre (high-left) framing as the rest of the set. Centre the heart+badge group within the design box. —
—
 bond_rank_5
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending Pink heart with a silver '5' badge, consistent with rank-4's tier colour and clearly readable. Craft and contrast are good on both backgrounds. Framing sits high-left of centre as with the whole rank family. Centre the heart+badge group within the design box. —
—
 bond_rank_6
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending Top-tier pink heart with a silver '6' badge; numeral is legible and the silver tier matches ranks 4-5 as intended. Heart gloss and badge rendering are clean and on-family. The shared high-left framing offset is the only issue. Centre the heart+badge group within the design box. —
—
 boon_bond_mult
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending The heart-plus-X multiply concept reads, but the heart is rendered noticeably smaller and flatter than the bond_rank hearts and sits low-left of centre, so it feels undersized in the box. The gold X badge overlaps the heart's top-right cleanly. Composition is slightly off-balance compared to its glossier siblings. Scale the heart up to match the bond_rank hearts and re-centre the heart+badge group in the box for visual consistency. —
—
 boon_branch_coexist
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A round-canopy green tree with a brown trunk and two amber orbs reads as 'growth/coexistence' reasonably well, and the dark-green clustering is tidy. However the small white scratch marks near the orbs look like stray fills/artifacts rather than intentional detail, and the trunk's pale bend reads slightly awkwardly. Centring is good. Remove or clean up the small white scratch marks near the orbs so they don't read as artifacts. —
—
 boon_chain_mult
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A row of three linked gold coin-discs plus the multiply X reads as a chain multiplier, and the badge is consistent with the other boon_*_mult icons. The coins are rendered fairly flat and dark-rimmed, sitting low-left so the composition feels bottom-heavy and a touch undersized in the box. It risks looking similar to coin_mult at a glance. Re-centre and slightly enlarge the coin cluster; make the 'chain/linked' read stronger (overlapping links) so it is clearly distinct from boon_coin_mult. —
—
 boss_ember_drake_v2
Vector icon (still) player-facingcharactersV2
 minor pending Strong fiery boar-like head with pale curved horns, glowing yellow eyes and a snouted nose reads as a fire beast, and the orange-to-dark gradient gives good menace. The single small flame curling at the right of the mouth is tiny and easy to miss as a 'drake' cue, reading more like a stray fill than fire. Outline and contrast are clean and it sits well in the box. Make the flame larger/more legible (e.g. breathe it forward from the mouth or add small flame licks) so the 'drake' fire identity reads at small size. —
—
 boss_mossback
Vector icon (still) player-facingcharacters
 minor pending A green humanoid face under a domed brown shell decorated with green/yellow/magenta growths and hex patterning reads as a mossy/turtle boss, distinct from the other green boss (quagmire). The neutral straight-line mouth and plain eyes make it feel a touch passive/less menacing than its boss siblings. The shell rim slightly overlaps the top magenta bound but is not clipped. Give the face a more characterful expression (a frown or set jaw) to read as a boss, and nudge the shell down a few pixels to sit fully clear of the top design bound. —
—
 boss_mossback_v2
Vector icon (still) player-facingcharactersV2
 minor pending Friendly green creature with a brown mushroom-cap crown dressed with hex/honeycomb shapes and two small flowers, which sells a forest/moss theme. The hex pattern on the cap is a little ambiguous (reads as honeycomb rather than moss/bark texture) and the face is quite gentle for a 'boss'. Craft is clean with good soft shading and centering. Swap the honeycomb-hex motifs for moss clumps or fungus spots to make the 'moss' read clearer, and give the eyes a slightly tougher set. —
—
 boss_quagmire
Vector icon (still) player-facingcharacters
 minor pending A green bog creature with curling antennae, red eyelids over yellow eyes and a barred grimacing mouth reads as a swamp boss, but it shares the same green family palette as mossback so the two can blur at thumbnail size. The two small red mouth-shapes floating up in the ring (extra antennae tips?) read as ambiguous stray marks. Craft and centering are otherwise solid. Shift the hue toward a sickly bog-teal to separate it from mossback, and clarify or remove the two floating red shapes near the antennae tips so they don't read as stray fills. —
—
 boss_storm
Vector icon (still) player-facingcharacters
 minor pending A dark thundercloud with glowing eyes, hanging rain streaks and a yellow lightning bolt reads clearly as a storm boss and is the most atmospheric of the set. However it abandons the family's circular ring-halo for a full dark gradient fill and a flat-bottomed body, so it sits as a stylistic outlier next to the other busts. The lightning bolt overlaps the centre crosshair area but is fine; framing fills to the bottom edge tightly. Give it the same circular ring-halo treatment behind the cloud so it doesn't read as a different art system from its siblings. —
—
 broom
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 minor pending It does read as a witch's besom: a long tilted wooden handle ending in a splayed straw/twig brush, with a sparkle accent for magic. The brush bristles are a bit chaotic and dark, reading more like a frayed rope tangle than tidy straw, and the composition runs corner-to-corner leaving the upper-left and lower-right fairly empty. Outline and handle gradient are clean and the knot/cap at the top end is a nice touch. Tidy and brighten the bristle cluster into clearer straw strands and pull the diagonal slightly more upright so it fills the box more evenly. —
—
 bug_ant
Vector icon (still) unusedcritters
 minor pending Reads as an ant with clear head, antennae, segmented thorax/abdomen and legs, in the family's glossy vector style. The dark head versus the rust-red gradient abdomen gives decent silhouette separation, but the head's pinkish-tan highlight blob looks muddy and the body is tilted/sprawled a bit awkwardly rather than crisply posed. The eye reads fine; legs are a touch thin and could be lost at small size. Clean up the muddy tan highlight on the head and slightly thicken the legs for small-size legibility. —
—
 bug_ant
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcritters
 minor pending The ant is drawn well with glossy segmented head/thorax/abdomen and thin legs, but across the strip the body, legs and antennae barely move so it reads as a static icon. There is no discernible walk cycle or antenna wiggle. No artifacts or drift, just an absence of motion. Add a small leg shuffle and antenna twitch (or a subtle body bob) so the ant reads as alive rather than a still sprite. —
—
 bug_caterpillar
Vector icon (still) unusedcritters
 minor pending Clearly reads as a caterpillar: rounded green segments receding to the right, cute face with eyes/smile, red antennae and little legs. Style and palette fit the family well. However the body tail extends slightly past the right magenta design-box edge, so framing is a touch off / clipping at the bound; the face also has a faint pink cheek blush that's slightly muddy. Pull the tail end in so the whole body sits inside the 64px box and tidy the soft pink cheek blush. —
—
 bug_dragonfly
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcritters
 minor pending The segmented teal body and faint paired wings are decently drawn, but across all 14 frames there is almost no change beyond a faint wing shimmer. For a dragonfly the wings should blur and beat fast; here it reads as a static sprite. No glow flash or drift, just lacking real motion. Add a fast wing-beat (alternating blur/position of the two wing pairs) and a gentle hover bob so it doesn't read as frozen. —
—
 bug_firefly
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcritters
 minor pending The green abdomen glow swells and recedes convincingly, suiting a firefly, over a static brown body with a red face. One frame flares much larger and brighter than its neighbours, which pops slightly as a glow spike rather than easing smoothly. Otherwise the pulse loops and the drawing is clean. Ease the glow pulse so the single oversized flare frame is tamed to match the surrounding swell, removing the brightness pop. —
—
 bug_snail
Vector icon (still) unusedcritters
 minor pending Reads clearly as a snail: cream body with two eye-stalks, on a foot/ground, carrying a brown spiral shell. The spiral and gradient are nicely done. The issue is the outermost shell coil trails off as a thin open line that runs up to the top-right and exits the design box, looking unfinished and pushing the composition past the magenta bound. Close or tuck the outer shell spiral so it forms a complete coil inside the box rather than a thin trailing line that clips the edge. —
—
 bug_snail
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcritters
 minor pending The brown spiral shell is nicely rendered with a soft gloss highlight and the foot/eyestalks are well drawn. Frame-to-frame change is minimal: only a slight bob of the body and a small highlight shift, so it reads as effectively frozen. No artifacts or drift, just too little motion. Add a slow eyestalk sway plus a subtle creep/squash of the foot so the snail visibly inches rather than sitting still. —
—
 canary
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 minor pending The domed cage with vertical bars, perch and a small yellow bird with orange beak reads correctly as a caged canary, though the bird is small and the cage bars wash out at icon size. Craft is clean but the bird competes with the busy cage for the limited pixels, hurting glance-readability. Framing is centred but the tall stand leaves a lot of empty box above/below the subject's mass. Enlarge the bird and thicken/reduce the cage bars so the yellow canary is the dominant shape at small size. —
—
 cat_berry
Vector icon (still) player-facingtileCategories
 minor pending Reads clearly as a clustered aggregate/grape-like berry bunch with a good leafy cap, well-centred in the box. The per-berry radial-gloss highlights and dark maroon outlines are a touch more rendered/glossy than the flatter family members (cattle, wood), so it sits slightly off-style. Contrast holds on both backgrounds. Flatten the per-berry highlights to a single small flat specular dot to match the family's flat-fill look. —
—
 cat_fruits
Vector icon (still) player-facingtileCategories
 minor pending It reads as fruit and the leaf helps, but the airbrushed radial gradient and soft inner glow break sharply from the family's flat-fill, hard-outline style and make it look like a glossy peach/plum rather than a generic fruit. The heavy upper-left gloss highlight flattens the form into a shiny blob. Centring and silhouette are fine. Re-render with flat color fills and a single small flat highlight to match the family, and consider a clearer apple silhouette for the generic 'Fruit' read. —
—
 cat_grass
Vector icon (still) player-facingtileCategories
 minor pending The three curved green blades read as grass, but the prominent yellow daisy perched at the top pulls it toward the 'flower' category and risks confusion with cat_flowers. The blades themselves are clean with a subtle center highlight and good contrast. Composition is a little sparse/left-of-center. Drop or greatly shrink the daisy so the icon reads purely as grass and stays distinct from the Flower category. —
—
 cat_mine_gold
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 minor pending Bumpy gold blob with darker pits and bright glints reads as a gold nugget, helped by the brown rim. The round form plus the dark circular pits flirts with reading as cheese or a moon. The big top-left highlight is good but the irregular outline is a touch soft. Make the pits read as facet cavities (angular, with metallic highlight edges) rather than round holes to avoid the cheese/moon read. —
—
 cat_mine_iron_ore
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 minor pending Grey faceted rock with embedded light speckle nodules and a bright silver wavy seam reads as raw iron ore. The wavy metallic band is a clever vein but its smooth ribbon shape looks slightly like a separate inserted object laid on top. Good contrast and centring otherwise. Integrate the silver vein into the rock facets (follow facet edges, less ribbon-like) so it reads as ore-in-stone rather than a band placed on top. —
—
 cat_mounts
Vector icon (still) player-facingtileCategories
 minor pending Reads as a four-legged mount/horse with a maned head, pointed ear and barrel body. The long curling tail extends to and slightly past the right magenta design-box bound, breaking the framing, and the small head plus oversized oval body make proportions read a bit awkwardly. Palette and outline are on-style. Tuck the tail inside the design box and balance head-to-body proportion so it reads more clearly as a horse. —
—
 cat_vegetables
Vector icon (still) player-facingtileCategories
 minor pending Reads as vegetables via the carrot (with green tops) and the red-and-white mushroom, but the carrot is clipped/cut off by the right magenta bound and overlaps the box edge, hurting framing. Two subjects also make it busier than the single-subject siblings. Colors and outlines are on-style and contrast is good. Pull the carrot fully inside the design box (or pick one hero vegetable) so nothing clips the bounds. —
—
 char_wren
Vector icon (still) player-facingcharacters
 minor pending A young freckled person in a green hood with two thin brown props flanking the shoulders reads as a forester/scout, fitting the green woodland family slot. The two vertical brown sticks (a staff left, and a curved bow-or-pole right) are thin and ambiguous, reading more as stray lines than a clear prop. Face craft and centering are good; the right prop runs close to the side bound. Thicken and clarify the prop(s) so they read as a definite tool (e.g. a clear bow or walking staff) rather than thin floating lines. —
—
 char_wren_v2
Vector icon (still) player-facingcharactersV2
 minor pending A friendly youthful face under a green hood with a small green leaf/feather pin reads as a woodland/ranger youth and is cohesive with the family. However the rosy-cheeked round face is quite similar to Mira and the baker in expression, leaning on the green hood as the main differentiator. Craft is clean with good highlights and framing. Strengthen a unique cue (e.g. a more distinct leaf/feather, freckles, or a slightly different hairstyle) so Wren reads as clearly distinct from Mira at small size. —
—
 coal_hammer
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsMine
 minor pending Reads as a hammer with a diagonal wood handle and a dark purple-black rectangular head dotted with coal flecks; the coal-association is subtle and could be missed. It shares the same handle+rectangular-head template as gold_pick/pickaxe so it risks blending with those siblings. Outline and handle shading are clean and it is well framed. Push the head colour more clearly toward chunky black coal lumps (not a flat slab) to distance it from the silver/gold hammer siblings. —
—
 coal_transmuter
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsMine
 minor pending A dark tapered vessel filled with glowing coal lumps and orange embers rising as dots reads as a fire/furnace pot, but 'transmuter' is abstract so the icon leans generic brazier. The ember dots are a bit scattered and the rising-spark cone is faint on the light half. It is centred and the warm glow gives good focus, but it sits close to the crucible sibling in concept. Tighten the rising sparks into a clearer plume and add a transmutation cue (e.g. a coal turning to a gem) to differentiate from the crucible. —
—
 cobblepath
Vector icon (still) player-facingrecipes
 minor pending The rounded stones with specular highlights are well rendered, but enclosing them in a dark rounded-rectangle 'tray' makes it read as a platter or tray-of-eggs rather than a paving path. The little green specks (moss?) help but the path identity is weak at a glance. Drop or lighten the dark container and let the cobbles fill the box as an irregular paved patch (uneven grout lines, no tray frame) so it reads as ground/path. —
—
 coke
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 minor pending A dark beveled hexagon with an inner glowing orange core and a bright zig-zag streak; the glow and ember palette sell 'processed fuel/coke' reasonably for a fantasy economy. The zig-zag reads a little like a stylized 'Z' or lightning bolt rather than fuel, so subject accuracy is only fair. Craft is clean with good contrast on both grounds. Make the inner element read more like glowing coke/coal embers (clustered hot lumps or cracks) rather than a single lightning-bolt streak. —
—
 cozy_bench
Vector icon (still) unusedcozyDecor
 minor pending Reads as a bench thanks to the horizontal wooden slats, dark armrest loop and two legs, but the perspective is ambiguous: the seat and back slats stack almost flat so it reads more like a stack of planks than a clearly 3D bench. The wood gradient and outline match the family. Centred and unclipped. Separate the seat slats from the backrest slats with a small vertical gap or slight angle so the seat-vs-back relationship is unmistakable at small size. —
—
 cozy_birdhouse
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcozyDecor
 minor pending A round yellow bird appears in the dark entrance hole and its eyes/expression shift slightly, so there is real but very subtle motion confined to the small hole. The birdhouse body and red roof are static and the change is easy to miss at a glance. The drawing (slatted wood, peaked red roof, perch) is clean and reads clearly. Make the bird pop further out of the hole or add a small head tilt/wing flick so the peek is legible at icon size. —
—
 cozy_fountain
Vector icon (still) unusedcozyDecor
 minor pending Reads as a fountain via the basin, central column and the blue arcing water sprays plus scattered droplets. However the grey stone column and basin are fairly desaturated/muted and the water arcs are thin, so the fountain identity leans heavily on the blue and could be missed at very small size. Framing and outline are fine. Strengthen the water (thicker arcs, a brighter top spout plume) and add a touch more contrast/blue tint to the basin so it reads as a water feature faster. —
—
 cozy_mailbox
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcozyDecor
 minor pending Only the small red side flag rotates a few degrees up and down; the box, lid and post are entirely static. It is a genuine, appropriate motion but very small in the frame, so at a glance the icon reads as nearly frozen. The mailbox drawing is clean and clearly readable. Increase the flag's rotation range and add a tiny lid bob or shadow shift so the motion is noticeable at thumbnail size. —
—
 cozy_street_lamp
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcozyDecor
 minor pending The post, base and lamp body are completely static; the only change is a few tiny white/cream glint flecks flickering around the glass head, which reads more as random sparkle noise than a designed shimmer. It is technically animated but barely, and the flecks pop in/out somewhat randomly rather than easing. The lamp drawing itself is solid and readable. Add a gentle pulsing glow halo on the lamp glass (ease in/out) and make the spark flecks rise and fade on arcs instead of random pops, so the light feels alive rather than noisy. —
—
 cozy_wind_chime
Vector icon (still) unusedcozyDecor
 minor pending It reads as a wind chime via the wooden top disc, the row of grey metal tubes and the round striker/clapper hanging below on a string. The identity is slightly soft because the tubes are short and uniform, edging toward reading like a xylophone or pan-pipe, and the hanging string between disc and striker is thin. Outline and shading are consistent with the family. Vary the tube lengths slightly and make the hanging cord a touch more visible so it reads as a dangling wind chime rather than a fixed instrument. —
—
 crab_pot
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 minor pending The cylindrical wicker/wire basket with hoop rim and a bright red crab inside reads as a crab pot, helped by the crab's pincers and eyes. The crab's two little white eye-dots sit oddly and read slightly like a face rather than a crab carapace at a glance. Overall framing and outline are clean and on-style. Make the crab read more clearly as a top-down crab (small legs flanking the body, eyes less prominent) so it isn't mistaken for a face. —
—
 craft_queue_skip
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A pink pentagonal gem with internal facet lines conveys the 'gem' premium-currency idea, and the small white triangle suggests skip/fast-forward; the pairing reads adequately. However the gem is rendered small and bottom-heavy in the box and the facet lines are slightly noisy, and the skip arrow is a little faint against the busy facets. Distinct enough from craft_queue. Enlarge and re-centre the gem, simplify the facet lines, and strengthen the skip arrow's contrast so the skip-with-gem meaning is unmistakable. —
—
 crop_corn
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcrops
 minor pending The kerneled cob with a green husk base is crisply drawn and the two thin silk/tassel tendrils at the top do wave between frames, but the cob body itself is essentially static so the overall motion is faint. It loops fine and has no artifacts, but at a glance it looks close to frozen apart from the wispy curls. Add a subtle whole-cob bob or husk-leaf flex so the motion reads beyond just the thin tassels. —
—
 crop_garlic
Vector icon (still) unusedcrops
 minor pending A cream/white ribbed bulb with vertical clove lines, a tan outline and a single curled top stem. It reads as a pale allium but the rounded body plus single curl makes it ambiguous against onion; garlic's papery cloved-bulb character is faint. Framing is good though the near-white body is low-contrast on the light half.
