Push the garlic identity: add papery-skin texture and a couple of distinct clove bulges at the base so it does not read as a generic onion, and slightly cool the outline. —
—
 crop_pumpkin
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcrops
 minor pending The pumpkin is well rendered with vertical ribs, a green stem and a glossy specular band, and the only animation is that highlight band sliding subtly across the ribs while the body stays put. It loops cleanly and has no glitches, but the motion is so slight the icon reads as basically static at a glance. Add a small squash/bob or stem flex in addition to the light sweep so the pumpkin clearly reads as animated rather than just a shifting shine. —
—
 crop_wheat
Vector icon (still) unusedcrops
 minor pending A golden sheaf of stacked grain heads above crossed stalks bound by a brown tie band. It reads as wheat/grain but the overlapping heads form a somewhat muddy horizontal mass and the upward straws look stiff; the bundle is busier and less crisp than its siblings. Centred adequately though the dense head-cluster reduces small-size clarity. Simplify the head cluster into a few clearly separated drooping grain ears with visible awns, and lighten/define the stalks so the sheaf reads cleanly at icon size. —
—
 crucible
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 minor pending A rounded vessel with a bright orange molten pool, a pale sun-disc backdrop and crossed grey tongs to the left reads plausibly as a crucible, but the pale circle behind it muddies the silhouette and looks like a stray fill. The molten gradient and tongs are nicely done. Concept-overlaps with coal_transmuter (both dark bowls + orange glow), so distinctness is borderline. Drop or darken the pale circular backdrop so the crucible bowl reads as a clean shape, and lean on the tongs to distinguish it from the transmuter. —
—
 cur_core_ingot
Vector icon (still) player-facingcurrencies
 minor pending A glossy blue trapezoidal metal block with a beveled lid, a vertical highlight and a small plus emblem on the front face — it reads more as a metallic chest or strongbox than a cast ingot. Outline is clean and the blue gradient/highlight give it solid volume. There is a stray pale-blue arc floating just below the body near the cast shadow that looks like a leftover stroke. Remove the stray pale arc beneath the body, and either flatten the lid or add a clearer poured-metal seam so it reads as an ingot rather than a chest. —
—
 cur_gems
Vector icon (still) player-facingcurrencies
 minor pending A downward-pointing faceted gem with a pink upper crown and a blue lower pavilion plus two sparkle glints — clearly reads as a gem/jewel. The two-tone pink/blue split is a little arbitrary and the faceting is fairly flat with thin muddy outline segments rather than crisp light/dark facet contrast. It is the smallest-filling icon in the family, sitting well below center and not filling the box. Enlarge and re-center toward the box, strengthen facet light/shadow contrast for sparkle, and either unify the hue or justify the two-tone split. —
—
 day_night_toggle
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A circle split into a navy starfield (white sparkles) and a yellow sun with rays cleanly conveys day/night, and the half-and-half motif is a smart toggle metaphor. The sun rays are a bit thin and the right side is somewhat empty compared to the busy night half. Reads small but the two halves feel slightly unbalanced in detail. Beef up and lengthen the sun rays so the day half carries equal visual weight to the night half. —
—
 decor_driftwood_arch
Vector icon (still) player-facingdecorations
 minor pending A pale tan U-shaped driftwood arch on two grey cobble feet reads as a weathered arch, and the bleached wood grain/highlights are well crafted. The dangling solid-green frond in the centre is ambiguous and reads more like a hanging gourd or leaf than driftwood, competing with the arch as the subject. The little orange star on the dark side is a stray decorative dressing element that adds noise. Drop or restyle the central green hanging element so it reads as seaweed/vine driftwood dressing rather than a separate fruit, and remove the stray orange star. —
—
 decor_pearl_fountain
Vector icon (still) player-facingdecorations
 minor pending An oval basin with a central pedestal, two arcing pale-blue water streams, and a glossy pale sphere on top reads as a water fountain, and the pearly orb supports the 'pearl' name. The central pedestal is a flat grey banded block that looks plain/mechanical against the soft water, and the arcs read slightly more like handles than falling water at small size. Otherwise framing and outline are clean. Give the water arcs more taper/droplet shaping and warm the grey pedestal slightly so it reads as carved stone rather than a flat banded cylinder. —
—
 decor_sundial
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending The oval bronze dial face with hour ticks and a brown angled gnomon plus the grey trapezoid pedestal does read as a sundial. The dial is shown in steep perspective while the pedestal is near-flat, so the two planes fight a little and it looks slightly toy-like. The small green vine on the base is a nice touch and contrast is fine on both halves. Reconcile the dial and pedestal perspective (flatten the dial tilt slightly) so the object reads as one consistent 3/4 view. —
—
 decor_wind_chime
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A row of vertical brass/silver tubes hanging from a small peaked wooden crossbar with a round clapper and red sail below reads as a wind chime. The pale blue motion swooshes on the left are a bit faint and read as stray marks at small size. The peaked roof element is more 'tent' than 'tree', so the 'Tree' in the label isn't conveyed. Strengthen or drop the faint blue motion lines; if the 'tree' identity matters, suggest foliage over the crossbar instead of a tent peak. —
—
 dish_cake
Vector icon (still) unuseddishes
 minor pending Reads clearly as a cake slice with stacked sponge layers, pink filling and a cherry on a plate; clean outline and good warm/pink palette consistent with the family. The cherry stem is an awkwardly long single diagonal stroke and the white frosting on top breaks into two ambiguous blobs that read a little like teeth. Light direction and contrast are otherwise solid on both backgrounds. Shorten and curve the cherry stem to a small natural arc, and smooth the top frosting into one continuous scalloped cap instead of two separate blobs. —
—
 dish_cake
Vector icon (animation) player-facingdishes
 minor pending Attractive pink-and-cream layered slice with a glossy red cherry and green stem on a grey plate, cleanly drawn. Comparing all 14 frames I see no meaningful change — the cherry, frosting layers, and plate are effectively frozen with at most a faint highlight flicker. It's a pretty still, not an animation. Add gentle motion suited to the subject: a subtle cherry bob/wobble on its stem, a soft icing-glaze sheen sweep, or a faint plate-rim glint, looping cleanly back to frame 1. —
—
 dish_honey_pot
Vector icon (still) unuseddishes
 minor pending Reads well as a honey pot: amber gradient body, glossy golden honey at the rim, a hexagon honeycomb motif on the cream label, and a wooden dipper resting in it. The yellow drip running down the left interior reads slightly like a small flame rather than honey, which is mildly confusing at a glance. Otherwise clean outline, good light, and centred framing. Reshape the left-side honey drip so it clearly hangs/drips downward (rounded blob tail) rather than tapering upward like a flame. —
—
 dish_honey_pot
Vector icon (animation) player-facingdishes
 minor pending Golden honey jar with a wooden dipper stick and a banded label; the jar body is essentially frozen for most of the strip. The only motion is a small honey bead that forms and elongates at the lower-right rim in the last few frames. Because that drop is absent at frame 1 and present at the end, the loop snaps slightly rather than easing back. Make the drip a full cycle — droplet swells, falls, then resets to nothing by the last frame so frame 14 matches frame 1; optionally add a slow honey-surface shimmer so the body isn't static. —
—
 drink_juice
Vector icon (animation) player-facingdrinks
 minor pending Bubbles/pulp drift upward in the orange liquid, so there is genuine motion, but it is fairly subtle and the wedge and striped straws sit completely static. It reads more as faint settling than lively fizz, and at a glance the icon can look near-frozen. Drawing itself is clean and appetizing. Increase bubble count/speed or add a gentle straw/wedge bob so the motion reads at a quick glance. —
—
 drink_milk
Vector icon (still) unuseddrinks
 minor pending A rounded white bottle with a glossy body and a red-and-white checkered cap reads as a milk/cream bottle, helped by the picnic-cloth cap motif. Craft is clean with a soft highlight streak, but on the dark right half the near-white body loses its outline and the silhouette gets faint. The pixel-blocky checker cap is also slightly inconsistent with the otherwise smooth-vector family. Add a subtle darker rim/outline so the white body holds its edge on dark backgrounds, and smooth the cap pattern to match the family. —
—
 drink_smoothie
Vector icon (animation) player-facingdrinks
 minor pending Small darker bits drift within the pink liquid, providing some life, but the effect is subtle and the straw and glass outline are static. The motion reads as slow internal settling rather than a clearly intentional churn or bob. Underlying art is clean with nice rim highlights. Add a subtle liquid-surface wobble or faster swirling bits so the animation is unmistakable in motion. —
—
 drink_tea
Vector icon (still) unuseddrinks
 minor pending A cream cup of amber tea on a saucer with steam and a small square teabag tag hanging on the right reads as tea and is differentiated from coffee by the lighter liquid and the tag. The tag and string push to the right edge, pulling the composition off-center and the steam wisp nearly reaches the top magenta bound. The cup-vs-saucer is a bit muddy in tone (cream on cream) compared to the crisp coffee cup. Pull the teabag tag inward and recenter the cup; lift cup/saucer contrast slightly so the cup edge separates from the saucer. —
—
 drink_water
Vector icon (still) unuseddrinks
 minor pending A tapered glass of pale blue water with a waterline and several stacked ice cubes plus small bubbles reads as iced water. Good glassy gradient and highlight. However the cluster of overlapping ice cubes is busy and reads slightly muddy/jumbled at small size, and the very pale glass loses definition where the cubes overlap. Simplify to 2-3 cleaner, more separated ice cubes with crisper edges so the cluster doesn't read as noise at icon scale. —
—
 drink_water
Vector icon (animation) player-facingdrinks
 minor pending Ice cubes shift and tiny bubbles drift slightly, so it is not fully frozen, but the changes are very small and easy to miss frame-to-frame. The overall read is close to static iced water rather than a settling-jostling motion. The blue gradient fill and translucent ice are drawn cleanly. Add a gentle ice bob/clink or a clearer condensation/bubble rise so the motion reads at a glance. —
—
 drink_wine
Vector icon (animation) player-facingdrinks
 minor pending The wine surface and a faint highlight on the bowl shift very subtly, but the glass is essentially static and reads as a still icon. There is no clear swirl, sway, or sparkle that a hot/active drink would have, so it looks pretty-but-frozen. The stemmed glass with crimson fill is well drawn and elegant. Add a slow wine swirl or a traveling rim/surface glint so the still-but-pretty glass actually animates. —
—
 festival_loaf
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftedProducts
 minor pending Same gold loaf base as bread but topped with magenta and purple berries plus a white icing swirl, clearly signalling a fancier festive bake. The toppings are a touch scattered and the cream swirl reads slightly as a stray light streak rather than a deliberate glaze. Still distinct from plain bread and well framed. Tighten the icing swirl into a clearer drizzle shape and balance the berry placement so the festive topping reads more intentional. —
—
 festival_recurrence
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A red-and-white striped peaked tent (or parasol) on a brown pole with a white pennant flag and two gold sparkles reads as a festival marker. The striped canopy and flag are charming and contrast well. The pole feels thin and a little disconnected from the canopy, and the shape is ambiguous between a circus tent and a beach parasol. Thicken/anchor the pole into the canopy and add small scalloped edge or bunting so it reads decisively as a festival tent rather than a parasol. —
—
 festive_candy_cane
Vector icon (still) unusedfestive
 minor pending Reads as a candy cane but the hook is rendered with radial sunburst stripes that look a bit like a fan/pinwheel rather than continuous diagonal striping, weakening instant readability. The cane top nudges right up to the magenta top bound and tilts, so framing feels cramped at the top. White-on-light contrast on the back of the hook is low. Carry the diagonal stripes smoothly around the hook (avoid the radial sunburst), straighten/recenter so the hook clears the top bound, and add a faint outline where white meets the light background. —
—
 festive_candy_cane
Vector icon (animation) player-facingfestive
 minor pending The cane body is completely rigid; the only motion is a spiky red sparkle/starburst flickering at the top of the hook plus an odd white teardrop element that reads more like a drip than a sparkle. The starburst is busy and slightly off-theme, and the white blob is ambiguous. Stripes and shape are clean and the loop is fine, but the subject does not move. Replace the spiky red burst and ambiguous white drip with a clean shimmer sweep along the stripes, or add a gentle cane sway so the candy itself animates. —
—
 festive_gift
Vector icon (animation) player-facingfestive
 minor pending The box itself is essentially frozen; the only motion is faint white sparkle accents popping in the top-left/right and a slight shimmer on the gold ribbon. Art is attractive (deep green box, glossy gold ribbon/bow, soft cast shadow) but the animation is barely-there twinkle rather than any movement of the subject. Loops fine, no artifacts. Add a small bob or a gentle ribbon-bow bounce so the gift reads as animated rather than a static icon with sparkles. —
—
 festive_holly
Vector icon (animation) player-facingfestive
 minor pending The leaves and berries barely move; motion is limited to tiny white sparkle/specular dots twinkling on the foliage and berries. The art is solid with layered green leaves and glossy red berries, but it reads close to a static icon with a faint sparkle overlay. Loops cleanly with no flash or drift. Add a subtle leaf sway or berry bob so the holly itself shows motion rather than just sparkle twinkle. —
—
 festive_pinecone
Vector icon (still) unusedfestive
 minor pending The layered teardrop scales and warm brown palette read as a pinecone, and the egg-like silhouette is acceptable. However the solid white wedge across the top is ambiguous (snow? a crack? a highlight?) and reads as a flat cut rather than the cone's tip, slightly hurting clarity. A couple of white dots scattered on the scales look like stray fills rather than intentional snow. Resolve the white top into a clear narrowing tip (or clearly readable snow dusting), and either remove the scattered white dots or commit to them as consistent snow specks. —
—
 fish_fillet
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 minor pending A peach-pink elongated slab with diagonal darker score lines suggesting cut flesh, tilted at an angle. It reads as a fillet/cut of meat but is generic and the lightest pink fill makes it feel flat and low-contrast on the light half. Framing sits a bit low-left of center. Add a darker skin stripe along one edge to read more clearly as fish, and reduce the tilt / re-center within the box. —
—
 furn_bookshelf
Vector icon (animation) player-facingfurniture
 minor pending There is real but very small motion: individual books lean/nudge slightly (the top-right book tilts) and faint sparkle dots flicker, but at a glance it reads almost frozen. The drawing itself is strong and readable with a varied book palette and a green pot. It loops fine, but the animation is so understated it barely registers as moving. Increase the amplitude of the book lean/jostle or add a clearer recurring motion such as the potted plant's leaves swaying so the animation reads at normal playback speed. —
—
 furn_rug
Vector icon (still) unusedfurniture
 minor pending Reads as a decorative rug — deep-red oval field with a concentric diamond medallion and a radiating gold fringe around the edge. Craft is clean and colours are confident, but the top-down orientation differs from the side-on furniture siblings so it sits slightly oddly in the set, and the busy medallion could be mistaken for a shield/emblem at very small sizes. Framing and contrast are fine. Optionally simplify the central medallion slightly so it reads as a rug pattern rather than an emblem at small sizes. —
—
 furn_table
Vector icon (still) unusedfurniture
 minor pending The round wood-grain tabletop on three legs reads as a table, but the bright glowing candle on top competes for the subject and adds ambiguity — at a glance it could read as 'candle' rather than 'table'. Table craft is clean with good grain rings and outline; the candle glow also spills above the design box. Otherwise well centred. Shrink or remove the candle (or make it a small dressing element) so the table is the clear primary subject, and keep any glow inside the design box. —
—
 gem_amethyst
Vector icon (animation) player-facinggems
 minor pending Nicely drawn cluster of purple amethyst points with good value separation and a darker base. Small white sparkle glints appear and disappear on the crystals, so it is animated, but the cluster body itself is static and the twinkle is quite subtle. Loops fine since only the small flecks change. Push the sparkle a bit more (slightly brighter/larger glints or a gentle traveling shine across a facet) so the motion is clearly readable at icon size. —
—
 gem_emerald
Vector icon (animation) player-facinggems
 minor pending Well-drawn emerald-cut rectangular stone with a clean step-cut bevel. It genuinely rotates/tilts in 3D across the strip, but around the mid-rotation frame the gem goes noticeably dark/dim before brightening again, reading as a flicker rather than a smooth pass. The drawing is strong; the lighting dip during the tumble is the only weak spot. Even out the per-frame brightness through the rotation so the gem doesn't dim to a dark silhouette mid-tumble; keep the specular consistent across the tilt. —
—
 gem_geode
Vector icon (still) unusedgems
 minor pending A grey rocky shell opens to a dark cavity radiating teal and purple crystal spokes with small dot inclusions, which does read as a geode cross-section. However the radial spokes plus scattered dots read faintly like a clock/compass face, and the interior is a bit busy/muddy at icon size. The rocky rind has nice soft shading and it is clearly distinct from the clean cut gems. Simplify the interior to fewer, chunkier crystal druzy facets and drop or reduce the scattered dots so it doesn't read as a clock dial. —
—
 gem_opal
Vector icon (still) unusedgems
 minor pending A rounded grey-rimmed cabochon with a milky white body scattered with soft pastel color blobs (blue, green, pink, purple, yellow) and one sparkle, conveying opal's play-of-color. The blobs are quite separated and soft so at a glance it can read like a button or petri dish rather than a glowing opal. Cleanly outlined, well centred, and distinct from the faceted gems. Blend/overlap the color blobs into more iridescent fiery streaks and add subtle internal sheen so it reads as opalescence rather than discrete dots. —
—
 gem_opal
Vector icon (animation) player-facinggems
 minor pending Smooth pearl/opal sphere with a soft top highlight and tiny iridescent lavender/pink flecks. Across frames the flecks drift and shift hue slightly and a faint inner tint moves, but the change is so subtle the icon reads as nearly frozen. The drawing is pretty but the animation barely registers. Strengthen the opal play-of-color: animate the iridescent flecks with more visible hue cycling and a slow drifting inner glow so the motion is clearly perceptible. —
—
 gem_topaz
Vector icon (still) unusedgems
 minor pending A warm amber teardrop/pear-cut gem with internal facet lines forming a central kite shape, a glossy highlight and two sparkles, in golden orange with a brown outline. It reads as a yellow/amber gem but the rounded teardrop with the central pointed facet veers slightly toward a leaf or flame silhouette. Clean craft and well centred, and the amber palette keeps it distinct from the other stones. Sharpen the facet edges (more angular pear-cut crown facets) so the silhouette reads unmistakably as a cut gemstone rather than a leaf/droplet. —
—
 gift_likes
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A skin-toned thumbs-up fist with a green cuff reads as a 'like', matching the label. The two horizontal lines on the side suggest folded fingers but read a bit like random scoring marks at small size. Shading is clean with a good outline, though the thumb proportion is a touch stubby. Make the finger-fold lines slightly curved/clustered so they clearly read as knuckles rather than parallel scratches. —
—
 gold_pan
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 minor pending A wide flat elliptical pan with scattered gold dots/nuggets and a thin arching wire handle reads as a gold-panning dish, though the high overhead ellipse can momentarily read as a UFO/saucer (as the thumbnail suggests). The radial highlight inside is attractive and the gold flecks sell the prospecting theme. Centred and unclipped, but the thin handle nearly disappears at small size. Tilt the pan to a 3/4 view and thicken the handle so it reads as a hand-held pan rather than a flat disc. —
—
 gold_ring
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 minor pending Reads as a gold ring with a mounted blue gem, but the foreshortened oval band plus the large diamond crowding it makes the ring shape slightly hard to parse at a glance. The gold gradient and crossing highlight bands are nice. The gem feels oversized relative to the band. Shrink the diamond slightly and strengthen the ring's inner hole/oval so the band reads as a ring more instantly. —
—
 golden_apple
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsPortal
 minor pending Reads as a golden fruit with a stem and a single green leaf, but the silhouette tapers toward the top and bulges low, so it reads a touch more like a pear than a round apple. The gold gradient and soft highlight are attractive and the leaf/stem combo is clean. Against its sibling golden_carrot/idol it fits the gold family well. Round out the top shoulders and reduce the lower bulge so the silhouette reads unambiguously as an apple rather than a pear. —
—
 golden_sheep
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsPortal
 minor pending The fluffy cloud-like golden fleece with stitched curl detail clearly reads as a sheep's woolly body, and the small dark head with a white eye dot at the right confirms it. However the head is quite small and merges into the body, and the legs are reduced to two thin dark sticks that nearly vanish, so at small size it can read as just a golden cloud/popcorn. Nice warm gold shading on the wool. Enlarge/separate the head slightly and strengthen the legs so the animal silhouette reads at thumbnail size. —
—
 hazard_gas_vent
Vector icon (still) player-facingmineHazards
 minor pending A translucent green ovoid plume with floating bubbles rises from a dark elliptical vent, with a small dark face and green mound at the base. The green glow reads as 'toxic gas' but the central dark blob with two pale eyes reads ambiguously as a tiny skull/creature rather than a vent. The soft translucency is attractive but the focal element is the weakest-defined of the set. Clarify the base element — either commit to a clear skull motif or remove the face so it reads cleanly as a gas vent rather than a creature. —
—
 hazard_mole
Vector icon (still) player-facingmineHazards
 minor pending A dark-brown mole with a pink snout, whiskers, claws and a spiky-fur back pokes out of a brown dirt mound; the animal silhouette reads, but the very dark body nearly merges into the dark background's right half, hurting legibility there. The pink nose and pale claws are the only strong focal points, and the pose is slightly cramped against the lower mound. Lighten the mole's body or add a subtle rim/outline highlight so the silhouette separates from the dark background at small size. —
—
 hazard_rats
Vector icon (still) player-facinghazards
 minor pending Two brown rats with pink ears, pink noses, white-dot eyes and long curling tails read clearly as a rat swarm, and the bold black outline holds up at small size. The light direction is consistent and the pair reads as a 'swarm' better than a single rat would. Minor: the rear rat's tail and body bleed into the dark right background and the front rat's body gradient is a touch muddy/flat where the two overlap. Add a faint rim/lighter edge on the rear rat's tail and right contour so it doesn't disappear on dark backgrounds. —
—
 hazard_smoke
Vector icon (still) player-facinghazards
 minor pending Stacked translucent grey puffs with a thin grey outline rising from a dark oval pit holding two orange embers reads convincingly as smoke, and the orange embers smartly disambiguate it from plain 'cloud'. The translucency that sells smoke also makes it low-contrast and slightly washy on the light half, and the puff outline gets faint. Framing leans low/heavy at the base but stays inside the box. Bump the puff opacity/outline a notch for more punch on the light background and lift the smoke column slightly to better fill the upper box. —
—
 hazard_wolf
Vector icon (still) player-facinghazards
 minor pending A grey snarling muzzle with yellow slit eyes, pointed ears, white fangs and a bold outline is well crafted and threatening, but the short rounded muzzle, small triangular ears and cat-like eyes read clearly as a CAT, not a wolf — it matches the 'hazard_wolf' contact-sheet sibling but contradicts the 'Wolves' label. Centred well and good contrast on both backgrounds. Craft is solid; the subject accuracy is the problem. Lengthen and narrow the snout into a canine muzzle, enlarge/round the ears, add a fur ruff and round the pupils so it reads as a wolf rather than a house cat. —
—
 helmet
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 minor pending A domed amber hard hat with a central glowing round lamp and a projected light cone reads correctly as a miner's helmet. The upward light cone is a nice touch but its pale triangle nearly clips the top magenta bound and can look like a stray fill on the light background. The dome gradient is a little plasticky/pillowy but contrast is fine and it is centred. Shorten or soften the light cone so it doesn't reach the box edge, and flatten the dome shading slightly to reduce the plastic look. —
—
 hoe
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending Wooden handle meeting a dark socket and a flat metal blade at the base reads as a hoe, though the very pale rectangular blade is slightly ambiguous. The blade sits low-left and nearly touches the bottom of the magenta box, leaving the composition bottom-heavy. Nudge the whole tool up and right to centre it, and angle the blade head slightly so it reads more clearly as a hoe edge. —
—
 honeyroll
Vector icon (still) player-facingrecipes
 minor pending The concentric spiral on a glazed golden roll reads as a cinnamon/honey bun, and the seed/specks add detail. The amber teardrop blob at lower-right is meant to be a honey drip but reads as a detached separate object, slightly muddying the silhouette. Light direction and outline are otherwise clean. Integrate the honey drip so it visibly oozes from the roll (a connected drizzle ribbon over the edge) rather than floating as a separate teardrop. —
—
 horseshoe
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftedProducts
 minor pending Solid U horseshoe in cool greys with dark nail holes and a beveled inner channel, reading instantly as a horseshoe and well distinct from the food siblings. One small pale yellow dot near the upper-left inner curve looks like a stray highlight/artifact rather than a nail head. Framing is good though it sits slightly high toward the top bound. Remove or recolour the lone pale-yellow speck on the inner-left curve so it doesn't read as a stray pixel. —
—
 instr_bell
Vector icon (still) unusedinstruments
 minor pending Clean golden bell with dark handle knob and clear soft highlights down the flare; the dark base ellipse with a center stud reads correctly. Framing is the weak point: the bell sits low and slightly right inside the 64px box, leaving a large empty wedge top-left and the design feeling off-center. Outline and gradient shading are confident and on-family. Recenter the bell within the design box and scale up slightly so it fills the frame more evenly. —
—
 instr_fiddle
Vector icon (still) unusedinstruments
 minor pending Reads clearly as a fiddle: scrolled pegbox, fingerboard with strings, amber body with f-holes, and a separate bow. The bow runs off to the right and is clipped right at the magenta design bound, and the body sits a touch left of center. Shading and outline are solid and match the family. Angle or shorten the bow so it stays inside the design box, and nudge the body toward center. —
—
 instr_horn
Vector icon (animation) player-facinginstruments
 minor pending The horn body itself barely moves; the only clear animation is the set of concentric gold sound-arc rings pulsing out from the bell mouth. The horn drawing is murky and very dark (the brass reads almost black with a small gold patch), so it looks more like a silhouette than a polished instrument. It loops fine but the subject is nearly static and low-contrast. Add a slight body bob or bell-flare wobble so the horn moves, and lighten/warm the brass shading so the form reads as a horn rather than a dark blob. —
—
 instr_pan_flute
Vector icon (still) unusedinstruments
 minor pending Reads correctly as a pan flute: a descending row of vertical tubes with dark open tops bound by two horizontal wraps. The diagonal hatching on the binding bands is a touch busy and the cluster sits high-left, leaving empty space lower-left so it feels slightly off-balance. Tube shading and outline are clean and on-family. Recenter the pipe cluster in the box and simplify the binding-band hatching so it doesn't read as noise at small size. —
—
 instr_tambourine
Vector icon (animation) player-facinginstruments
 minor pending The frame is essentially fixed; the only changes are the gold jingle dots shifting slightly around the rim and a faint concentric ripple pulsing in the background disc. There is no real shake/tilt of the tambourine itself, so it reads closer to frozen than playing. The drawing (cream skin, wood rim, jingles) is clean and the loop is seamless, but the motion is too slight. Add a clear shake or rotational wobble of the whole tambourine with the jingles bouncing, so it reads as being struck/shaken rather than nearly static. —
—
 ironframe
Vector icon (still) player-facingrecipes
 minor pending Craft is solid (cool metal, beveled square, glossy ball nodes at corners and mid-edges) but the subject is ambiguous — it reads as a camera mount / mechanical bracket rather than a clear 'iron frame'. The hollow-frame intent is muddied by the heavy dark fill in the centre. Make the frame genuinely open (show through the centre to the background) and simplify the ball nodes to corner brackets/bolts so it reads as a structural iron frame. —
—
 jade
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 minor pending A polished emerald-green ellipse with a big glossy highlight and a thin ring reflection, strong glass-like rendering. It reads as a precious green stone, but it is visually very close to its sibling tile_fish_jade (same green orb), distinct mainly by being smoother. The heavy gloss leans more bubble than cut gem. Push toward a faceted cut-gem read (a few flat facet planes) so polished jade is clearly distinct from the rough jade tile. —
—
 keeper_deer_spirit
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A dark-green quadruped with branching pale antlers, glowing yellow eyes and a soft white aura reads as a forest spirit-deer, fitting the 'keeper' family style. The body is quite blobby and the four legs merge into a dark mass, so the deer silhouette is soft. The glowing antlers and eyes are the strongest read and do the lifting. Tighten the leg separation and slim the body slightly so the deer anatomy reads more clearly at small size. —
—
 keeper_stone_knocker
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A rounded boulder-creature with a thick dark outline, two glowing orange eyes, a zig-zag mouth and small green moss flecks reads as a stone elemental, consistent with the keeper family. The body is fairly flat grey with soft pillow-style shading and low internal contrast, so form is a little muddy. The face is the clear focal point and carries the read. Add a stronger directional light/facet to the stone body to break the flat pillow shading and give it rocky volume. —
—
 magic_fertilizer
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 minor pending Clearly reads as a written scroll/parchment with two wooden roller knobs, purple wavy text lines, and a faceted red diamond seal in the corner. The top wooden bar sits right on the upper magenta bound and the right knob nudges past the right edge, so framing is a touch tight. Craft is solid with good gradient and a confident outline. Nudge the scroll down/in a few pixels so the top roller bar and right knob clear the 64px design box. —
—
 magic_key
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 minor pending Reads as a key: a round looped bow at upper-left, a banded shaft, and a toothed bit at the lower-right, with a warm glow behind the teeth signaling 'magic'. The diagonal layout leaves the upper-right and lower-left corners empty and the bit/teeth end is a little blobby and hard to parse as wards. The bow and shaft shading are clean and confident. Clarify the toothed bit so the key wards read distinctly, and tilt the key a bit more horizontal to fill the box more evenly. —
—
 magic_seed
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 minor pending Reads as a rounded green potion with a wooden cork, strong rim highlight and dark base — appropriate for its 'Potion' label and green #38c878 cue. The pasted white label with text lines feels generic and muddies what should be a magical liquid, and it overlaps the dark glass awkwardly. Outline and gloss are otherwise on-style. Drop or shrink the paper label so the green liquid reads as a magic potion rather than a bottled product, or replace it with a swirl/sparkle accent. —
—
 map_caves
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 minor pending A dark domed shape with a bright yellow oval glow and a small dark slot below reads cleanly as a lit lantern, fitting 'Lanternlit Caves'. The flat near-black body has decent rim shading and four little stud highlights, but the form is a touch ambiguous (could read as a bell or helmet) and the cave context is implied only by the dark mass. Outline and contrast are clean and it sits well within the box. Add a hint of cave-mouth framing (rocky arch silhouette behind/around the lantern) so it reads as caves rather than just a lamp. —
—
 map_meadow
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 minor pending A green hill with a horizontal-rail wooden fence and a sun reads as a fenced meadow/pasture, matching 'Greenmeadow'. The fence pickets are a slightly garish yellow and the rails are a muddy brown that flattens against the green, making the fence read a bit busy and the mound somewhat plain. Framing and outline are fine, but it is the weakest-crafted of the green nodes. Cool the fence pickets to a more natural light-wood tone and add a few flowers or grass tufts on the mound so it reads as a lush meadow rather than just a fenced slope. —
—
 map_quarry
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 minor pending A faceted grey stone block with a wood-handled pickaxe driven in and a cracked seam reads as a quarry/mining site, fitting 'Cracked Quarry'. The pickaxe head silhouette at top is a little hard to parse and there are some stray light grey shards/marks near the base that look like leftover bits rather than intentional debris. Centring leans slightly low-left and the flat grey faces are a touch plain, but it's serviceable. Clean up the stray pale shards near the base and clarify the pickaxe-head shape; add subtle facet shading to break up the flat grey planes. —
—
 miners_hat
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsPortal
 minor pending Reads as a miner's hard hat: a domed brown helmet with a front-mounted lamp emitting a pale triangular light beam upward. The dome shading and the glowing lamp are clear, but the light beam shoots UP and OUT of the design box past the magenta bound, which is awkward framing and pushes the helmet low. The beam is also quite pale and could be mistaken for empty space. Contain the light beam within the box (shorter/angled forward) and lift the helmet to better center it so nothing clips the magenta bound. —
—
 mortar_pestle
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 minor pending Reads as a mortar and pestle: a wide grey stone bowl holding green ground herb/paste, with a pestle resting diagonally into it from the upper-right. The grinding-paste detail is a nice touch, but the pestle is rendered very dark and reads almost as a flat black bar rather than a rounded grinding tool, and the bowl's interior is somewhat muddy where bowl, paste and pestle overlap. Outline is clean and the silhouette is recognizable. Add shading/highlight to the pestle so it reads as a rounded 3D grinding stick rather than a flat dark bar, and clean up the muddy overlap in the bowl. —
—
 nature_feather
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 minor pending Reads as a feather thanks to the white central rachis and diagonal barb hatching, but the outer outline is a hard pointed ellipse that looks more like a stylized leaf than a soft plume. The barbs are neat parallel strokes rather than feathery, so it leans rigid. Color and framing are fine and it's distinct from the green leaf. Soften the outer silhouette with a few split/frayed barb tips and slight asymmetry so it reads as a feather rather than a hard-edged leaf shape. —
—
 nature_leaf
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 minor pending Reads as a leaf but the silhouette is nearly circular with a very heavy near-black outline, making it feel more like a lollipop or coin than foliage. The internal veins and highlight blob are fine, but the rounded shape plus thick rim hurts the leaf read, and the outline is heavier than its siblings. Framing leans slightly high with a long bare stem. Give it a more pointed/ovate leaf tip and thin the outline to match the family weight so it reads clearly as a leaf, not a disc. —
—
 nature_mushroom_cluster
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 minor pending Comparing adjacent frames the cluster is effectively frozen — the caps, stems and green moss mound show no perceptible bob, sway or highlight travel, so it reads as a static icon dropped into the strip. The underlying drawing is fine: rounded tan caps with rim shading on a green base and a soft shadow. Because it's pretty but not animated it should at least breathe. Add a gentle cap bob / mound breathe or a slow highlight pass so the cluster actually animates instead of holding still. —
—
 nature_spiderweb
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 minor pending It does read as a spider web (radial spokes plus concentric spiral with a few blue dew beads), but the line work is very low-contrast and washes out badly on the light background, hurting readability at small size. The blue droplet beads are nicely rendered but compete with the dewdrop icon. Centered and symmetric, just too faint. Increase the web line contrast (darker/thicker strands or a subtle outline glow) so it survives on the light background at icon size. —
—
 nature_spiderweb
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 minor pending The web mesh itself stays rigid, but small dew/sparkle dots twinkle and shift position across the strands, so it is technically animated via an overlay rather than the web moving. The strip renders quite small/low-detail and the strand drawing is faint against the split background. The twinkle is subtle and the web's rigidity makes it read closer to a still than the swaying subjects in this batch. Add a faint overall web shimmer or a slight sag/sway of the strands so motion isn't only the sparkle overlay, and render the web lines with a touch more contrast. —
—
 oar
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 minor pending A wooden shaft with a rounded leaf-shaped blade and a contrasting grip tip reads as an oar. The blade is a little stubby and rounded so it edges toward looking like a paddle or small spoon at a glance. Clean outline, good wood shading and a clear highlight crease on the blade keep it on-style. Lengthen and flatten the blade slightly into a more elongated oar paddle shape to remove the spoon/paddle ambiguity. —
—
 ore_silver
Vector icon (still) unusedores
 minor pending The same faceted sphere as copper/iron but with bright white-silver zigzag bands, which at least reads as a different metal and is brighter/cooler. It still shares the reused ball silhouette with two siblings, so distinctness is weak, and the white veins are slightly chalky rather than metallic-reflective. Outline and contrast are solid. Give the silver veins a cooler blue-grey metallic gradient instead of flat white, and ideally vary the silhouette so it is not the third copy of the same rock-ball. —
—
 ore_silver
Vector icon (animation) player-facingores
 minor pending A clean low-poly silver nugget with cool grey shading and sharp white sparkle points; mean brightness eases up and down (~127->133->127) so a faint specular shimmer travels across it, but the change is quite small and reads as nearly still. Drawing and palette are solid and metallic. Loops cleanly with no artifacts. Increase the amplitude of the highlight sweep or add a brief discrete sparkle/glint pop so the motion is more clearly readable at icon size. —
—
 pact_scroll
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending The rounded top/bottom edges and flat side make this read more like a cylindrical can or jar than a scroll or pact document; the orange chart icon in the centre is an odd, unexplained motif. Craft is fine (cream parchment gradient, ruled lines, dark outline) but the silhouette undersells 'scroll' and the mountain/chart glyph is confusing. Curl the top and bottom into visible rolled scroll ends (and drop or replace the orange chart glyph with a seal/sigil) so it reads as a rolled document rather than a canister. —
—
 pact_violation
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A faceted red orb split by a thick white-outlined lightning/crack reads reasonably as a broken or violated pact. However the white bolt sits a bit off-centre to the right and competes with the gem facets, making the read slightly busy. Contrast and gloss are otherwise clean. Centre the crack/bolt on the orb and thin its outline slightly so the fracture reads as the gem breaking rather than a separate overlaid bolt. —
—
 pet_cat
Vector icon (animation) player-facingpets
 minor pending The drawing is clean and cute with green eyes and a striped head, and the bushy tail sweeps from the left side around to the right across the strip. The only moving part is the tail; the head, ears and body are completely frozen so it reads as barely animated. No artifacts and it loops acceptably since the tail returns near its start. Add a subtle secondary motion such as an ear twitch, a slow blink, or a gentle breathing bob so the cat doesn't look static apart from the tail. —
—
 pet_chick
Vector icon (animation) player-facingpets
 minor pending Charming round yellow chick with a wispy head-tuft and orange beak/feet. Frame 2 shows a full hop with the body lifting and feet off the ground, and one later frame is a blink, but the remaining ~11 frames are essentially identical and frozen. The single hop is a hard pop rather than an eased jump, so motion feels sparse and slightly abrupt. Spread the hop over several eased frames (anticipation crouch then arc) and add a gentle idle bob between hops so the chick isn't static most of the loop. —
—
 pet_dog
Vector icon (still) unusedpets
 minor pending Reads as a puppy — floppy brown ears, cream muzzle/belly, pink tongue, dark nose, curled tail. Slightly monkey-ish because the wide flat muzzle plus rounded ears read a touch primate at a glance, but the tongue and proportions pull it back to dog. Outline and shading are clean and on-family; well centred. Tighten the muzzle so it's less round/flat and seat the ears higher and more hound-like to kill the faint monkey read. —
—
 pet_frog
Vector icon (animation) player-facingpets
 minor pending Expressive green frog with big domed eyes and a pale belly; the first ~5 frames have strong motion — the mouth opens wide into a croak and the body inflates — but from frame 6 onward it settles into a near-static smiling pose for the rest of the strip. The animation is front-loaded and the loop is poor: the big open-mouthed start does not match the flat resting end, so the wrap pops. Redistribute the croak across the loop and let the mouth close back to the resting pose at the end (or re-open at the very end) so the first and last frames match and motion isn't crammed into the first third. —
—
 pet_hedgehog
Vector icon (animation) player-facingpets
 minor pending Nicely drawn hedgehog with a cream face and a brown spine-cap; over the strip the spines progressively bristle, growing longer and spreading wider — a distinctive, on-theme motion. The problem is the loop: it starts with short flat spines and ends with fully extended spikes, so the wrap from last frame back to first is a hard pop rather than a return. No glow or drift, just the loop discontinuity. Make the bristle motion cyclic (extend then relax back to the starting flat state within the 14 frames) so the spikes return to their start and the loop wraps smoothly. —
—
 pet_lamb
Vector icon (still) unusedpets
 minor pending Reads as a lamb — bumpy cloud-like wool outline, cream face with dark dot eyes and a small mouth, curly fleece swirls, tiny dark hooves. Like the goat, the white body leans low-contrast on the light half, though the cloud-bump outline and C-curl fleece marks keep it readable. Tucked legs make it read as a near-static woolly ball, slightly limbless. Slightly deepen the body's outline/shadow so the fleece reads against the light background, and hint at a bit more leg so it doesn't read as legless. —
—
 pet_lamb
Vector icon (animation) player-facingpets
 minor pending Lovely woolly lamb with curl-textured fleece, rosy cheeks and a tiny crown of horns; the craft is the strongest in the set. However motion is almost absent — only frame 2 shows a small head tilt/bounce and the other ~13 frames are identical, so it reads as effectively frozen. No artifacts, but it barely qualifies as animated. Add a continuous idle such as a slow fleece-breathing rise/fall or an ear flick across the whole loop instead of a single isolated bob. —
—
 pet_piglet
Vector icon (animation) player-facingpets
 minor pending Cute round pink piglet with a snout and curly tail; across the strip the ears and one front trotter shift slightly and the eyes glance to the side near the end. The changes are very subtle and there is no clear single motion beat, so it reads as gently fidgeting rather than purposefully animated. No flashing, popping or drift. Commit to one readable idle motion — e.g. an ear flick paired with a breathing bob — with a bit more amplitude so the piglet doesn't look near-frozen. —
—
 phase_charter_stirs
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A wood-rod pennant in tan/terracotta with a small cream note and a dark diagonal pen reads loosely as 'charter being written/stirring,' but the pen is rendered as a thin black silhouette that reads more like a dart or stick at small size. The banner family styling and gloss are consistent; framing is good. Make the pen/quill clearly a writing implement (lighter colour, nib detail, resting on the note) so the 'charter stirs' authoring metaphor reads at a glance. —
—
 philosophers_stone
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsPortal
 minor pending A pentagonal red faceted gem with a bright central glint sits inside a thin gold circle — reads clearly as a magical stone/gem. Craft is solid with believable facet shading, but the white facet lines plus the four-point starburst are a touch busy and the gem slightly overlaps/breaks the thin gold ring at the bottom. Distinct from siblings, good contrast on both grounds. Tuck the gem fully inside the gold ring (or thicken the ring) and tone down the white facet lines so the central glint reads as the focal point. —
—
 player_shuffle
Vector icon (still) player-facingplayerTools
 minor pending A side-on brown horn with a flared bell, gold key-shaped buttons, three yellow sound-wave arcs and a blue circular refresh arrow reads as a reshuffle horn, but the body is a bit busy and the dark padded mouthpiece at far left looks like a separate blob. The blue loop arrow is the strongest 'reshuffle' cue and is legible; sound arcs and arrow both compete for attention. Light direction is consistent and it sits centred, though the horn's flat brown midsection is slightly muddy. Simplify the horn body and lift the mouthpiece value so it doesn't read as a detached lump; make the blue reshuffle arrow slightly larger/cleaner as the primary symbol so the intent reads faster at small size. —
—
 quest_order
Vector icon (still) player-facingquests
 minor pending The motif is a brown handled basket/hamper with a little hanging label, intended to read as a fulfillment 'order' but it leans close to quest_collect's basket, risking a look-alike clash. The scroll, seal and craft are all on-family and clean. The distinguishing tag/label is small and the basket is fairly plain, so the order-vs-collect distinction is weaker than ideal at glance size. Differentiate the order motif more strongly from quest_collect, e.g. emphasize the shipping/tag element (a clear checklist clipboard or a tied delivery crate with a prominent tag) so the two baskets don't read as duplicates. —
—
 rake
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending Wooden handle and a wide tined head read correctly as a rake. The tines are dark and muddy where they overlap the handle, reducing clarity, and the head sits low-left close to the box edge. Lighten/separate the tines for cleaner contrast and shift the composition up to centre it better. —
—
 rare
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 minor pending Reads strongly as a treasure chest — rounded lid, gold star burst, central keyhole lock and dark metal banding — which is close to but not exactly the 'Lockbox' label (it skews chest rather than box). Craft is good: bold outline, believable wood grain shading, metal corner caps. Well centred and clipping-free within the magenta bounds. Optional: square off the lid and de-emphasise the treasure star to read more as a utilitarian lockbox than a pirate chest. —
—
 reef_crab
Vector icon (animation) player-facingreef
 minor pending There is motion but it reads as a slight whole-body bob plus a small claw/leg twitch rather than a distinct scuttle; the eye-stalks and claws shift only subtly between frames. The crab drawing itself is decent though a bit muddy against the dark accent disc, and the shadow is heavy. It loops without artifacts but the animation feels timid rather than lively. Increase the claw raise and add an alternating leg-step or sideways sway so it reads as a scuttle rather than a faint bob. —
—
 reef_jellyfish
Vector icon (still) unusedreef
 minor pending Reads clearly as a jellyfish: domed blue bell with white highlights and trailing pink/cyan tentacles. The drawing is clean, but a large soft radial glow surrounds it that, on the dark half, blooms into a visible grey halo and slightly muddies the silhouette at icon size. The bell and tentacles themselves are well drawn and centred. Tighten or remove the wide radial glow so the bell silhouette stays crisp and does not become a grey halo on dark backgrounds. —
—
 reef_octopus
Vector icon (still) unusedreef
 minor pending Reads clearly as an octopus: bulbous purple head with two big cartoon eyes, a smile and a fan of eight tentacles with suckers. Glossy purple gradient and clean outline look good and on-style. The lower tentacles are a little busy and merge into a slightly tangled mass at small size, and a faint grey smudge of shadow sits under the body on the light half. Increase spacing/contrast between the lower tentacles so they read as distinct legs rather than a blob, and tidy the soft under-body shadow. —
—
 reef_starfish
Vector icon (animation) player-facingreef
 minor pending It is animated mainly by a slow whole-icon rotation with twinkling speckle highlights rather than any organic arm flex, so it leans toward a rigid spin. The starfish itself is well crafted with a warm orange ramp, dark outline and bumpy texture dots. It loops acceptably and has no blow-out, but the motion is the least lifelike of the set. Add a subtle per-arm curl/breathing in addition to the rotation so the limbs feel alive rather than spinning as one rigid shape. —
—
 region_forest
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A dark-green glass orb with an outlined triangle reads as a single conifer/forest marker, but the green-symbol-on-green-orb combination is low-contrast and the lone triangle is generic (could read as 'play' or a tent). There is also a tiny stray orange/brown pixel near the triangle base. It is the murkiest of the region orbs. Lighten the tree glyph (or add a trunk and a second smaller tree) for stronger contrast against the green orb, and remove the stray brown pixel at the triangle base. —
—
 region_mine
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A grey glass orb with a snow-capped twin-peak mountain reads as 'mountain/region' more than 'mine' specifically. Contrast is acceptable (white snow cap helps) but the all-grey orb feels muddy and the mountain motif overlaps conceptually with region_moor's hill. It does fit the family but is not strongly 'mine.' Add a mine-specific cue (a pickaxe, mine entrance, or ore/gem glint) so it reads as a mine rather than a generic mountain region. —
—
 region_moor
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending A brown glass orb with a low rolling hill outline and a small green grass sprig reads loosely as moor/heath/peat. The motifs are subtle and low-contrast (brown line on brown orb), so the read is weak at small size and the hill silhouette risks confusion with region_mine's mountain. Family styling and gloss are consistent. Increase contrast of the hill outline and play up moor-specific cues (more heather/grass tufts or a boggy texture) so it reads distinctly from the mine/mountain orb. —
—
 rifle
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending The wooden stock, grey barrel and trigger area read as a rifle, and a small gold rectangle below works as a cartridge/bullet. The whole gun sits fairly small in the box and the barrel/stock contrast is a little flat, but it is recognizable. Scale the rifle up to fill more of the design box and add a touch more highlight to the barrel for crisper metal. —
—
 rope_coil
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 minor pending Stacked tapered tan rings with rope-twist hatching and a single strand looping down at the left read as a coil of rope, though the conical stack also flirts with a beehive/basket silhouette. The twist detailing and brown outline are clean and the trailing loop is the clearest rope cue. Centred and unclipped, but the cone taper undercuts the 'rope' read. Make the coil more cylindrical (even-diameter loops) rather than a tapered cone, and emphasise the loose trailing end to lock in the rope reading. —
—
 rune_stone
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 minor pending A tilted dark stone tablet bearing a cyan glowing upward double-arrow (Tiwaz-like rune) — reads as a rune stone, helped by the engraved-crack detail and a subtle top highlight. The neon-cyan rune is attractive but the slab is quite dark/muddy on the dark background, costing some contrast on that half. Centred and distinct, though the very dark stone fill flattens the form. Lighten the stone fill a notch (or add a rim-light edge) so the slab silhouette holds up on the dark background, and ensure the rune isn't clipped by the slab edge. —
—
 saddle
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending The glossy domed brown form with a single dashed seam reads more as an insect carapace or coffee bean than a saddle; there is no recognizable pommel, cantle, stirrup, or skirt silhouette. A small brown peg leg pokes out the back-right which is confusing. Craft is clean (good outline, soft highlight) but subject accuracy is the weak point. Redraw with a clear saddle profile: a curved seat with a raised pommel at front, a back cantle, and a hanging stirrup/fender, viewed at three-quarter so the silhouette reads as tack rather than a bean. —
—
 scythe
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending Reads cleanly as a bladed harvest tool: angled wooden shaft with a metal collar/grip mid-shaft and a curved steel blade at top, good metallic highlight and clean outline. The issue is family-level: it is pixel-identical to the sickle icon, so the two are indistinguishable look-alikes despite being separate keys. A true scythe should have a long shaft and a larger sweeping blade versus the short one-handed sickle. Differentiate from sickle: give the scythe a longer two-grip snath and a longer, lower-angled blade so the silhouette is distinct from the short curved sickle. —
—
 season_winter
Vector icon (still) player-facingfixed-icons
 minor pending Darker teal-blue orb carries a white snowflake with a slightly chunkier, more 3D arrowhead look; it reads as winter/cold but is visually close to region_tundra's blue snowflake-orb. The flake's centre is a little soft/muddy where the arms meet compared to the crisp tundra gem. Contrast and gloss are otherwise solid. Sharpen the snowflake's central junction and push the orb hue cooler/darker to harden the distinction from region_tundra's brighter faceted flake. —
—
 settlement_unfounded
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A dashed brown placeholder box with a small flag and an amber question mark reads as an undiscovered/empty settlement site, which is conceptually right. But it is busier and lower-contrast than its founded/complete siblings, and the elements (flag, dashes, question mark) crowd inside the small box so it reads less instantly. The faint fill makes it weak on the light half. Simplify to one dominant cue (centred question mark inside the dashed plot), drop or shrink the flag, and raise the dash/question-mark contrast so it reads on the light background. —
—
 sickle
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending Same well-crafted bladed-tool image as scythe: angled wooden shaft, metal collar, curved steel blade with clean outline and good highlight. As a sickle it should read as a short one-handed curved knife, but here it is identical to the scythe icon, so the pair are visually interchangeable. Subject is in the right ballpark but not specifically a sickle. Redraw as a compact one-handed sickle — short handle, deeply hooked blade — so it is clearly distinct from the longer scythe. —
—
 sledgehammer
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 minor pending The head reads as a hollow open-faced box with a glassy blue-grey vertical gradient rather than a solid heavy steel sledge block, so it looks more like a light mallet than a weighty sledgehammer. The wooden handle is noticeably bent/curved instead of a straight confident shaft, which weakens the tool read. Palette, outline and drop shadow are otherwise consistent with the coal_hammer/pickaxe siblings, but it risks looking like a slight variant of coal_hammer. Make the head a solid filled steel block (flat metallic shading, not a hollow recessed panel) and straighten the handle to a clean diagonal; bulk up the head slightly so it reads heavier and clearly distinct from coal_hammer. —
—
 spell_arcane_missile
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 minor pending Glossy purple orb with an elliptical ring, a soft lavender comet streak to the lower-left and four-point sparkles — clean gradient and confident highlight. The craft is good and it is distinct in the family, but it reads as a ringed planet or comet rather than an 'arcane missile'; the ring in particular pulls it toward Saturn. Centred and well within the magenta bounds. Drop the Saturn-style ring and reshape into a directional bolt/dart of arcane energy (an elongated leading point + trailing motion streak) so it reads as a projectile, not a planet. —
—
 spell_heal
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 minor pending The cross shape is locked static and centered; the animation is the surrounding green aura gently pulsing in/out plus small white sparkles twinkling near the corners. It's pleasant and loops cleanly but the icon itself never moves, so it reads as a still icon with an ambient glow rather than a real animation. The cross drawing is clean with a soft inner glow. Animate the cross itself — a gentle breathing scale pulse or an upward shimmer sweep across the symbol — so the heal feels active rather than just a static badge behind a pulsing glow. —
—
 spell_lightning
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 minor pending The bolt shape itself is essentially static and centered; nearly all the motion is the background radial glow brightening and dimming (a flash pulse) plus tiny white highlight shifts on the bolt. It reads more as a background flicker than the bolt crackling or animating, so it feels half-animated. The bolt drawing is clean with nice glossy blue-to-white shading, and it loops fine. Add actual motion to the bolt itself: jitter/redraw the zigzag segments or pulse a white core flicker along the bolt so the energy crackles rather than only the backdrop flashing. —
—
 spell_poison
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 minor pending Green gradient teardrop with a bright specular streak, scattered bubble circles and a small drip below, in a green glow — reads as poison/toxin well. However the outline is a heavy near-black stroke that is noticeably thicker and darker than the lighter outlines used across the rest of the family, so it sits slightly apart stylistically. Framing and contrast are fine. Lighten/thin the black outline to match the softer outline weight of the sibling icons so it is consistent with the family. —
—
 spellbook
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 minor pending A maroon hardcover book seen face-on with a golden glowing eye on the cover and page-edge hatching on both sides — reads as a forbidden/eye spellbook. The symmetric page-edges on both left AND right make the volume read slightly flat/ambiguous (looks more like a closed double-sided object than a single book), and the dark cover loses some edge definition on the dark background. Distinct subject, clean eye motif. Give the book a slight 3/4 tilt or a visible spine so it reads as a single closed tome, and add a rim-light on the cover edge for separation on dark. —
—
 spyglass
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 minor pending The tapered two-section brass tube with banding rings and a blue glass lens at the wide end reads clearly as a spyglass. However the narrow eyepiece end extends past the left magenta design bound, so the icon is off-centre and clips the box. The metallic shading and lens are nicely done. Recentre and scale the spyglass to sit fully inside the design box so the eyepiece end no longer crosses the magenta bound. —
—
 supplies
Vector icon (still) player-facingmissingItems
 minor pending A wooden barrel with vertical staves topped by provisions (a red apple, breadstick/loaf, utensils) — reads as a stash of supplies. Good barrel shading, but a brown crate/strap attachment on the right edge clips and overruns the magenta design box, and that side element is murky/ambiguous on the dark field. The contents on top are slightly busy at icon size. Pull the right-side crate/strap inside the design box and simplify it so the silhouette stays clean and centered. —
—
 terrier
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 minor pending Reads as a small dog/terrier: pointed alert ears, a round head with a dark eye, a tan oval body with a tapered tail and four blocky legs. The body shading uses a pale cream-to-gold gradient that is a touch washed-out and plasticky next to the warmer family palette, and the stubby rectangular legs feel stiff. Outline and framing are solid. Warm up and de-glossify the body gradient to match the family browns, and shape the legs with a slight taper/paw rather than flat rectangles for a livelier silhouette. —
—
 tile_bird_chicken/trans-summer-autumn
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_bird_chicken
 minor pending The pad believably dries from green summer grass toward the brown autumn mound, and the hen identity is preserved and centred throughout. Early frames add small yellow-orange flecks meant as falling leaves, but a few read as thin streaky comet/spark dashes rather than clearly leaf-shaped, slightly cheapening the effect. No white-out or popping, so it stays minor. Make the falling-leaf flecks in the first ~4 frames read as small leaf shapes (2-3px tumbling marks) rather than thin diagonal streaks so they don't look like sparks. —
—
 tile_bird_clover
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingFarm
 minor pending It reads as a green four-leaf-ish sprout with a tiny pale bud at the center, but the leaves are plain rounded blobs rather than the notched heart-shaped trefoil/quatrefoil lobes that say 'clover' at a glance, so it can read as a generic seedling. Craft is fine: clean dark-green outlines, decent light side, centred composition. The empty upper half of the box leaves it sitting a touch low. Add the characteristic clover-leaf notch (heart-shaped lobes with a pale chevron line) to the leaflets and tighten them into a clearer four-leaf cluster to lock the clover read; nudge the whole subject up slightly to better fill the box. —
—
 tile_bird_clover
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 minor pending Clearly reads as clover/shamrocks with clean leaf clusters, good green hue-ramp from dark outlines to bright fills, and tiny pale flower dots dressing the pad. Subject is well centred on the mossy pad and the silhouette is crisp with no fringing. Only quibble: this is a plant, not a bird, so it is mislabelled within the tile_bird_* group, but as art it is solid. — —
—
 tile_bird_hen
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 minor pending Reads as a brooding/nesting hen: small head with red comb tucked onto a large brown body, dotted speckle wing detail, and a white egg beside it that reinforces the hen theme. The very low-slung sitting pose makes the head almost merge into the body, slightly hurting glance-readability versus the upright chicken. Craft is clean with a nice warm gradient and the egg adds useful identity. Lift or separate the head from the body mass slightly so the bird silhouette reads faster at small size. —
—
 tile_bird_melon
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 minor pending Unmistakably a watermelon with bold dark-green stripes over light green and a small brown stem, sitting centred on the pad with a clean rounded silhouette. Shading reads a touch flat/uniform across the rind so it looks slightly more like a beach ball than a heavy fruit, but it is clean with no artifacts. Like clover this is a non-bird subject in the tile_bird_* group. Add a stronger highlight band and deeper bottom shadow on the rind to sell the fruit's roundness and weight. —
—
 tile_bird_phoenix
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 minor pending Conveys a phoenix: glowing orange body, upswept flame-like wings, a flame crest, a dark sweeping tail and sparkle accents, all over a warm radial glow. The fiery glow is dramatic but somewhat washes out the silhouette and the wing/flame shapes get busy and slightly hard to parse at small size. The glow also bleeds well outside the 64px box, which could clip or halo oddly when tiled. Tighten the glow radius within the design box and simplify the wing/flame shapes so the bird silhouette reads more cleanly at icon size. —
—
 tile_cattle_longhorn
Vector icon (still) player-facingcattle
 minor pending Reads as a brown bovine with a pair of long curved horns sweeping back over the head, a gradient-shaded tan body and blocky legs, matching the family style and distinct from the cow. The wide back-swept horns read more like a ram/curl than the forward-spreading sweep of a classic Texas Longhorn, and the white facial highlight blob is a bit heavy. Outline, centring and contrast are otherwise solid. Reshape the horns to spread more horizontally/forward (the signature wide Longhorn span) and tone down the white face highlight so it doesn't read as a glare patch. —
—
 tile_fish_jade
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 minor pending A glossy green ball with a top highlight and a few darker green pits/spots suggesting raw stone. Craft is clean, but it reads as a generic green orb and sits very close to the polished jade gem sibling; the rough-vs-polished distinction is subtle at small size. Give the rough jade an irregular chunky angular nodule silhouette rather than a perfect sphere to separate it from the polished jade gem. —
—
 tile_fish_kelp
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 minor pending A vertical winding green stem with alternating pointed leaves and a few small grey bubbles to one side; the leaf shapes read as a terrestrial vine or bean plant rather than kelp/seaweed. It is the busiest, least kelp-like icon and the silhouette is thin and tippy. Framing is tall and slightly off-center. Switch to broad ribbon-like kelp fronds off a central stipe instead of pointed vine leaves, and add a holdfast at the base so it reads as seaweed. —
—
 tile_fish_kelp
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fish_*
 minor pending The seaweed/kelp blades read well and the bottom tidepool/rock pad is centred and detailed, giving a nice 'underwater plant' impression. However the dark olive fronds are low-contrast against the dark cell half so the upper silhouette muddies, and there is a small detached blue-striped fish floating at mid-right that adds clutter and competes with the main subject. The frond interiors are also fairly flat with little internal hue-ramp. Lift the frond highlights (warmer/lighter green edge light) for silhouette pop against dark backgrounds, and either remove the loose side fish or fold it into the base so the kelp reads as the single subject. —
—
 tile_fish_mackerel
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 minor pending A side-profile blue fish with a white eye, gilled head, vertical dark dorsal bars and a clean fanned tail; confidently drawn with good outline and contrast. The issue is consistency: its silhouette is essentially identical to the sardine sibling, differing only by hue and stripes, hard to tell apart at icon size. Differentiate the two fish in shape: give mackerel a fatter body with bold tiger-stripe bars and the sardine a slimmer body, so they are not interchangeable silhouettes. —
—
 tile_fish_sardine
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 minor pending A side-profile silvery-grey fish with a white eye, gill curve, faint lateral line and fanned tail; clean and readable as a small fish. As with mackerel the silhouette is near-identical to its sibling, so the two fish read as recolors of one shape rather than two species. Slim and shorten the body and brighten to a true silver belly so the sardine is clearly distinct from the chunkier striped mackerel. —
—
 tile_fish_sardine
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fish_*
 minor pending The slender silver-blue fish is anatomically clear with a pointed snout, eye and forked tail, and the little white water-splash flecks near the tail add life. The main weakness is centring/composition: the fish sits high and its nose pushes to the very top edge while the green pad floats low and slightly disconnected beneath it, so the subject does not sit comfortably ON the pad. Body shading is a bit flat/uniform silver with limited hue-ramp compared to the mackerel. Lower and re-seat the fish so it rests centred on the grass pad (close the gap between fish and pad), and add a touch more belly-to-back tonal ramp for volume. —
—
 tile_fruit_blackberry
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fruit_*
 minor pending Reads clearly as blackberries: clustered dark purple-black drupelets with bumpy aggregate texture, green serrated leaves, and a small white blossom. The very dark berry mass risks reading as a muddy blob at true board size since it sits close to its own outline, but the leaf and blossom rescue subject clarity. Centring on the pad is fine; no stray pixels noted. Push a touch more rim-light/lighter purple highlights onto the berries so the drupelet clusters stay separated at small size rather than merging into one dark mass. —
—
 tile_fruit_coconut
Vector icon (still) player-facingfruits
 minor pending The three germination pores and the round brown body do point to a coconut, but the dense radiating tan strokes read more like a hairy pollen-burst or fibrous seed than coconut husk fibre. The face-like arrangement of the three holes also slightly fights legibility at small size. Calm the radiating strokes into a coarser fibrous-husk texture (fewer, thicker, more clustered fibres) so the round husk reads first and the three pores read as detail, not a face. —
—
 tile_fruit_jackfruit
Vector icon (still) player-facingfruits
 minor pending The diamond lattice over a yellow-green sphere reasonably evokes jackfruit's knobby rind, and the small leaf helps. However it is fairly generic and could be mistaken for a patterned melon or durian; the diamonds are flat and uniform rather than reading as raised bumps. Give the diamond bumps slight individual shading/raise so they read as 3D knobs, and consider a more oblong jackfruit silhouette to differentiate from the round melon read. —
—
 tile_fruit_starfruit
Vector icon (still) player-facingfruits
 minor pending It is drawn as a literal five-pointed gold star (the carambola cross-section) with faceted segments, which is a clean readable shape but reads primarily as a generic reward/star icon rather than a fruit. Within a fruit family this risks being mistaken for a currency or rating token, and the gold colour overlaps with the golden apple's palette. Shift the colour toward yellow-green and add a slight 3D ridged-fruit body (or ribbed edges) so it reads as a carambola fruit rather than a flat gold reward star. —
—
 tile_grain_manna
Vector icon (still) player-facinggrain
 minor pending Three rounded cream globules with gold outlines and heavy soft radial gradients plus an outer glow on the dark side. As a mythical resource manna has no canonical form so the soft-orb read is defensible, but the glossy gradient and glow break from the flatter, harder-edged style of its siblings and make it read more like dewdrops/pearls than a grain. Framing is good and centred. Flatten the radial gradients toward the family's cleaner cel shading and drop the outer glow so it sits stylistically with corn/buckwheat/rice. —
—
 tile_grass_grass
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingFarm
 minor pending Reads fine on its own as a tuft of tall grass blades rising from a brown soil mound, with believable blade overlap and a lighter green highlight blade. The concern is family distinctness: it is nearly identical to tile_grass_meadow, which is the same clump differing only by a tiny flower. Craft is solid (clean rounded blades, consistent light), framing centred. Differentiate the three grass tiles more strongly - give plain 'grass' a looser, more splayed/windblown silhouette or a distinct blade shape so it does not collide with meadow grass at thumbnail size. —
—
 tile_grass_grass
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grass_*
 minor pending Reads clearly as a tuft of grass with a believable hue-ramp from dark base to bright lime crown and a clean fluffy silhouette; the soft oval pad with small yellow dot-flowers anchors it and is centred. The blob is a touch generic and the flower dots are a little arbitrary/noisy, but pixel clusters are clean with no fringing or stray pixels against the dark side. — —
—
 tile_grass_meadow
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingFarm
 minor pending A near-twin of tile_grass_grass: the same dense clump of green blades on a soil mound, distinguished only by one small white/yellow daisy nestled low on the left, which is easy to miss at icon size. The flower itself is a slightly muddy little cluster. Otherwise craft and centring are fine. Make the meadow read distinct from plain grass: enlarge or add a couple more clearly-shaped flowers (lift one higher among the blades), and clean up the flower so it does not read as a smudge at small size. —
—
 tile_grass_spiky
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grass_*
 minor pending Clearly reads as a stiff spiky succulent/agave rosette radiating from a centre, with a sharp silhouette and a cool teal-to-dark-green ramp that distinguishes it from the warmer grass tiles. The blade interiors are slightly flat and a few central blades read a touch muddy where they overlap, but it's centred on a clean pad with no fringing. Add one brighter teal/highlight pass along the upper edge of the central blades to lift the muddy overlap zone and give the rosette more dimensional pop. —
—
 tile_herd_boar
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 minor pending The dark near-black body, spiky mane ridge, curled tail and small white tusk read as a wild boar, and the single red eye adds a feral note. The problem is family distinctiveness: it is very close to the warthog and shares the bristle-back/dark-body language, so the trio (boar/hog/warthog) blurs together at icon size. Outline and shading are clean and well-centred. Push differentiation from the warthog — e.g. make the boar darker/leaner with a more pronounced spiky dorsal ridge and drop the tusks (reserve big tusks for the warthog), or shift hue slightly cooler. —
—
 tile_herd_boar
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 minor pending Reads correctly as a shaggy wild boar with tusks, lighter snout and dark legs, decently shaded and centred on the pad. Craft is fine but the dark-on-dark fur flattens the form so it reads a touch muddy at glance size, and it is almost indistinguishable from tile_herd_hog and tile_herd_warthog. The silhouette is clean with no obvious fringing.
