Differentiate it from hog/warthog — e.g. push warmer/redder fur or a distinct mane stripe, and add a touch more rim light so the legs separate from the body. —
—
 tile_herd_goat
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 minor pending It carries back-swept ribbed horns, a small beard wattle and a slotted goat eye, but the cream gradient body and head proportions read almost identically to the alpaca and lack a clear goat read at a glance. The big plain oval body with no neck definition feels underdesigned versus siblings. Outline and shadow are clean and framing is centred. Strengthen the goat read: add the signature rectangular pupil more prominently, a longer chin beard, and a perkier upright/short-tail and slimmer caprine body so it stops echoing the alpaca. —
—
 tile_herd_hog
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 minor pending A warm mid-brown rounded body with a clear pink-ringed snout, white eye and a small bristly back ridge — reads as a hog/boar hybrid. It sits awkwardly between the pink pig and the dark boar/warthog and is not strongly distinct from them; the back-bristle detail is faint at size. Craft is solid: clean outline, smooth gradient, consistent shadow, well-centred. Differentiate from boar/warthog and pig — lean it toward a domestic hog (rounder, smoother back, larger snout, drop or reduce the spiky mane) or commit it to the wild cluster, so the four pig-likes don't all crowd the same read. —
—
 tile_herd_hog
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 minor pending Reads as a wild hog with a long snout, bristled back and stocky legs, warmer brown than the boar and reasonably shaded and centred. It is competent but nearly a sibling to tile_herd_boar and tile_herd_warthog, so on a shared board the three would read as the same tile. Silhouette is clean with no transparency artifacts. Make it visually distinct from boar/warthog — lean into a lighter, more domestic brown or a different pose so the three wild-pig tiles don't read as duplicates. —
—
 tile_herd_warthog
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 minor pending Dark brown body with prominent curved white tusks, a spiky dorsal mane, a facial wart spot and curled tail — it does read as a warthog. But it overlaps heavily with the boar (both dark, bristly-backed, tusked), hurting at-a-glance distinctiveness within the trio. Craft itself is clean: solid outline, gradient body, consistent shadow, well-centred. Differentiate from the boar: make the warthog grayer/leaner with much bigger outward-curving tusks and a more prominent warted face, and keep the boar darker/spikier-backed without tusks. —
—
 tile_herd_warthog
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 minor pending Correctly reads as a warthog — long snout, prominent curved tusks, bristly mane and stocky grey-brown body, centred on the pad with a clean silhouette. The art itself is competent but it is the third dark wild-pig in this batch and overlaps heavily with boar and hog at board glance size. Tusks are its main distinguishing feature; otherwise it blends into the trio. Strengthen the warthog-specific cues (bigger tusks, facial warts/skin patches, leaner grey body) so it is unmistakable next to boar and hog. —
—
 tile_mine_gold
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 minor pending A round yellow-gold nugget with a dark rim, glossy highlight blob and several brown pits plus pale sparkles, reading as a gold nugget though the round cratered look drifts toward a ball/moon/cheese. The smooth circular silhouette is less mineral than the faceted siblings, weakening the 'ore lump' feel. Palette and gloss are clean and clearly gold. Roughen the silhouette into a more irregular, faceted nugget chunk so it reads as raw gold rather than a smooth cratered sphere. —
—
 tile_mine_gold
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mine_*
 minor pending Grey rock pile with bright yellow-gold vein cracks reads as a gold-ore deposit, helped by small gold nugget pebbles scattered at the base. The gold veins are clear but the rock mass is mid-grey and slightly muddy/low-contrast, so the gold signal is carried mostly by thin lines rather than the mass itself. Subject is centred and the silhouette is clean, but it reads a touch generic versus the punchier gem/copper tiles. Push warmer ochre tones into the rock near the veins and add a couple more glinting gold highlights so the deposit reads as gold from the mass, not just the crack lines. —
—
 tile_mine_iron_ore
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 minor pending A grey faceted rock with a bright wavy metallic vein band and scattered pale flecks; the vein clearly signals 'ore' but the icon is nearly identical to tile_mine_silver in shape, palette and vein motif. On its own the craft is clean with good top-light, but the silver/iron pair is a genuine look-alike problem. Slightly cooler/neutral grey here versus silver's brighter tone is the only differentiator. Push iron toward a warmer brown-grey or add rusty/orange oxidation flecks so it is unmistakably distinct from the silver ore sibling. —
—
 tile_mine_silver
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 minor pending A grey faceted rock with a bright silvery wavy vein band and pale flecks; cleanly crafted with a strong specular vein, but it is almost indistinguishable from tile_mine_iron_ore aside from a marginally brighter, cooler sheen. The shared vein-on-grey-rock motif makes the two siblings collide. Good contrast and centring otherwise. Brighten and cool the silver further (whiter highlights, blue-grey cast) and/or change the vein/flake pattern so it clearly diverges from iron ore. —
—
 tile_mount_horse
Vector icon (still) player-facingmounts
 minor pending The body and legs are well-crafted and the warm brown is appealing, but the silhouette lacks any signature horse feature (no flowing mane, no long tail, ears are a small nub) so it reads as a generic brown quadruped rather than specifically a horse. Side-by-side it is nearly the donkey's body in brown, making the two too similar in shape. Outline and framing are clean. Add a clearer horse signal: a flowing mane along the neck and a longer flowing tail, and give it more distinct erect ears so it doesn't read as the donkey recolored. —
—
 tile_special_dirt
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftedProducts
 minor pending This is a pixel-art mound of tilled dirt with a green sprout on a grassy disc, well crafted in its own style but stylistically an outlier from the flat-vector food family around it. It reads as a planting plot / dirt tile rather than a 'crafted product', which is a category-fit issue more than a craft flaw (it is shared with cat_special_dirt). Framing and clarity are fine. None for craft; note the style/category mismatch — it is pixel-art among vector food icons, so it will look inconsistent if shown in the same grid. —
—
 tile_tree_birch
Vector icon (still) player-facingtrees
 minor pending The cluster of glossy green spheres with a slender cream trunk marked by short dark dashes does read as a birch, and the white-spotted bark is the right species cue. However the canopy is the same bubbly green-sphere shape as the oak, so the two read as near-duplicates from a distance; only the trunk color distinguishes them. Outline and lighting are clean and the white highlight blobs sit consistently top-left. Make the birch canopy lighter/airier or more vertical and the leaves smaller to differentiate it from the oak's heavier round crown. —
—
 tile_tree_palm
Vector icon (still) player-facingtrees
 minor pending The segmented tapering brown trunk topped with spiky green fronds and a cluster of orange coconuts does read as a palm, and the coconut cluster is a nice species cue. But the fronds are bunched only to the upper-right rather than fanning out symmetrically, leaving the silhouette lopsided and slightly cramped against the top of the box. Outline and palette are otherwise clean and it is distinct from siblings. Spread the fronds more symmetrically around the crown so the palm reads from any angle and balances the silhouette. —
—
 tile_tree_willow
Vector icon (still) player-facingtrees
 minor pending The drooping vertical green strands hanging from a rounded mound do gesture at a weeping willow, but the strands read as a stiff comb/fringe rather than soft cascading foliage, and the heavy domed cap gives it a mushroom-like silhouette. The dark trunk is quite muddy and low-contrast against the dark background. The two white highlight dots on the cap also read oddly like eyes, lending an unintended face. Vary the length and curve of the hanging strands and lighten/clean the trunk so it cascades like willow foliage instead of a rigid mushroom fringe. —
—
 tile_veg_beet
Vector icon (still) player-facingvegetables
 minor pending A round magenta bulb with concentric ring detailing, a glossy highlight, and three small green leaf sprigs on stems reads clearly as a beet/root vegetable. Clean confident outline and consistent top-left light. The concentric rings are a nice touch but it sits dangerously close to the turnip in both silhouette and palette. Push the color cooler/deeper red-purple and reshape the silhouette to a more pointed beet root to differentiate it from the turnip sibling. —
—
 tile_veg_pepper
Vector icon (still) player-facingvegetables
 minor pending A bulbous red lobed body with strong glossy highlights and a tall brown stem; the lobing suggests a bell pepper but the smooth round top plus prominent stem reads more like an apple or cherry-tomato than a pepper. Craft is clean but subject accuracy is weak. Color is also very close to the mushroom-red family hue. Emphasize the characteristic 3-4 vertical bell-pepper lobes and a shorter green stem with a recessed shoulder so it clearly reads as a pepper rather than an apple. —
—
 tile_veg_squash
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_veg_*
 minor pending Reads as a round ribbed pumpkin/squash with clear vertical lobes and a small top nub, centred on the pad. The ribbing and warm yellow-to-orange shading are clean, but the stem is very small/indistinct so it leans more 'pumpkin' than a specific squash, and the upper highlight is a touch flat. Still polished and game-ready overall. Add a slightly larger brown/green stem nub and one extra shadow value on the lower-right lobes to deepen the form and disambiguate it as squash. —
—
 token_hearth_stone
Vector icon (still) player-facingcurrencies
 minor pending Matching wooden-ring token with a grey disc and a white-capped stacked-stone cairn emblem — reads as the stone variant and sits well in the set. However there is a small stray green smudge on the lower-left of the cairn that looks like an artifact, and the grey cairn on a grey disc has slightly lower internal contrast than its siblings. Framing and outline are otherwise clean and centered. Remove the stray green smudge near the lower-left stones and slightly darken the disc or lighten the cairn for crisper separation. —
—
 treasure_coin_stack
Vector icon (animation) player-facingtreasure
 minor pending The stack is essentially frozen across all 14 frames; I can detect at most a faint highlight shimmer but no real bob, spin, or coin settle. The underlying art is clean and reads well, with crisp rim ellipses and a clear face coin. As-is it is a still-but-pretty icon that needs actual motion to justify being an animation. Add a subtle settle/bob or a traveling specular glint sweeping up the stack edges, or a top coin that drops in to loop. —
—
 treasure_crown
Vector icon (animation) player-facingtreasure
 minor pending Motion is minimal: the only frame-to-frame change is a small gem glint that toggles across the jeweled band, while the crown body stays static. The drawing is appealing with pearl finials, a faceted gem row and warm gold ramp. It is close to a frozen icon and would benefit from a clearer animated sparkle sweep across the gems. Sequence the gem glints into a left-to-right traveling sparkle and add a gentle highlight pulse on the gold to read as animated. —
—
 treasure_goblet
Vector icon (still) unusedtreasure
 minor pending Reads as a jeweled gold goblet filled with red liquid, with three blue diamond inlays on the bowl and a sparkle. The footed stem and cup shape are clean, but the blue gem inlays sit very close in hue/shape to the crown's jewels, slightly muddying family distinctiveness. Overall craft is solid and it is well-centred. Consider recoloring or reducing the rim gems so the goblet reads more as a vessel than a gem object, improving distinction from the crown. —
—
 treasure_goblet
Vector icon (animation) player-facingtreasure
 minor pending Effectively frozen across the strip; the goblet shape, gem band and red rim do not change beyond an almost-imperceptible highlight. The drawing is strong with a flared cup, knurled stem knop and a clear blue-gem band over gold. It reads as a static icon that should have some sparkle or liquid/glint motion. Add a traveling glint on the cup and a blinking sparkle on the gem band, or a subtle shimmer of liquid at the rim. —
—
 treasure_gold_key
Vector icon (animation) player-facingtreasure
 minor pending The key holds the same diagonal pose every frame with only a faint sparkle near the tip and a barely-there highlight shift, so it reads as effectively frozen. The art itself is nice: round-lobed bow, beveled shaft and a clear bit, with a soft drop shadow. It needs a clearer twinkle or a slow glint sweep to feel animated. Add a moving specular glint that travels along the shaft and a stronger blinking sparkle on the bow to give it life. —
—
 treasure_ring
Vector icon (animation) player-facingtreasure
 minor pending The ring is held in a fixed pose; I see a faint white sparkle near the ruby crown but no rotation, bob, or sustained glint, so it is near-frozen. The art is clean with a believable gold band loop, claw prongs and a bright ruby facet. It needs a more visible twinkle or a glint sweep around the band to justify the animation. Add a star-sparkle that blinks on the ruby plus a specular highlight that arcs around the gold band. —
—
 turns_remaining
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 minor pending A clear hourglass (wood frame, falling sand) sits on a glossy rounded badge, reading correctly as time/turns left. The hourglass itself is clean, but the hot-pink/magenta badge is an odd, off-brand colour for a time indicator and clashes tonally with the warmer/earthier family. The sand and frame are slightly muddy where they meet at the pinch. Recolour the badge to a neutral or cool slate/amber tone consistent with the UI family, and tidy the central pinch of the hourglass for crisper sand. —
—
 ui_devtools
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 minor pending Reads as tools/settings overall, but the crossed metal items are a bit muddy where they overlap and the gold ribbed block at lower-left is ambiguous (file? grip?). The dark metallic heads lose definition against the dark half. Increase separation/contrast between the two crossed tools and clarify or drop the gold block so the silhouette is cleaner at small size. —
—
 ui_people
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 minor pending Reads as 'people/group' via the layered figures, but the rear brown figure is cropped tightly and its half-head sits low-contrast, looking slightly like an artifact rather than a clean second person. The front figure is clear; the composition pushes right of center and the back figure clips the box edge a touch. Nudge the pair toward center and give the rear figure a fuller, higher-contrast silhouette so it clearly reads as a second person, not a smudge. —
—
 ui_puzzle
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 minor pending Instantly reads as a single jigsaw puzzle piece, on-style with the family's glossy gradient look. It sits noticeably small inside the 64px box with a lot of empty margin, and the dark navy outline is quite heavy relative to the small fill. Scale the piece up ~15-20% to better fill the design box and lighten the outline weight slightly. —
—
 ui_shop
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 minor pending The red-and-white striped awning over a brown counter reads clearly as a shop/market stall and fits the family style. The produce/goods on the counter are a bit muddy and indistinct, but the awning carries the read. Sharpen the counter goods (clearer fruit/jar silhouettes with more contrast) so the lower half isn't a muddy brown band. —
—
 ui_star
Vector icon (still) player-facinguiElements
 minor pending A warm gold five-point star with clean form that reads instantly. However the top point has a stray white triangular notch/highlight that looks like a rendering artifact rather than intentional shine. Remove or soften the white triangular notch at the top point so the star edge is clean. —
—
 water_pump
Vector icon (still) player-facingrecipes
 minor pending Reads clearly as a water pump: the grey arcing handle, gold side-spout, and small blue water droplet sell the function well, and it sits on a believable wooden plinth. Craft is solid — consistent red body shading, clean rivet dots, good light direction and shadow. Minor issue: the grey handle terminates abruptly in mid-air with no visible attachment to the body, reading slightly like a detached arc, and the body's left-edge highlight stripe is a touch heavy/blobby. Anchor the grey handle visibly into the pump body (a small pivot/joint where it meets the housing) and soften the bright vertical highlight on the red body so it reads as a curved sheen rather than a separate stripe. —
—
 weapon_axe
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweapons
 minor pending It is animated but only just: the head and haft do a small rigid pendulum tilt with almost no articulation, so it reads as the whole icon rocking rather than the axe doing anything. The drawing itself is solid (faceted steel head, clean haft). Motion is very subtle and a touch lifeless for a weapon. Add a touch more swing arc plus a brief edge-gleam sweeping across the blade so it reads as weight settling rather than a slight whole-icon tilt. —
—
 weapon_crossbow
Vector icon (still) unusedweapons
 minor pending Reads as a crossbow: chunky wooden stock/grip with a thin gray bow-limb arc and a bolt with a pale arrowhead flying right. The grey limb arc is low-contrast and nearly vanishes against the light background, so on a quick glance it can look more like a toy pistol than a crossbow. Decent wood gradient and clean stock outline; well-framed. Darken/thicken the bow-limb arc and string for stronger contrast so the crossbow silhouette reads at a glance, especially on the light background. —
—
 weapon_crossbow
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweapons
 minor pending There is some change — the string and white bolt shift slightly across frames as if loading — but it is faint and the heavy stock stays rigid, so it skirts being frozen. The wood stock is well shaded though the silhouette is a little busy/hard to parse at a glance. Loop is fine. Make the bolt-fire/reload more pronounced (string snap forward then ease back) so the animation clearly registers, and slightly simplify the stock for readability. —
—
 weapon_dagger
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweapons
 minor pending Animation is present but minimal: the blade sways a hair and a tiny white glint pops on the red pommel gem around the mid frames, otherwise it is near-frozen. Underlying art is clean and reads clearly at small size. Loops fine but the motion barely registers as a flipbook. Strengthen the pommel-gem twinkle into a brief star-sparkle and add a small blade gleam so the icon clearly animates rather than sitting still. —
—
 weapon_helmet
Vector icon (still) unusedweapons
 minor pending Reads clearly as a metal helmet: domed top, riveted cheek plates, a black T-shaped visor slit, and a red plume. Strong steel gradient and contrast on both halves. The red plume sits awkwardly off to the side and looks a bit like a detached blob rather than a crest sprouting from the helm. Anchor the plume to the helmet crown (a small base/mount) so it reads as an attached crest rather than a floating red shape. —
—
 weapon_spear
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweapons
 minor pending It moves only slightly — the spearhead bobs up a touch and the whole spear gives a faint sway, with little articulation, so it borders on frozen. The leaf-blade and ribbed grip are nicely drawn. Loop is safe but the motion is too timid to clearly read as animated. Add a gentle thrust/recoil bob along the shaft axis or a gleam travelling up the spearhead to make the motion legible. —
—
 weapon_sword
Vector icon (still) unusedweapons
 minor pending Reads as a sword: tapered steel blade with a metallic gradient, a wood/gold crossguard and a glowing gold pommel. Clean outline and consistent light. The issue is family-level: its blade is essentially the same asset as weapon_dagger, so the pair are near-duplicates rather than clearly different weapons. Keep the sword as the longer-bladed reference and push the dagger much shorter/stubbier so the two are unmistakably distinct. —
—
 weather_comet
Vector icon (still) unusedweather
 minor pending Reads clearly as a comet: a bright blue glassy sphere with a soft diagonal trail and small sparkles. The sphere sits right-of-center and the trail clips off the left magenta bound, so framing is loose; the soft glow is heavier than the rest of the family's crisp outlines. Shift the whole comet up-left so the sphere is more centered and the tail stays inside the box; tighten the glow to better match the family's hard-outlined style. —
—
 weather_lightning
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweather
 minor pending The bolt is dim/near-invisible most frames then flares bright yellow with a glow halo around frames 5 and 11, which correctly conveys a flashing strike, but the flash is mostly a global brightness pulse on a static bolt shape rather than the bolt itself crackling or changing path. Between flashes the icon is effectively frozen, so much of the strip reads as no-motion. The strike does loop and the glow is intentional rather than an artifact, so it is acceptable but underwhelming. Vary the bolt's zigzag path or add a brief secondary fork on each flash so it crackles instead of just brightening, and tighten the flash so the dead frames feel less frozen. —
—
 weather_rainbow
Vector icon (still) unusedweather
 minor pending The rainbow arc itself is crisp with correct ROYGBIV-style banding, but the two flanking clouds have an odd cross-hatched/scribbled grey texture that looks like overlapping line-art rather than the family's solid clouds. The composition is also quite wide and the clouds read as muddy at small size. Redraw the flanking clouds as solid, clean-outlined puffs matching the family style (no cross-hatch scribble); slightly narrow the overall arch for better centering. —
—
 weather_rainbow
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweather
 minor pending The rainbow arc and cloud bases are largely static; the only change is a faint pale shimmer/sheen that sweeps across the upper-left bands on a few frames, which is subtle enough to nearly read as frozen. The rainbow itself is drawn well with clean ROYGBIV banding and grounded cloud feet, but the shimmer is weak and slightly washes the bands lighter where it passes. Motion is present but barely perceptible. Strengthen and smooth the shimmer sweep into a clear traveling highlight along the arc, or fade the whole rainbow in/out, so the animation is actually noticeable; ensure the highlight doesn't desaturate the bands. —
—
 weather_sun_cloud
Vector icon (animation) player-facingweather
 minor pending Across all 14 frames the sun, rays, and cloud appear essentially identical with no detectable change, so the icon reads as frozen rather than animated. The underlying drawing is clean and appealing (warm gradient sun, crisp rays, soft cloud with good overlap depth), but a still pretty icon with no motion is exactly a 'should animate' case. No artifacts, but also no life. Add a slow pulse or gentle rotation to the sun rays, or a subtle drift to the cloud, so the icon actually animates instead of sitting static. —
—
 wedding_pie
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftedProducts
 minor pending Same lattice pie base as the 'pie' icon, dressed up with a white daisy flower, cream dollops along the rim and dual steam wisps to signal a celebration bake. The wedding cue reads, but the base is near-identical to pie so at thumbnail size the two can blur together. Cleanly drawn and well centred. Push the wedding differentiation further (e.g. tiered/white-iced crust or a clearer floral garland) so it is unmistakable from the plain pie at small size. —
—
 wheelbarrow
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsFarm
 minor pending Reads as a wheelbarrow: a red angled tray heaped with brown soil (dotted with seeds/stones), a single dark front wheel with a grey hub, a support leg, and a separate brown handle grip at upper right. The detached floating handle bar on the right reads oddly because it is disconnected from the tray, and the single visible wheel-plus-leg arrangement is slightly ambiguous. Otherwise outline and palette are on-family. Connect the right-side handle visually to the tray (or extend a frame rail to it) so it does not float as a separate bar, clarifying the wheelbarrow structure. —
—
 wtool_drill
Vector icon (animation) player-facingworkshopTools
 minor pending The drill body is effectively frozen across all 14 frames — no rotation, recoil, or bob — with only a tiny sparkle flickering at the bit tip. It is essentially a still icon with a twinkle, which undersells a power tool. The drawing itself is solid (orange housing, black grip, metal chuck). Add a spinning/blur effect on the bit and a small body recoil or shake so it reads as a running drill instead of a static prop. —
—
 wtool_hammer
Vector icon (animation) player-facingworkshopTools
 minor pending The icon tilts through a swing arc and frame 6 fires a clean 4-point sparkle on the head — visible motion, no blown-out flash, head is nicely drawn with a metal bevel. Motion is a rigid whole-icon rotation rather than an organic articulated swing, and start/end angles differ enough to risk a slight loop hitch. Note: it reads clearly as a hatchet/axe, not a hammer. Tighten the swing so first and last frames match for a clean loop; if the subject is meant to be a hammer, redraw the head as a claw/peen hammer instead of an axe blade. —
—
 wtool_nail
Vector icon (animation) player-facingworkshopTools
 minor pending The nail rocks and changes angle frame to frame, so it is animated, but it is a rigid whole-icon rotation rather than any organic motion and the angle swing is fairly mechanical. The metallic drawing is clean with a good highlight ramp on the head and shaft. Loop is roughly acceptable as it returns near the starting tilt. A nail has little natural motion — consider a subtle settle/glint or a tiny driven-in bob instead of the stiff pivot, or leave it as a near-still icon. —
—
 wtool_paintbrush
Vector icon (still) unusedworkshopTools
 minor pending Reads as a paintbrush: wooden handle, chrome ferrule, blue bristle tuft with a drip below. The wet-blue bristle sells it, but the layout (handle + ferrule + tip) is nearly identical to the chisel, so the two risk being confused at a glance. Craft and contrast are solid. Splay or flatten the bristle tip slightly (fan it out) to differentiate the brush silhouette from the chisel's solid tip. —
—
 wtool_paintbrush
Vector icon (animation) player-facingworkshopTools
 minor pending There is a subtle bob/sway of the whole brush frame to frame, but the bristles stay rigid rather than flexing, so it reads as a gentle whole-icon nudge instead of a painting stroke. Motion is present but understated. The drawing is clean — wood handle, metal ferrule, and ramped blue bristles. Flex the bristle tip (a small drag/whip on the brush stroke) rather than nudging the whole sprite, for organic brush motion. —
—
 wtool_pliers
Vector icon (still) unusedworkshopTools
 minor pending With the two red handles splayed wide and dark crossed blades up top it reads more like pruning shears or loppers than gripping pliers; the pivot and jaws are a bit muddy where the dark blades overlap. Craft is fine (clean outline, good red contrast), but the splayed-open pose hurts the 'pliers' read and overlaps conceptually with scissors. Close the jaws more and show short parallel gripping jaws (not long crossing blades), or accept it as shears and relabel; tighten the dark overlap at the pivot for clarity. —
—
 wtool_saw
Vector icon (animation) player-facingworkshopTools
 minor pending There is real frame-to-frame change — the blade travels left-to-right with a scattering wood-dust trail beneath it. But it reads as a rigid horizontal slide of the whole icon rather than a back-and-forth sawing stroke, and the last frame sits far right while the first sits far left, so the loop will hard-jump on reset. The blade and wood handle are well drawn. Make it an oscillating push-pull saw stroke (right then back) so the cycle returns to the start frame, eliminating the teleport on loop. —
—
 wtool_screwdriver
Vector icon (animation) player-facingworkshopTools
 minor pending The red handle and metal shaft barely move across the strip — at most a faint tilt, with no clear twisting/screwing rotation or bob. It reads as effectively frozen, which is flat for a tool that should turn. The drawing is clean with a nice glossy red handle and tapered tip. Add a back-and-forth twist of the handle (rotation around the shaft axis) so it reads as actively driving a screw. —
ore_gold_nugget
Vector icon (still) unusedores
 minor verified The clustered-bubble gold shape with sparkles reads as gold and is a distinct silhouette, with a warm brown outline and good metallic highlights. However the rounded bubbly lobes read a little like a flower or cluster of grapes rather than a hard mineral nugget, and there are faint diagonal pickaxe-ghost lines crossing it. Color and shine are strong. Tighten a couple of lobes into harder angular facets so it reads as a mineral nugget rather than a soft cluster, and clean up the faint diagonal cross lines. fixed unbalanced save/restore (was BROKEN: leaked transform drifted nugget off-cell after frame 1) — all 14 frames now render in-cell
—
 ach_champion
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — Crossed swords are an instantly readable champion/combat symbol with a clean dark-brown outline against the gold disc, good gloss highlight up top. The two-star ribbon nicely signals a mid/high tier. Well centred within the magenta box and consistent with the gold-rim siblings. — —
—
 ach_first_blood
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A single upright sword reads clearly as a first combat kill, with a nice bronze rim, warm glossy disc gradient and a single-star ribbon that correctly signals an entry-tier milestone. The sword's outline is clean and well centred. Distinct from Champion's crossed swords. — —
—
 ach_first_catch
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — The blue fish in profile with a forked tail reads instantly as 'catch/fishing', sitting cleanly on the bronze glossy disc with a one-star ribbon. Clearly a junior version of Master Angler (same fish, bronze vs gold, one vs three stars), which is good intentional tiering. Well centred and crisp. — —
—
 ach_forester
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A rounded green tree canopy with a brown trunk reads clearly as forestry, with a clean silver rim and blue ribbon. There is a faint ghosted second tree shape behind it that slightly muddies the canopy, but the primary subject is unmistakable and well centred. Good contrast of green on the silver disc. Remove or tone down the faint ghosted tree behind the main canopy so the silhouette stays crisp. —
—
 ach_fowler
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A plump golden bird in profile (round body, beak, eye, dark wing outline) reads clearly as a fowl/bird, distinct from the other animal badges. Clean dark outline against the silver disc, good centering and a tidy blue one-star ribbon. Craft is confident. — —
—
 ach_master_angler
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — The blue fish reads instantly as fishing mastery, and the three-star pedestal-style ribbon clearly signals the top tier above First Catch. Gold rim and glossy disc are clean and premium-looking, well centred in the box. Strong, coherent tier-cap design. — —
—
 ach_orchard_friend
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A glossy red round fruit with a small brown stem and one green leaf sits centered on a clean silver medallion; reads clearly as orchard fruit. Outline is confident, light direction consistent (top-left), framing well-centered within the box. The fruit reads more as a generic apple/plum than anything orchard-specific, but that is acceptable. — —
—
 ach_pollinator
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A bold magenta-pink flower with a yellow disc center reads instantly as a bloom/pollination subject. High contrast against the silver, clean black outline, well-centered and fills the box nicely. Strong, distinct icon within the family. — —
—
 ach_polymath
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — Three radiating green leaf clusters form a trefoil on a warm gold medallion with a red ribbon and 2-star plaque; the gold tier and emblem read as a higher achievement. Craft is clean with consistent shading, though the leaf trefoil is a somewhat abstract stand-in for 'polymath' (broad mastery). Framing and contrast are good. — —
—
 ach_tide_runner
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A clean blue fish with a white eye-highlight sits centered on the silver medallion with a 2-star plaque; reads instantly as fish/sea/tide. Confident outline, good contrast, well-framed. Solid, distinct icon. — —
—
 ach_town_planner
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A navy drafting compass (dividers) is centered on the silver medallion, a clear and apt symbol for a planner. Clean confident strokes, good contrast, well-framed within the box. Reads well and is distinct from siblings. — —
—
 ach_veg_patron
Vector icon (still) player-facingachievements
 good — A banded orange carrot with a green leafy crown sits centered on the silver medallion and reads instantly as a vegetable. Clean outline, good warm/cool contrast against the silver, well-framed. On-subject and distinct. — —
—
 amulet
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 good — Reads clearly as a magic amulet: a circular bronze medallion with tick-marks around the rim, two small bail/chain nubs at top, and a luminous red cabochon gem at its center. Outline is clean and confident, the gold-to-bronze gradient gives nice volume, and the radial glow on the gem sells the 'magic' quality. The two thin diagonal chain stubs at upper-right are a little ambiguous and could read as stray spikes at thumbnail size. Make the two upper chain stubs read more clearly as a hanging cord or loop so they don't look like stray spikes at small size. —
—
 anchor
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 good — Clean classic anchor silhouette with the ball ring, stock crossbar and curved flukes reading instantly at small size. The thin grey tip accents on the flukes are nearly lost against the dark body but don't hurt legibility. Solid dark outline and consistent flat shading fit the family. — —
—
 anvil
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 good — Clean reads instantly as an anvil: dark wedge-shaped body with a flat highlighted top face, tapered waist and stepped base. Confident black outline, simple grey top highlight gives just enough form, consistent with the family's flat-with-soft-shadow style. Well-centred within the box and not clipping. — —
—
 arcane_candle
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 good — Cream wax taper on a gold/brass holder with a clean orange-to-white flame and a soft warm halo reads instantly as a candle on both backgrounds. Outline is clean, light direction consistent, good contrast against the dark side. The flame's tiny black wick dot is a touch heavy but acceptable. — —
—
 arcane_crystal_ball
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 good — Deep purple sphere with a lighter spiral swirl, white sparkle dots, and a crisp rim highlight sits on a brown tripod stand and reads immediately as a crystal/scrying ball. Light direction (upper-left highlights) is consistent and the magical glow halo carries it on the dark half. Stand legs are a bit thin but framing and centring are solid. — —
—
 arcane_crystal_ball
Vector icon (animation) player-facingarcane
 good — The inner spiral/nebula visibly rotates and redistributes across frames, an organic swirl that perfectly suits a scrying orb rather than a rigid slide. Nicely drawn purple orb with rim light on a gold tripod stand. The swirl is continuous so the wrap from last to first frame is acceptable. — —
—
 arcane_potion_blue
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 good — Conical Erlenmeyer flask with a clean dark outline, glossy blue liquid, a curved surface meniscus and rising bubbles reads instantly as a potion/flask. Light direction and rim highlights are consistent and the blue glow halo ties it to the family. Well centred and clearly distinct from the round red potion. — —
—
 arcane_potion_blue
Vector icon (animation) player-facingarcane
 good — Fine white fizz/sparkle drifts and repositions inside the conical flask frame-to-frame, a believable bubbling motion fit for the subject. The flask is well drawn with a dark outline and bright blue fill, readable on both light and dark halves. Motion is subtle but present and loops without a hard jump. — —
—
 arcane_potion_red
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 good — Round flask with a wooden cork, glossy red liquid, surface highlight band and bubble dots reads clearly as a red potion and pairs well as a sibling to the blue flask while staying distinct in shape and colour. Outline and highlights are clean with a consistent light direction. The red glow halo is a bit heavy/diffuse but works on dark. — —
—
 arcane_potion_red
Vector icon (animation) player-facingarcane
 good — Bubbles/white speckles inside the flask shift and rise across frames and the pink accent disc breathes subtly, so it is clearly animated with motion that suits a liquid. The glass-bottle drawing is crisp with a good rim highlight and cork. First and last frames sit close enough to loop without a jump. — —
—
 arcane_spell_tome
Vector icon (still) unusedarcane
 good — A red-brown leather tome with gilded corner brackets, page edges down the side and a blue diamond gem clasp on the cover reads clearly as a spell book/grimoire. Outline is clean and the gem provides a nice focal point; it is distinct from every other icon in the family. Slightly dark overall and the gold corners are a touch noisy, but it holds on both backgrounds. — —
—
 astral_eclipse
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 good — Instantly reads as a solar eclipse: dark central disc, bright golden corona ring, irregular radiating light rays, and a small bright bead at upper-right like the diamond-ring effect. Light direction and contrast are consistent and it holds on both backgrounds. Confident and distinct from the sun sibling. — —
—
 astral_eclipse
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 good — The corona rays around the black eclipse disc clearly flicker and rotate, with ray length and brightness changing across frames so the corona shimmers convincingly. The dark central disc with a warm amber ring and radiating filaments is well drawn and on-color. Loop reads fine as the corona cycle returns near the start. — —
—
 astral_full_moon
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 good — Clean circular disc with a defined blue-grey rim and several darker crater dots, plus a small highlight arc, clearly reads as a full moon. Solid vector shading and a confident outline make it work on both halves. The soft outer halo is tasteful and does not overwhelm the form. — —
—
 astral_galaxy
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 good — Clear rotational motion: the ring of small blue-white star specks orbits around the warm glowing core across the frames, reading convincingly as a spinning galaxy. The cream core, purple halo and scattered dust are nicely rendered with a soft depth gradient. The orbit cycles so it loops smoothly without a jump. — —
—
 astral_north_star
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 good — The four diffraction spikes clearly pulse/extend and the central glow twinkles frame-to-frame, giving a convincing sparkle rather than a static icon. The crisp white core fading into a cool blue halo is clean and well crafted. Loop is smooth as the spike pulse returns to its starting length. — —
—
 astral_ringed_planet
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 good — Excellent: a warm banded sphere with a tilted ellipse ring passing front and back, plus a small bright moon, reads instantly as a ringed planet. Confident outline, consistent light direction (highlight upper-left), and clean banding give it the most polished craft in the set. Holds strongly on both backgrounds. — —
—
 astral_ringed_planet
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 good — There is visible motion: a bright specular glint travels around the ring (upper-left to lower-right across the frames) and the ring shimmer shifts, giving a sense of orbit/sparkle. The banded ochre planet with the elliptical ring is well drawn with good shading. Loop is acceptable as the glint cycles back toward its start position. — —
—
 astral_shooting_star
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 good — A large four-point star at upper-right with a fading diagonal trail of smaller sparkles clearly reads as a shooting star with motion. Warm golden palette and the tapering trail communicate direction and speed well. Works on both halves, though the trail naturally fades more on the light side. — —
—
 astral_sun
Vector icon (still) unusedcelestial
 good — A bright orange-yellow disc with a soft highlight and a ring of tapered radiating rays reads instantly as a sun. Clean confident outline, consistent upper-left light, and good contrast make it strong on both backgrounds. Clearly distinct from the eclipse (no dark disc) and well centered in the box. — —
—
 astral_sun
Vector icon (animation) player-facingcelestial
 good — Clear frame-to-frame motion: the radiating rays rotate/cycle and pulse in length while the core glow breathes, which reads as a lively spinning sun. The hue-shifted amber-to-orange core with a darker outline is well crafted and the rays animate organically rather than sliding the whole icon. Loop is smooth since the ray cycle returns near the start. — —
—
 audit_bell
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — Instantly reads as a bell: domed golden body with vertical gradient, dark rim/base, a wooden handle bar across the top and red ringing-arc marks on each side that convey sound. Clean confident outline, good contrast on both backgrounds, well centred and filling the box. The diagonal handle and the body sit together convincingly. — —
—
 banner_acorn
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — Forest-green pennant with a brass top rail, subtle gloss streak and drop shadow; the central brown acorn (rounded nut with a hatched cap) reads correctly as an acorn. Consistent with the banner family in shape and finish, and the emblem has enough contrast against the green to be legible. Well centred within the box. — —
—
 banner_hearth
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — Warm amber/gold pennant with brass rail; a stylised flame (red outline, yellow-white core) sits above a dark log/grate bar, reading clearly as a hearth fire. Strong contrast, clean outline and the family's signature gloss streak. Well centred and the flame silhouette is confident. — —
—
 banner_sun
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — Crimson pennant with brass rail and a bright yellow sun (solid disc plus eight straight rays) centred on the field. The sun is unmistakable, contrasts well against the dark red, and the rays are evenly spaced and crisp. Framing and family consistency are spot on. — —
—
 biome_farm
Vector icon (still) player-facingmineHazards
 good — Clean circular medallion with a dark-green ring, a gradient sky, a red barn, sun with rays, and a row of yellow sprout/crop shapes; reads instantly as 'farm' at small size. Outline is confident and contrast is strong on both backgrounds. The crop shapes are slightly ambiguous (read as spoons/sprouts) but acceptable. — —
—
 biome_fish
Vector icon (still) player-facingmineHazards
 good — Strong circular badge with layered blue water, white wave lines, a sailboat, a pale sun and a brown dock piling breaking the rim. Reads clearly as a harbor/sea biome and is well distinguished from the farm. Outline and contrast hold up on both light and dark halves. — —
—
 biome_mine
Vector icon (still) player-facingmineHazards
 good — Dark night-mountain medallion with a central round gold ore deposit framed by brown timber supports, plus a small pickaxe at lower left and a star sparkle. Reads as a mine/ore badge and pairs cohesively with farm and harbor. The pickaxe is small and muddy but the ore focal point carries the read. Slightly enlarge and brighten the pickaxe so it doesn't blur into the dark rock at small size. —
—
 bird_cage
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 good — Strong silhouette: red domed top with finial, vertical bars, dark round base, and a clearly readable little yellow bird on a perch inside. Light direction and contrast are clean and it is well centred in the box. — —
—
 bird_feed
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 good — A tapered wooden tub brimming with golden grain/seeds reads convincingly as bird feed. Outline is clean, the seed cluster has good warm shading, and one stray seed pops above the pile for life. — —
—
 bld_apiary
Vector icon (still) player-facingbuildings
 good — Reads cleanly as a domed straw skep hive with horizontal coil banding and two bees, mounted on a wooden trestle with three small flowers below; warm honey-gold palette is confident with good contrast on both backgrounds.
