—
 lantern
Vector icon (still) player-facingrecipes
 good — Dark-metal lantern body with a glazed window, a bright orange/white flame inside, a top arched handle and a warm radial glow reads instantly and beautifully. Strong focal contrast, clean outline, the glow halo sells the lit state on both backgrounds. Among the strongest icons in the set. — —
—
 lifebuoy
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 good — Classic ring buoy with alternating red and cream segments and a rope-bound brown hub centre reads instantly as a lifebuoy. The radial dash strokes suggest lashings and the colours are strong and high-contrast on both backgrounds. Clean circular outline, well centred. — —
—
 magic_mirror
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 good — Reads cleanly as a magic mirror: an upright oval framed in patterned gold with corner studs, a small ornamental crest at top, a short handle/foot at the bottom, and a blue glass face crossed by a bright diagonal glare streak that sells the reflective surface. Strong contrast and a premium ornate frame. Well centered with a clear silhouette. — —
—
 magic_wand
Vector icon (still) player-facing(aliases)
 good — Instantly reads as a magic wand: faceted blue gem with a clear cross-shaped glint, warm wooden handle, glow halo and two small sparkle accents. Diagonal composition fills the box well and is balanced on both backgrounds. Clean outline, good light direction, distinct from siblings. — —
—
 magnet
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsMine
 good — Textbook U-shaped red horseshoe magnet with grey N and S pole tips clearly labelled, instantly readable and unambiguous. Glossy highlight on the curve gives clean form and the dark-red ramp reads well on both backgrounds. Centred and unclipped; the labelled poles make the subject unmistakable. — —
—
 map_crossroads
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 good — A two-armed wooden signpost on a tan dirt circle reads instantly and unambiguously as a crossroads/waypoint. Clean dark-brown outlines, warm tan boards with engraved line detail, and a consistent light direction; the small framed sun icon at top-left is a minor stray element but doesn't hurt readability. Well centred and fills the box nicely with a believable ground oval and shadow. — —
—
 map_fairground
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 good — A classic striped big-top tent with a dark arched entrance, scalloped valance, gold star finial and scattered sparkles reads immediately as a fairground. Confident crimson/cream stripes, clean outline and good contrast on both backgrounds; the little blue pennant by the star is slightly muddy but minor. Well framed and centred within the design box. — —
—
 map_home
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 good — A timber-framed cottage with a red-brown shingled roof, central door, two windows and a smoking chimney reads instantly as home. Warm consistent palette, clean outlines, tidy window mullions and a believable smoke puff; light direction is coherent and it is well centred with a grounding shadow. Strong, on-style family member. — —
—
 map_orchard
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 good — A bushy multi-lobed green canopy with red apples and a brown trunk reads immediately as a fruit orchard tree. Nice layered foliage circles with subtle lighter highlight blobs, clean dark outlines and good apple contrast; one fallen apple on the ground is a charming touch. Well centred and fills the box; solidly on-style. — —
—
 map_pit
Vector icon (still) player-facingmapNodes
 good — Two crossed silver swords bound with rope over a dark cauldron-like pit with a red interior reads clearly as an arena/combat 'Pit'. Crisp blade highlights, a clean wrap detail and good contrast against both backgrounds; the dark-red pit gives it a fittingly ominous tone. Well framed and centred, distinct from the other nodes. — —
—
 meat
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftedProducts
 good — Glossy red-brown roasted meat lump with a tan bone sticking out top-left, grill-mark hatching and a wisp of steam, reading as a cooked meat portion. Warm orange highlight gives juicy volume and the outline is clean. It is clearly differentiated from cured_meat by colour and the rawer rounded shape. — —
—
 milk
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftedProducts
 good — Pale off-white bottle with a ribbed dark-red cap and a cream label carrying a blue liquid droplet, reading clearly as a bottle of milk. Cool grey outline and subtle vertical highlight give convincing glass. Centred and balanced with a grounding shadow. — —
—
 milk_churn
Vector icon (still) player-facingtoolsFarm
 good — A classic riveted metal churn shape with a domed lid, banded body, side handles and a vertical highlight reads clearly as a milk churn. Cool steel gradient and outline are clean and it is well centred. — —
—
 mine_cart
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 good — A brown wood cart with metal bands and rivets, two dark wheels and a heaped mound of grey ore lumps sitting on a short grey rail reads clearly as a mine cart. Good depth from the band shading and the ore pile, confident outline. Well-centred and fills the box; one wheel sits near the lower bound but stays inside. — —
—
 nature_acorn
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 good — Instantly reads as an acorn: glossy gradient nut, cross-hatched cap with a clear stem, and a strong dark outline that holds up on both backgrounds. Light direction is consistent (highlight upper-left), framing is centered and fills the box well. The soft inner highlight on the nut is slightly blobby but acceptable. — —
—
 nature_cattail
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 good — Clearly a cattail: the cylindrical brown ribbed spike with a thin spadix tip sits among green sword-blades, which is botanically correct. Outline and shading are clean and the silhouette is distinct from everything else in the family. Slightly busy/thin at icon size but the central spike still anchors it. — —
—
 nature_cattail
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 good — The reeds bend and the brown seed-head bobs and leans across frames in a coordinated wind sway, which is the strongest organic motion in the batch. Drawing is clean with tapered green blades, a banded velvety spike and a contact shadow. Loops well with no teleport. — —
—
 nature_clover
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 good — Reads immediately as a four-leaf clover: four heart-lobed leaves with radial shine lines and a green stem, distinct from the single leaf icon. Strong dark outline, consistent glossy highlights, well centered. The radial sparkle streaks are a touch heavy but reinforce the lucky-charm read. — —
—
 nature_clover
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 good — Each lobe shifts subtly and the glossy highlights travel across the four leaves, reading as a soft breathe/bob rather than a static icon. The art is glossy and well-shaded with crisp lobe veins and a stem. Motion is on the gentle side but clearly present and loops cleanly. — —
—
 nature_dewdrop
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 good — Unmistakably a dewdrop: classic teardrop silhouette, blue radial gradient, bright specular highlight and a small secondary bubble plus a faint smile-reflection. Outline is clean and it sits centered in the box. The little curved reflection at the bottom is slightly face-like but doesn't break the read. — —
—
 nature_dewdrop
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 good — The internal highlight/sheen drifts and the drop subtly squashes while a small secondary drip forms near the base in later frames, which suits a water motif. Glassy blue rendering with a bright catch-light and a faint ripple shadow is appealing. Loops acceptably with no blow-out flash. — —
—
 nature_feather
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 good — The feather sways side to side around its quill and the diagonal barb striations plus the cool highlight shift frame to frame, giving real plume motion. The rendering is attractive with layered blues, fine barb lines and a soft contact shadow. Loop is smooth with no popping. — —
—
 nature_leaf
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 good — The leaf tilts and rotates on its stem across frames (facing-left to more edge-on) with the specular highlight sliding across the blade, so it reads as a genuine breeze sway rather than a whole-icon slide. Drawing is clean with a clear midrib, soft shading and a grounded shadow. First/last frames are close enough to loop without a hard jump. — —
—
 nature_mushroom_cluster
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 good — Clearly a mushroom cluster: two domed tan caps with cream spots, pale gilled stems, and a green grassy base with dapple highlights. Outline and shading are confident, light is consistent, and it's well centered. The two caps slightly merge where they overlap but the cluster read is strong. — —
—
 nature_pebbles
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 good — Reads cleanly as stacked pebbles/a cairn: three smooth grey stones graduated large-to-small with crisp specular highlights and soft outlines. Light direction is consistent and the silhouette is distinct. Centering is good though the stack sits slightly low, leaving a little headroom at top. — —
—
 nature_seashell
Vector icon (still) unusednature
 good — Strong read as a scallop shell: fan silhouette with a scalloped wavy top edge, radiating ribs converging to a hinge knob at the bottom, warm peach-to-cream gradient and brown outline. Light is consistent and framing fills the box well. The ribs are a touch thin/faint in the middle but identity is clear. — —
—
 nature_seashell
Vector icon (animation) player-facingnature
 good — The shell rocks/tilts gently around its hinge with the highlight sliding across the ribbed fan, giving a believable rocking-on-sand motion. Rendering is warm and clean with a brown outline, ribbed gradient fan and a hinge knob. Loop is smooth with no flicker or drift out of cell. — —
—
 net
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsSea
 good — The round mesh hoop with a long wooden handle and a small blue fish inside clearly reads as a catching net. The mesh weave is legible and the fish adds instant context for its purpose. Well outlined and on-style, angled nicely within the box. — —
—
 notif_fail
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — A dark-rimmed red glass orb with a bold white X reads instantly as failure/error. Clean light direction (top-left highlight, soft drop shadow), confident outline, strong contrast on both backgrounds. Well centred and fills the box without clipping. — —
—
 notif_success
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — A green glass orb with a bold white checkmark reads immediately as success and is a perfect colour/symbol sibling to notif_fail. Matching gloss highlight and drop shadow keep the pair consistent; contrast holds on light and dark. Centred and well framed. — —
—
 old_capital_seal
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — A deep-red faceted (decagon) gem with an embossed dark crown reads as a regal seal/emblem. The faceted polygon distinguishes it from the round region orbs, and the crown silhouette is legible. Gloss highlight and contrast are solid; centred and within bounds. — —
—
 ore_coal
Vector icon (still) unusedores
 good — A clean dark charcoal faceted nugget with subtle blue-grey highlight planes and three white sparkles, reading clearly as coal at small size. Confident black outline, consistent top-left light, good contrast on both backgrounds. The faint internal facet lines on the right are a touch busy but acceptable. — —
—
 ore_coal
Vector icon (animation) player-facingores
 good — A dark gem-cut coal polyhedron with crack lines; an orange inner ember flares brightly at two beats (frames ~1 and ~9, max brightness ~222-227 vs ~205 baseline) then dims, reading as a believable glowing-ember twinkle. Motion is a localized glow pulse rather than a rigid slide, which suits a hot-coal subject. First and last frames sit at the same dim resting state so it loops cleanly. — —
—
 ore_copper
Vector icon (animation) player-facingores
 good — A faceted grey nodule laced with orange copper streaks and small teal oxidation flecks; the highlight and the patina specks twinkle/migrate slightly frame to frame for a gentle living shimmer. The orange-vs-teal complementary palette reads clearly as copper and is appealing. Loop is seamless and there are no flashes or drift. — —
—
 ore_crystal_vein
Vector icon (still) unusedores
 good — A cyan/teal faceted crystal nested in a grey rock socket with clean highlights and two sparkles, reading clearly as a crystal vein and visually distinct from every sibling. Good light direction and contrast on both backgrounds. Slightly dark rock surround on the dark BG but the crystal still pops. — —
—
 ore_crystal_vein
Vector icon (animation) player-facingores
 good — A rounded grey ore with a cracked-open core exposing a bright cyan/teal crystal that pulses; mean brightness clearly oscillates (~107->118->100->115) so the vein convincingly brightens and dims like glowing geode crystal. The glow pulse is the right kind of motion for the subject and stays inside its cell. Endpoints are close enough to loop and there is no harsh white-out flash. — —
—
 ore_iron
Vector icon (animation) player-facingores
 good — A spherical faceted ore with grey body and warm rust-orange ore bands plus a couple of glowing flecks; a soft specular highlight and the ember flecks shift gently across the frames giving a low-key shimmer. Motion is subtle but present and appropriate for a solid metal rock. Loops without a visible jump; drawing is clean and well-shaded. — —
—

 good — An angular faceted shard in deep purple-black with a glassy lilac highlight streak and two sparkles, reading convincingly as obsidian (volcanic glass) and clearly distinct from the rock-balls. —
 ore_obsidian

 good — An angular near-black obsidian shard with cool violet facets and a bright lavender highlight running down its edge; the rim highlight and a small interior sparkle glide subtly across the frames for a glassy sheen. —

Vector icon (still) player-facingplayerTools

 side_beat
Vector icon (still) unusedfixed-icons
 good — A wooden arrow signpost on a post with grass tufts and a gold sparkle above reads well as a side quest / point of interest. Clean outline, believable wood gradient and a clear light direction; the sparkle adds the 'special' cue without clutter. Well composed and distinct from other icons. — —
—
 sifting_screen
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsMine
 good — Reads clearly as a circular sifting screen: warm wood-brown rim, a fine grey cross-hatch mesh, and three to four glossy grey/gold pebbles sitting in it. The two small support legs underneath are a touch thin and ambiguous, but the silhouette and mesh texture sell the concept instantly. Outline, soft shadow and gloss highlights are consistent with siblings (helmet, mine_cart). — —
—
 skull_candle
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 good — A pale skull with orange glowing eye sockets and a triangular nose, jaw teeth, and a small lit candle with flame rising from the crown — reads clearly as a skull candle / necromancy item. Confident outline, warm interior glow ties the eyes and candle together, good contrast on both grounds. Centred and distinct from the family. — —
—
 soup
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftedProducts
 good — Glossy brown bowl holding a yellow soup surface dotted with red/green/white vegetable bits, a wooden ladle leaning out and rising steam, reading clearly as soup. Outline and gradients are clean and the bowl has good volume. Centred with a grounding shadow. — —
—
 spell_arcane_missile
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 good — The glowing purple orb pulses and its ringed/elliptical energy shape rotates while a soft motion-trail streak and white sparkle twinkle in the corner, reading as a charged arcane projectile. Motion is organic and on-theme; brightness stays controlled with no blown-out flash, and it loops cleanly. Attractive glossy purple shading with good highlight. — —
—
 spell_fireball
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 good — Bright orange-to-yellow radial orb with flame tendrils sweeping off the left/top and small ember dots flicking off the right, all sitting in a warm glow. Reads instantly as fire, good contrast on both backgrounds, confident shapes. There is a faint darker curl on the orb's face but it does not hurt readability. — —
—
 spell_fireball
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 good — The glowing orange-yellow orb stays centered while the inner flame licks and the dark swirl pattern rotates frame-to-frame, with subtle scale/flare pulsing that reads as living fire. Motion is organic and well-suited to a fireball; the loop is clean since first and last frames sit at similar brightness. The radial accent glow behind it adds depth without blowing out to white. — —
—
 spell_heal
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 good — Rounded green plus with a darker outline, soft inner highlight and a few four-point sparkles in a green glow. Instantly reads as heal/restore, crisp and high-contrast on both halves, well centred. Cleanest and most legible icon in the set. — —
—
 spell_ice_shard
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 good — Three faceted light-blue crystals (a tall central spike flanked by two shorter ones) with dark blue outlines and bright facet highlights, plus frosty sparkles. Reads clearly as ice/crystal, good faceted shading and strong silhouette. Well framed and distinct from the other blue icons. — —
—
 spell_ice_shard
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 good — The three faceted crystals hold their shape and position while bright twinkle sparkles pop on and off across the facets, giving a believable icy shimmer. The shards themselves barely move, but the sparkle cycle is the intended motion and it loops without a hard jump. Drawing is crisp with clean blue rim-light and white highlights. — —
—
 spell_lightning
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 good — Bold zig-zag bolt in white-cored blue with small node dots at the tips and a soft blue glow behind it. Reads instantly as lightning, strong contrast especially on the dark half. The rounded ball-tipped strokes make it feel slightly diagrammatic but the bolt silhouette is confident and clear. — —
—
 spell_poison
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 good — The teardrop holds its silhouette while lighter green bubble specks shift and rise inside it, and a small drip detaches below in some frames, reading as an active toxic ooze. Motion is subtle but organic and fits poison; it loops acceptably. Clean glossy drop with a bright highlight and good rim definition. — —
—
 spell_shield_bubble
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 good — Translucent blue sphere with a hex-cell honeycomb pattern, a bright rim glow and a curved top highlight reading as a force-field dome. Reads clearly as a shield/barrier and the hex pattern differentiates it from the plain orbs. Contrast holds on both backgrounds; well centred. — —
—
 spell_shield_bubble
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 good — The bubble holds its round shape while a bright specular highlight sweeps around the rim and the hex-grid sheen shifts, giving a convincing energy-barrier shimmer. The sweeping reflection is organic and well-matched to a shield, and the loop is smooth with no popping. Nice depth from the dark-to-light gradient and the faceted hex interior. — —
—
 spell_wind
Vector icon (still) unusedspells
 good — Pale-blue curling gust lines with a spiral curl on the right and three small green leaves being carried along, conveying motion clearly. Reads as wind/gust and the leaves add a nice farming-flavoured detail; distinct from the rest of the set. The very pale gust strokes have low contrast on the light half but stay legible thanks to the leaves and curl. — —
—
 spell_wind
Vector icon (animation) player-facingspells
 good — This is the most clearly animated of the set: a green leaf travels and rotates left-to-right across the cell while pale-blue swirl/streak gusts curl and shift behind it, genuinely conveying blowing wind. Motion is dynamic and well-suited; the one risk is the leaf traveling far enough that the loop has a noticeable reset jump between last and first frame. The leaf and wisp drawings are simple but readable. Tighten the loop so the leaf's exit and entry positions line up (or have it circle back) to avoid a hard teleport between the final and first frame. —
—
 station_bakery
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftingStations
 good — Strong read: a warm copper-to-brown domed oven with concentric arch banding, a softly glowing golden loaf in the mouth, and a small chimney with rising stone smoke puffs. Outline is clean and dark, light direction is consistent top-left, and the warm palette clearly says 'bakery oven'. The smoke puffs sit high and slightly disconnected from the dome but still read as rising heat. — —
—
 station_decor
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftingStations
 good — A round purple ceramic vase with a glossy highlight and three distinct flowers (pink, white daisy, purple) on green stems reads cleanly as 'decor'. Outline is confident, the vase has a believable rim and bulge, and the flower trio adds color variety that distinguishes it from the brown-wood siblings. Crafting-station- as-decor is a slight conceptual stretch but the icon itself is clear and attractive. — —
—
 station_forge
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftingStations
 good — A dark trapezoidal hood over a glowing red coal bed with three small flame licks, mounted on a brown stone base with a grey anvil-like inset, plus a chimney puff on top. The glowing molten coals and flames give it a clear 'forge/heat' read and it is well distinguished from the bakery's warm dome by its cooler grey-and-dark palette. Framing is well-centred and contrast is strong on both backgrounds. — —
—
 station_larder
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftingStations
 good — A two-door wooden cabinet with latticed glass-pane doors showing pink/red preserves and green produce inside, round knobs, a bottom drawer, and a red tomato garnish on top — reads convincingly as a larder/pantry. Clean dark outline, consistent wood gradient, and the colorful jars inside sell the 'food storage' idea. The small items behind the panes get a little busy at icon size but still parse. — —
—
 station_workshop
Vector icon (still) player-facingcraftingStations
 good — A brown workbench with a dark pegboard behind it holding a hand saw and a grey clamp/vise, with two round dowel/peg accents and a wood mallet handle on the bench. It reads clearly as a 'workshop' and the tool-on-pegboard motif distinguishes it well from the other cabinet/oven shapes. Outline is clean and the bench legs ground it; the saw blade teeth are a touch noisy but the silhouette is legible. — —
—
 stone_hammer
Vector icon (still) player-facingmissingItems
 good — Reads as a crude stone hammer: chunky grey faceted head with incised crack/score marks and edge highlights on a ribbed brown wooden handle, set at a dynamic angle. Outline and shading match the tool family. Tilted framing fills the box well without clipping the head. — —
—
 stonework
Vector icon (still) player-facingrecipes
 good — A masonry stack of beveled rectangular stone blocks in staggered courses reads clearly as stonework, with a tiny chisel/tool icon adding a 'crafted' cue. Cool grey-tan shading and dark mortar gaps give depth. Confident outline; distinct from the rounded cobblepath sibling. — —
—
 tarot_card
Vector icon (still) unusedtoolsPortal
 good — A tilted dark tarot card with a gold border, radiant sun-with-eye motif, scattered star dots, and the legible legend 'XIX / THE SUN' — reads unmistakably as a tarot card. Strong gold-on-navy contrast, clean border, and the rakish tilt gives it life. Centred within the box and clearly distinct from siblings. — —
—
 tile_bird_chicken
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Reads instantly as a chicken: rounded cream body, brown outline, a small red comb and red wattle, single dark eye, and two orange legs. Clean confident outline, consistent warm gradient body, good contrast on both backgrounds. The orange legs splay slightly but framing is well-centred within the box. — —
—
 tile_bird_chicken/idle-autumn
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_bird_chicken
 good — Clean rounded white chicken with red comb and orange beak/feet on an autumnal green-brown pad; the idle reads as a gentle peck-and-blink loop (eyes close, pink blush appears, body settles) that is organic and not a rigid slide. Pixel craft is solid with soft cream shading and consistent centring on the pad across all 9 frames. Loop appears seamless from first to last frame. — —
—
 tile_bird_chicken/idle-spring
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_bird_chicken
 good — Identity matches the other seasons; the spring pad is a bright green grass disc dotted with small white/yellow flowers, clearly seasonal. Idle is a believable head-bob/peck with closing eyes and blush, body squashes subtly, well centred frame-to-frame. No flicker, popping or pad jump. — —
—
 tile_bird_chicken/idle-summer
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_bird_chicken
 good — Consistent chicken on a fuller, darker summer-green pad; the peck/bob loop with eye-close and blush is the same well-judged organic motion as the other seasons. Shading is clean, edges are crisp, and the subject stays centred on the pad through all frames. Seamless and readable at tile scale. — —
—
 tile_bird_chicken/idle-winter
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_bird_chicken
 good — Strong seasonal read: the pad becomes a soft blue-white snow/ice disc and a small grey breath puff rises above the head mid-loop, a nice cold-weather touch. The hen identity is preserved and the peck/blink idle stays organic and centred. No white-out, flicker, or popping. — —
—
 tile_bird_chicken/trans-autumn-winter
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_bird_chicken
 good — Across 17 frames the pad cleanly transitions from the brown autumn mound with leaf flecks to a pale snow/ice disc, while the chicken keeps its shape, comb and feet throughout. Progression is smooth and well staged with no white-out, bloom, or brightness overshoot mid-transition, and the subject stays centred. A small breath puff appears near the cold end, reinforcing the seasonal shift. — —
—
 tile_bird_chicken/trans-spring-summer
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_bird_chicken
 good — The pad progresses from a light flowered spring disc to a fuller, deeper summer-green disc while the hen identity, pose and centring hold steady across all frames. Motion is smooth with no white-out, flicker, or pad jump, and the seasonal read (flowers fading into denser growth) is clear. Subtle and game-ready. — —
—
 tile_bird_dodo
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Distinctly a dodo: big rounded body, oversized round head, a chunky downturned hooked orange beak, and a small pale tuft tail at the back. Brown radial gradient body with a darker wing patch, clean thick outline, two orange feet. Distinct from the chicken/goose siblings and well framed. — —
—
 tile_bird_dodo
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Instantly reads as a dodo with its characteristic heavy hooked beak, round body and stubby tail plume. Shading is deliberate with warm grey-brown body, lighter belly and clean orange legs; silhouette is crisp against the dark half with no halo. Sits centred on the grass pad. — —
—
 tile_bird_goose
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Clearly a goose/duck: long upward-curving cream neck, small head with orange beak, broad oval body with a darker wing, orange webbed feet. Clean outline and readable silhouette that distinguishes it from the rounder chicken. Slightly muted tan wing fill but contrast holds on both backgrounds. — —
—
 tile_bird_goose
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Reads immediately as a white goose; the orange bill, eye and webbed feet give clear contrast and the body uses soft cool-grey shadows to model the feathers without muddiness. Silhouette is clean with no stray pixels or anti-alias halo on the dark side. Centred on the pad with a nice cast-shadow grounding it. — —
—
 tile_bird_hen
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Clearly a hen: small red comb, brown body with speckled feathering, and yellow legs. The feather shading has good warm-brown ramping and the dark outline keeps the silhouette readable against both cell halves. Well centred on the grass pad, no fringing. — —
—
 tile_bird_melon
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingFarm
 good — Clearly a watermelon: round green rind with darker vertical stripes, a brown stem cap, a leaf and a curling tendril, plus a glossy highlight on the upper-left that gives it volume. Strong dark-green outline and consistent light direction; well centred and reads at small size. Distinct from the grass clumps despite the shared green. — —
—
 tile_bird_parrot
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Unmistakably a parrot: red head and body, white eye patch, black hooked beak, a rainbow-striped wing (red/orange/yellow/green) and a blue tail, perched on a brown bar. Vivid, high-contrast and clearly the most colourful of the family. Framing sits on the perch nicely and is well centred. — —
—
 tile_bird_parrot
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Strong read as a scarlet macaw: red body with a layered red/green/blue wing, hooked beak and long tail. The wing colour-banding is deliberate and vivid, the silhouette is crisp and it sits centred on the pad. Colours are saturated but cohesive enough with the cosy palette. — —
—
 tile_bird_pheasant
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Strong pheasant read: dark green head with a red eye-mask wattle, a white neck ring, rich chestnut body with barred flank striping, and a long swept brown tail extending right. The long tail and ring clearly separate it from the other rounder birds. Clean outlines and good contrast on both halves. — —
—
 tile_bird_pheasant
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Reads clearly as a pheasant with the green head, red face wattle, white neck ring and long barred copper tail. Shading on the body has nice warm coppery ramps and the long tail keeps a clean edge. Well centred and grounded on the pad with no fringing. — —
—
 tile_bird_phoenix
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Reads as a mythical firebird: golden-yellow body with orange-tipped flame plumage and dramatically raised wings. The warm yellow-to-orange ramp gives a glowing feel without a blown-out halo, and the silhouette stays crisp against the dark cell. Centred on the pad and visually distinct from the realistic birds in a good way. — —
—
 tile_bird_pig_in_disguise
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Clearly the gag it intends: a pink pig face (snout, ears) peeking out with a tan feathered/spotted costume draped over a pink body, plus a curly pig tail. The joke reads at a glance and it is visually distinct from every real bird in the set. Outlines are clean and the pink-on-tan contrast holds on both backgrounds. — —
—
 tile_bird_pig_in_disguise
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — The gag reads correctly: a pink pig face peeking out of a green duck-head hood atop a brown duck body, which is exactly the intended 'pig in disguise' joke. Clusters are clean, the pink face contrasts well against the green hood, and it sits centred on the pad. Silhouette is tidy with no stray pixels. — —
—
 tile_bird_rooster
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Reads immediately as a rooster: bold red comb and wattle, white eye, an arched dark-brown sickle tail, a deep red-brown body and two upright orange legs. The tail and tall comb clearly distinguish it from the hen/chicken. Clean confident outline and strong contrast on both halves. — —
—
 tile_bird_rooster
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Strong rooster read: large red comb and wattle, golden-orange body hackles and a glossy dark green-black sickle tail. The body shading has rich warm ramps and the tail keeps a clean curved silhouette against the dark side. Centred and grounded on the grass pad. — —
—
 tile_bird_turkey
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingFarm
 good — Reads as a turkey: round brown body, white-and-black eye, red wattle/snood by the beak, fanned brown tail feathers with tan accents, and two yellow legs/feet. Outline is clean and the symmetric tail fan is appealing; light direction is consistent. The eye sits a bit high and slightly off-axis but it does not hurt the read. — —
—
 tile_bird_turkey
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Reads clearly as a turkey: brown fanned tail with lighter feather tips, dark body and red head/wattle. The fanned tail has deliberate feather banding and the silhouette stays clean across both cell halves. Sits centred on the pad with a grounding shadow. — —
—
 tile_bird_wild_goose
Vector icon (still) player-facingbirds
 good — Reads as a wild goose (Canada-goose style): dark brown-black head and neck with a white cheek patch, olive-brown oval body with a small pointed tail, dark webbed feet. The dark head plus white cheek differentiates it well from the pale domestic goose sibling. Clean outline, though the body's olive fill is a touch flat/low-contrast versus the lighter goose. — —
—
 tile_bird_wild_goose
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_bird_*
 good — Clearly a wild (Canada) goose: black head and neck with a white cheek strap, grey-brown body and dark legs, distinct from the white domestic goose tile. Shading is muted and naturalistic with a clean silhouette and no halo on the dark cell. Centred on the pad with a soft cast shadow. — —
—
 tile_cattle_cow
Vector icon (still) player-facingcattle
 good — Reads instantly as a cow: white body with confident black Holstein spots, a pink-snouted head with a horn nub, a pink udder, and four blocky legs. Clean heavy black outline, consistent flat shading, good contrast on both light and dark backgrounds, well centred within the design box. The white body sits a touch high so the legs land near the lower bound, but nothing clips. — —
—
 tile_cattle_cow
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_cattle_*
 good — Instantly reads as a Holstein dairy cow: clean black-and-white patching, pink muzzle, a small bell, and tidy legs with hooves. Silhouette is crisp against the dark side with no halo or stray pixels, and shading on the body gives it gentle volume. It sits centred on the round grass pad with little yellow flower dots that match the cosy farm look. — —
—
 tile_cattle_longhorn
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_cattle_*
 good — Clearly a longhorn: warm tan/ochre body with a slightly lighter belly, a darker dorsal stripe, and the signature wide pale curving horns that read at a glance. Edges are clean, the silhouette is crisp on both light and dark halves, and the four legs are well separated with defined hooves. Centred nicely on the matching grass pad, consistent in palette and rendering with the cow. — —
—
 tile_cattle_triceratops
Vector icon (still) player-facingcattle
 good — Clearly a triceratops: green body with a domed neck frill bearing two bony bumps, three head horns, a beaked face and a single round eye, plus blocky legs consistent with the family. Heavy clean outline, good gradient shading and strong contrast on both backgrounds; well centred and fills the box without clipping. Distinct in silhouette and colour from its cow/longhorn siblings. — —
—
 tile_cattle_triceratops
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_cattle_*
 good — Unmistakably a triceratops: green body, bony neck frill, and the three-horn brow are all legible at board size, with a believable cosy-creature dino read rather than a generic lizard. Body shading uses a darker green ramp for volume and the silhouette stays crisp against the dark side with no fringing or strays. One minor concern is that the green body sits close in hue to the green grass pad, so the lower legs have slightly lower contrast against the ground than the other two. Optionally nudge the lower-leg/belly shadow a touch darker (or warm the pad green slightly) so the green dino separates a bit more from the green pad. —
—
 tile_coin_golden
Vector icon (still) player-facingcurrencies
 good — A circular gold coin with a milled/notched outer rim, an inset ring and an embossed four-point compass star, with a believable top-left specular highlight — instantly reads as a gold coin. Strong contrast and clean concentric construction hold on both backgrounds. Well centered and fills the design box nicely. — —
—
 tile_coin_golden
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_coin_*
 good — The front coin reads instantly as gold currency: warm-gold face, a darker amber rim band giving real metallic thickness, and a legible wheat-sheaf motif that ties it to the farm theme. A second smaller coin peeks behind it for a tidy stacked-coin silhouette, and it sits centred on a clean green oval pad. Pixel clusters are clean with no visible transparency fringing, stray pixels, or dark-side halo, and shading uses a deliberate gold-to-amber ramp rather than muddy noise. — —
—
 tile_fish_clam
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 good — A brown-rimmed tan shell with a fan of ridge lines radiating from the hinge and a pale inner lip, sitting as a wide ellipse. Reads clearly as a bivalve shell and is distinct from the oyster sibling. Centered, fills the box well, clean outline. — —
—
 tile_fish_clam
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fish_*
 good — Clean fan-ribbed bivalve silhouette in warm sandy tans with soft top-light highlight and darker hinge shading, reading instantly as a closed clam. The grass pad is centred, oval, with tidy speckle detail and no transparency fringing or stray pixels against the dark side. Shading is a touch soft/airbrushed rather than crisply ramped, but it is polished and game-ready. — —
—
 tile_fish_cocoa
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 good — A vertical brown pod with lengthwise ribs and a lighter highlight stripe, clean dark outline and good rounded form. Reads as a cocoa pod and is correctly distinct from the cocoa-bean cluster. Slightly tilted but acceptably centered. — —
—
 tile_fish_ink
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 good — A round purple-domed octopus head with two big white eyes and a fan of dangling tapered tentacles in darker indigo; charming and instantly readable as an octopus (ink source). Clean outline and a distinctive silhouette within the family. Well centered, tentacles stay inside the box. — —
—
 tile_fish_mackerel
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fish_*
 good — Strong readable fish with the characteristic dark wavy dorsal stripes, silver-blue body, distinct eye and forked tail, sitting curved on a centred grassy oval. —
 tile_fish_oyster
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 good — An angular tan two-half shell cracked open with a small bluish pearl on the cream inner flesh; reads clearly as an oyster and the pearl sells it. Distinct from the rounded ridged clam by its faceted shell shape. Centered, fills the box, clean dark outline. — —
—
 tile_fish_oyster
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fish_*
 good — Clearly reads as an open oyster: rough grey fluted outer shell, pale smooth inner nacre, and a centred highlighted pearl with a convincing specular dot. The blue water rim at the base distinguishes its pad from the plain grass tiles and is centred and tidy. Edges are clean with no fringing; shading on the shell is slightly soft but the form is convincing. — —
—
 tile_flower_pansy
Vector icon (still) player-facingflowers
 good — Instantly reads as a pansy: five rounded petals in deep violet with a characteristic warm golden/orange face at the center and a tiny white eye, on a green stem with leaves. Well-centered within the magenta box, fills the frame nicely without clipping. — —
—
 tile_flower_pansy
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_flower_*
 good — Reads instantly as pansies: two purple-and-cream blooms beside a golden-yellow bloom with the characteristic dark face-blotch, all clustered on a rounded green pad with small leaf accents. Subject sits centred on its ground pad and the colours fit a cosy farm palette well. — —
—
 tile_flower_water_lily
Vector icon (still) player-facingflowers
 good — Reads immediately as a water lily: layered pink concentric petals with a yellow stamen center, sitting on a notched dark-green lily pad with radial vein lines and a subtle water-ripple highlight behind it. —
 tile_flower_water_lily
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_flower_*
 good — Clearly a water lily: a layered pink lotus bloom with a lighter center sits atop a flat round lily pad whose radial leaf segments and the classic single notch read correctly. —
 tile_fruit_apple

 tile_fruit_apple

 good — Instantly reads as a classic red apple: rounded silhouette, warm red body with a clean highlight band and darker shadow at the base, a brown stem and a single green leaf. —
 tile_fruit_coconut

 good — Reads as a coconut: round brown nut with subtle fibrous husk texture, a small top crown, and two glossy green leaves.  tile_fruit_golden_apple

—
 tile_fruit_golden_apple
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fruit_*
 good — Clearly a golden/magic apple: apple silhouette in saturated gold with a bright highlight, gold-tinted leaves, and small sparkle accents that signal 'special' without clutter.  tile_fruit_jackfruit

 tile_fruit_lemon
Vector icon (still) player-facingfruits
 good — Classic tapered lemon silhouette with a clean brown outline, a glossy top highlight and scattered pore dots that sell the citrus skin; the green leaf reads clearly. Instantly legible and well-centred on both backgrounds. — —
—
 tile_fruit_lemon
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fruit_*
 good — Instantly a lemon: oval yellow body with the telltale nipple tip, a clean highlight on the upper-left and warmer shadow below, plus a green leaf. —
 tile_fruit_pear
Vector icon (still) player-facingfruits
 good — Unmistakable pear silhouette (narrow top, bulbous bottom) with a confident dark-green outline, a soft highlight on the upper-left and pore dots adding believable skin texture. Well-framed and clearly distinct from the apple and lemon. — —
—
 tile_fruit_pear
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fruit_*
 good — Reads as a pear from the unmistakable narrow-top, wide-bottom silhouette, in a soft yellow-green with a long brown stem and a leaf. Shading gives it volume with a highlight up top and shadow at the base, and the muted hue still stays distinct from the grass pad thanks to the outline. Centred and clean with no fringing. — —
—
 tile_fruit_rambutan
Vector icon (still) player-facingfruits
 good — The glossy red core ringed by soft red-pink spines (spinterns) reads exactly as a rambutan and is the most distinctive icon in the set. Consistent light direction on both the core highlight and the spines, with a clean confident look. — —
—
 tile_fruit_rambutan
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fruit_*
 good — Clearly a rambutan: round red fruit covered in soft greenish-red hair-like spines (the defining trait), with a stem and leaf, plus a couple of small fallen fruit on the pad. The spiny halo is rendered as deliberate radiating strokes rather than random noise, keeping the silhouette legible. Centred on the grass oval with clean edges. — —
—
 tile_fruit_starfruit
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_fruit_*
 good — Reads as a starfruit: ribbed yellow oblong body with the lengthwise ridges, accompanied by a sliced star-shaped cross-section in front that makes the identity unambiguous, plus a leaf. The ribbing shading and the star slice are clean and deliberate, and the warm-yellow ramp gives good volume. Centred on the pad with crisp edges and no halo. — —
—
 tile_grain_buckwheat
Vector icon (still) player-facinggrain
 good — Clean confident vector with a sturdy green stem, two dark arrow-leaves and a tidy cluster of white five-petal flowers with brown centers; light direction and outline are consistent with the family. It reads slightly generic as a white wildflower rather than buckwheat specifically, but buckwheat does flower in small white clusters so it is acceptable. Well centred within the magenta box with no clipping. — —
—
 tile_grain_buckwheat
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grain_*
 good — Cohesive cream/ivory flower clusters on green-to-olive ramped stems sit cleanly centred on the oval grass pad, with a believable buckwheat-in-bloom silhouette. The stems are thin but read distinctly against the cell, and the shading has a soft warm-highlight/cool-shadow ramp. Edges are crisp with no visible halo against the dark side; one or two tiny stray flower specks at the top but negligible. — —
—
 tile_grain_corn
Vector icon (still) player-facinggrain
 good — Instantly reads as corn: gold kernel-textured cob with a brown tip, framed by two tall green husk leaves, strong contrast on both backgrounds. The kernel texture is a regular ring/bead pattern that looks a touch mechanical rather than organic, but it does not hurt legibility. Centred and filling the box cleanly. — —
—
 tile_grain_corn
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grain_*
 good — Instantly readable as corn — yellow ears with husk highlights, arching blade leaves in a green hue ramp, and a tasselled top, all centred on the grass pad. Silhouette is tall and clear at board size and the leaf shapes have deliberate light/shadow banding. No transparency fringing or stray pixels against the dark half. — —
—
 tile_grain_manna
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grain_*
 good — Soft golden feathery plumes fanning out from a green base read well as an ornamental/exotic grain, sitting centred on the pad. The plume texture uses a tan-to-cream ramp with subtle shading and reads cohesively at glance size. Silhouette is a touch soft/wispy by nature but stays clean with no halo or stray pixels on the dark side. — —
—
 tile_grain_rice
Vector icon (still) player-facinggrain
 good — Reads clearly as rice in a paddy: tall green stalks bending under golden ear clusters set over a blue water ellipse, with good light direction and clean outlines. The water base intentionally differs from the dirt-pad of its siblings, which reinforces the rice-paddy idea and reads well. Centred with the grain heads near the top edge but not clipping the magenta bounds. — —
—
 tile_grain_rice
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grain_*
 good — The arching fountain of green blades tipped with heavy drooping golden grain heads reads clearly as ripening rice and is well centred on the grass pad. Good green-to-gold gradient sells the ripening, and the bending arcs give it organic shape. Edges are crisp; the wide droopy spray is a bit busy but stays legible with no fringing against the dark half. — —
—
 tile_grain_wheat
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingFarm
 good — Clearly an ear of wheat: stacked golden grain kernels in a teardrop head, fine awns rising from the top, and green leaf blades splaying out at the base. Warm gold gradient with darker outlining reads well and is distinct from the flour sack's stamp. Centred and clean, with no muddy areas. — —
—
 tile_grain_wheat
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grain_*
 good — Textbook-readable wheat — upright golden stalks with bearded (awned) seed heads and a warm yellow-to-amber ramp, centred firmly on the grass pad. The seed-head detailing is deliberate and the silhouette is bold and the strongest-reading of the set. Clean edges, no transparency halo, no stray pixels. — —
—
 tile_grass_heather
Vector icon (still) player-facinggrass
 good — Tall green stalks (mixed dark/light green) fan up from a brown soil mound, topped with four purple five-petal flowers with pale-yellow centers; clean flat-vector shapes, confident outlines, consistent light direction, and reads well on both light and dark backgrounds. Well-centered in the magenta box with no clipping and a soft ground shadow. The blooms read as generic purple flowers rather than heather's tiny bell-like clustered florets, but for a stylized board tile this is an acceptable, attractive interpretation. Optional: tighten the four blooms into a denser clustered spray of smaller bells toward the stalk tips to read more specifically as heather rather than a generic flower. —
—
 tile_grass_heather
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grass_*
 good — Immediately reads as flowering heather: vertical green stalks topped with purple-magenta blossoms, good vertical rhythm and a clear silhouette. The purple has a sensible light-to-dark ramp, the green base ties to the pad, and the centred oval pad with tiny yellow accents is clean with no halo or stray pixels. — —
—
 tile_grass_meadow
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_grass_*
 good — Reads convincingly as tall meadow grass: arching blades with pale beige seed-head tips give it identity and separate it from the spiky tile, with layered greens reading as depth. Silhouette is crisp, blades have clean edges, and it sits centred on a tidy oval pad with no transparency fringing. — —
—
 tile_grass_spiky
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingFarm
 good — The sharp triangular spikes give this a genuinely different silhouette from the other two grass tiles - it reads as stiff, bladed/spiky tufts with pale-green chevron highlights catching light. Layered dark-and-light spikes on a soil mound; clean confident edges and centred framing. This is the most distinct of the grass trio. — —
—
 tile_herd_alpaca
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 good — The tall S-curved neck, cream fleece body and little face with a fringe tuft and stubby ears clearly read as an alpaca/llama, well distinguished from the other quadrupeds by its silhouette. Clean confident outline, warm gradient body, consistent soft shadow matching the family. Framing is good — the tall neck reaches near the top of the box but does not clip the magenta bounds. — —
—
 tile_herd_alpaca
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 good — Instantly reads as an alpaca/llama: caramel body with a clear neck, fluffy crown, and a lighter cream muzzle with two dark eye dots. Shading has a believable light-from-upper-left ramp, the silhouette is crisp, and it sits dead-centre on the flowered grass pad. No fringing or stray pixels against the dark side. — —
—
 tile_herd_goat
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 good — Clearly a goat: brown head with backswept horns, a beard, a yellow neck bell, and a white body with brown rear patch. Hue ramp and outlining are clean, the bell adds welcome character, and it sits centred on the pad. No haloing against the dark half. — —
—
 tile_herd_pig
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 good — Unambiguous pink body, pink disc snout with two nostrils, perky triangular ear, curly tail and trotters — instantly reads as a pig and is the clearest of the swine group. Clean outline, soft pink gradient with a tasteful highlight, consistent cast shadow, well-centred in the box. Strong, distinct family member. — —
—
 tile_herd_pig
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 good — Unmistakably a cosy pink pig: rounded body, snout with two nostrils, perky ears, curly tail and trotters, with soft pink-to-rose shading. Silhouette is crisp, it is centred on the flowered pad, and there is no fringing against the dark side. Cleanly distinct from the wild-pig tiles, which is exactly what the roster needs. — —
—
 tile_herd_ram
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 good — Fluffy cream fleece body with a bumpy cloud outline plus the defining large curled spiral horns frame the brown face — clearly a ram and well separated from the hornless sheep. Outline and shading are clean and the wool texture reads at size. The big horns are confidently drawn and the icon is centred without clipping. — —
—
 tile_herd_ram
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 good — Reads immediately as a ram thanks to the bold spiral horns, with a tan woolly body, darker face and clean legs. Shading is deliberate, the horns have a nice highlight ramp, and it sits centred on the pad with a crisp silhouette. No stray pixels or halo against the dark half. — —
—
 tile_herd_sheep
Vector icon (still) player-facingherdAnimals
 good — Classic fluffy white fleece with a cloud-bump outline, dark face with white eye and tiny ear/horn nub — reads immediately as a sheep and pairs naturally with the ram while staying distinct (no big horns, white vs cream fleece). Clean outline, nice soft fleece shading, consistent shadow, well-centred. Strong icon. — —
—
 tile_herd_sheep
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_herd_*
 good — Classic cosy sheep: cloud-like cream fleece with subtle shadow dimples, a dark face and ears, and small dark legs. The fleece texture is rendered cleanly without noise, the silhouette is crisp, and it is centred on the flowered pad. No fringing against the dark side. — —
—
 tile_mine_coal
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 good — A faceted near-black polygon rock with subtle grey facet seams and small pale specks, reading clearly as a lump of coal. Good restrained contrast keeps it legible on both grounds despite the dark value, and it is distinct from the grey ores by colour. The pale dot inclusions are a tad random but acceptable as mineral flecks. — —
—
 tile_mine_coal
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mine_*
 good — Clean faceted black rock with believable cool-grey highlights along the top facets that read clearly as glossy coal; silhouette is crisp and the green grass pad sits neatly centred beneath it. Edges are tight against the dark side with no visible halo or stray pixels. The facets are a touch flat/low-contrast in the lower mass, but it reads instantly and looks game-ready. — —
—
 tile_mine_copper_ore
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 good — A warm brown faceted rock with a bright wavy copper-gold vein band across the middle and small green (malachite) and tan flecks, which reads convincingly as copper ore. The warm palette cleanly separates it from the grey-ore siblings and the vein motif ties it to iron/silver. Clean outline and good top-light; framing is well centred. — —
—
 tile_mine_copper_ore
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mine_*
 good — Vivid teal/green crystal cluster bursting from a grey stone base reads clearly as a gem-ore deposit, with decent faceted highlights on the crystals and a warm-grey rock that contrasts well. Subject is centred on the grass pad and the silhouette is clean against the dark half. Colour leans more emerald than 'copper' so the metal identity is ambiguous, but as an ore tile it is polished. If copper identity matters, warm the crystals toward orange/turquoise-copper-oxide rather than pure emerald green to better signal copper. —
—
 tile_mine_gem
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 good — A cyan pentagonal crystal with a dark teal outline, large faceted planes and a couple of white highlight glints, reading as a raw/uncut gem and clearly distinct from the polished cut_gem. Strong saturated contrast holds up on both grounds. Facets are clean and the form is well centred. — —
—
 tile_mine_gem
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mine_*
 good — A bright sapphire-blue rectangular gem with clean bevelled facets sits in a grey stone cradle, instantly readable as a gem and the strongest-contrast piece in the set. Centred on the pad with a crisp silhouette and no fringing against the dark side. Shading on the surrounding stone is a bit flat and uniform, but the focal gem is sharp and game-ready. — —
—
 tile_mine_iron_ore
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mine_*
 good — A single chunky grey boulder with strong diagonal orange/rust veins reads clearly as iron ore, and the warm vein colour contrasts well against the cool stone. Silhouette is bold and crisp, centred on the grass pad with small rubble pebbles at the base and no edge fringing. The veins are slightly bright/saturated (almost lava-like), but it stays legible and polished. — —
—
 tile_mine_stone
Vector icon (still) player-facingexistingMine
 good — A grey multi-faceted boulder with clear facet seams, a few small flecks and a clean top-light gradient, reading plainly as a plain stone/rock. It is distinct from the iron/silver ores because it has no metallic vein band, just dry faceted stone. Confident outline, well centred and legible on both grounds. — —
—
 tile_mine_stone
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mine_*
 good — A neutral grey boulder built from cobbled facets reads correctly as plain stone, with soft top-light highlights and darker grounding at the base. It is centred on the grass pad with a clean silhouette and no halo against the dark half. The faceting is a bit soft/repetitive and there is faint debug-style text baked along the bottom of the pad, but the rock itself is clean and game-ready. Check the strip render for faint text/watermark artifact along the bottom of the pad and remove it if it is not part of the source tile. —
—
 tile_mount_donkey
Vector icon (still) player-facingmounts
 good — The grey body plus two upright pointed ears reads cleanly as a donkey at icon size, and the cool grey distinguishes it from the brown horse sibling. Outline is confident, the bean body and blocky legs match the family style, and framing is centred within the magenta box. The pose (head/neck reaching down-forward) is a touch awkward but stays legible. — —
—
 tile_mount_donkey
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mount_*
 good — Reads instantly as a donkey: correct grey body, darker mane and muzzle, upright ears and slim legs all legible at board size. Shading is clean with a soft belly shadow and no visible noise; silhouette is crisp with no fringing or stray pixels against the dark side. It sits well centred on the green flower-dotted pad. — —
—
 tile_mount_horse
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mount_*
 good — Clearly a chestnut horse with a black mane, tail and lower legs, white blaze and four white socks; proportions and pose read well at tile size. Hue ramp on the brown coat is deliberate with warm highlights and cool shadow, edges are crisp and the silhouette is clean against both halves. Centred on the pad, though the long tail pushes slightly toward the right edge. — —
—
 tile_mount_mammoth
Vector icon (still) player-facingmounts
 good — The radiating fur-spike fringe around the silhouette plus the pale curved tusks and small rounded ear read convincingly as a woolly mammoth and clearly differentiate it from the smooth-bodied horse/donkey. Light direction and the brown gradient are consistent with the family, outline is clean. Framing is well-centred; the tusks stay inside the magenta bounds. — —
—
 tile_mount_mammoth
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mount_*
 good — Unmistakably a woolly mammoth: shaggy brown coat, domed head, raised trunk and two large pale curved tusks all read clearly. Fur is shaded with a believable hue ramp, the silhouette is crisp and tusks pop cleanly; no stray pixels or halo on the dark side. Well centred on the pad. — —
—
 tile_mount_moose
Vector icon (still) player-facingmounts
 good — The broad palmate (paddle) antlers are the strongest subject cue in the whole family and read instantly as a moose, with the dark muzzle/dewlap reinforcing it. Body, legs and tail match the family style with a clean confident outline and consistent brown shading. Antlers fill the upper box without clipping the magenta bounds; composition is balanced. — —
—
 tile_mount_moose
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_mount_*
 good — Reads as a moose at a glance thanks to the broad palmate antlers, dark brown body, dewlap and long legs. The very dark coat is a touch low-contrast against the dark half of the cell so some body detail flattens, but the silhouette and antlers stay legible; no fringing or stray pixels. Centred on the pad with antlers spreading near but not over the top edge. Lift the body shadows a half-step or add a subtle rim highlight so the dark coat separates better from dark backgrounds on the board. —
—
 tile_special_dirt
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_special_*
 good — A rounded dark-brown soil mound with a small two-leaf green sprout sits centred on a green grassy pad with a few dark pebble accents; volume reads via darker shadowing at the base and a lighter top. Silhouette is clean and crisp against the dark half, with no visible AA halo or stray pixels. The sprout adds clear 'plantable plot' meaning and the cosy palette is consistent. — —
—
 tile_special_giant_pearl
Vector icon (still) player-facingfish
 good — A big pale-lavender pearl with a soft cross-shaped specular highlight nestled in a small dark-rimmed tan shell base; the scale and glow sell it as a special/giant pearl, distinct from the small oyster pearl. Clean rendering and good contrast on both backgrounds. Centered and well framed. — —
—
 tile_special_giant_pearl
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_special_*
 good — An open cream/tan fluted scallop shell cradles a large pale pearl with a crisp specular highlight, centred on the green pad. The ribbed shell shading and rim give it volume, the pearl reads as round and lustrous, and the silhouette is clean with no transparency fringing against the dark side. Distinctive and premium-feeling while staying within the cosy farm palette. — —
—
 tile_tree_birch
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_tree_*
 good — Instantly reads as a birch: the pale trunk with dark bark flecks against airy lime-green foliage is unmistakable and distinct from the other conifers. Pixel craft is clean with a clear light-from-upper-left ramp on the canopy, a tidy silhouette, and a well-defined oval grass pad with a few flower/pebble accents. The subject sits centred on the pad and I see no transparency fringing or stray pixels against the dark side. — —
—
 tile_tree_cypress
Vector icon (still) player-facingtrees
 good — The tall pointed bullet silhouette with dark outline, horizontal lighter-green banding and a few orange dots reads instantly as a slim cypress/conifer and is clearly distinct from every sibling. Light direction is consistent and contrast against both backgrounds is strong. Well framed and centered within the box. — —
—
 tile_tree_cypress
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_tree_*
 good — Clean tapered conical conifer with believable layered foliage clumps and a darker core for depth, reading clearly as a slim cypress that contrasts well with the fir and oak. Silhouette is crisp, shading uses cool dark greens with subtle lighter edges, and there is no visible halo or stray pixels on the dark half. It is centred on a small green pad, though the pad is the least decorated of the set (nearly plain grass). — —
—
 tile_tree_fir
Vector icon (still) player-facingtrees
 good — The four-tier stacked triangular conifer with a red/gold star ornament on top reads immediately as a fir/Christmas tree and is the most charming of the set. Tiers have clean dark outlines and a believable top-left light, and the little brown base anchors it. The decorative star may push it toward a holiday read rather than a plain fir, but it is distinct and well crafted. — —
—
 tile_tree_fir
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_tree_*
 good — Classic tiered fir silhouette with distinct drooping branch layers and a small trunk peeking at the base, clearly distinguishable from the narrower cypress. Foliage uses a decent dark-to-mid green ramp with lighter sunlit edges, the silhouette is crisp, and the grass pad with a couple of accent dots reads cleanly. Subject is centred on the pad and I see no fringing or halo against the dark side. — —
—
 tile_tree_oak
Vector icon (still) player-facingtrees
 good — A bold lobed round canopy of green spheres over a thick ribbed brown trunk reads cleanly as a classic broadleaf oak, with confident dark outline and consistent top-left highlights. Contrast is strong on both backgrounds and framing is well centered. Its main weakness is shared-DNA with the birch canopy, but the heavier crown and brown trunk keep it the more 'oak'-like of the pair. — —
—
 tile_tree_oak
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_tree_*
 good — Strong broadleaf oak: a chunky brown trunk with root flare supports a rounded clustered canopy rendered as deliberate dark/mid/light-green leaf clumps, giving good volume and a clear cosy-tree silhouette. Pixel clusters are clean rather than noisy, the light direction is consistent, and the green pad with small accents is centred under it. No transparency fringe or stray pixels visible on the dark side. — —
—
 tile_tree_palm
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_tree_*
 good — Unmistakable palm: a gently curved tapered brown trunk with segment banding topped by several radiating green fronds, clearly distinct from every other tree in the batch. Fronds have a clean mid/dark-green ramp and crisp pointed tips, the silhouette is sharp, and the trunk curve is centred so the canopy sits well over the grass pad. I see no anti-alias halo or stray pixels against the dark half. — —
—
 tile_tree_willow/idle-autumn
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_tree_willow
 good — Rich autumn gold-to-amber canopy with hue-shifted highlights and clean drooping fronds; the trailing wisps sway subtly across the 8 frames and a scatter of fallen orange leaves animates on the pad. Identity and centring hold; motion is organic, not a slide. — —
—
 tile_tree_willow/idle-spring
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_tree_willow
 good — Vivid spring green with a slightly lighter, fresher palette than summer and tasteful pink blossom specks plus tiny white petals on the ground for seasonal cue. The cascading fronds bob gently and seamlessly; pad and trunk stay centred. Clean clusters, good readability at tile size. — —
—
 tile_tree_willow/idle-summer
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_tree_willow
 good — Deeper saturated summer greens with strong volume and well-anchored brown trunk; the 9 frames show a soft cantilever sway in the trailing fronds that reads as a breeze rather than a rigid shift. Centring is solid and the canopy silhouette stays consistent. Polished and game-ready. — —
—
 tile_tree_willow/idle-winter
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_tree_willow
 good — Bare brown branch armature with white snow caps on the fronds and a clean snow mound at the base; the season reads instantly and the subject is unmistakably the same willow. Slight branch settle/sway across frames keeps it from feeling frozen. Edges are crisp and the pad stays centred. — —
—
 tile_tree_willow/trans-autumn-winter
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_tree_willow
 good — Across 33 frames the amber canopy thins, fronds go bare-brown with orange leaf-litter pooling on the pad, then snow whitens the branches and ground in a believable progression; identity is preserved throughout. Measured luminance rises smoothly to the snowy end (max frame jump ~4.6, no white-out or bloom spike). Pad stays centred; no popping. — —
—
 tile_tree_willow/trans-spring-summer
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_tree_willow
 good — The 17 frames darken and saturate the canopy from fresh spring green to summer's deeper green while the blossom flecks fade out; the shape and trunk hold steady and the pad grass thickens slightly. Luminance descends monotonically (132 to 104, max jump 4.4) so there is no flicker or overshoot, and the final frame matches the summer idle. A subtle but clean handoff. — —
—
 tile_tree_willow/trans-summer-autumn
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_tree_willow
 good — Over 16 frames the canopy warms convincingly from summer green through yellow-green to the full gold of the autumn idle, with orange leaf-litter beginning to gather on the pad; identity is intact. Luminance rises gently and plateaus (max jump 6, no spike), so there is no white-out or popping. Centring and silhouette stay stable across the colour shift. — —
—
 tile_veg_beet
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_veg_*
 good — Reads instantly as a beet: glossy crimson bulb with a clean highlight band and well-articulated green leaf cluster with red stems above. The silhouette is crisp, shading uses a believable dark-to-light ramp, and it sits centred on the round grass pad with a few sparkle accents. Edges are clean against the dark side with no obvious fringing. — —
—
 tile_veg_broccoli
Vector icon (still) player-facingvegetables
 good — Instantly reads as broccoli: a cluster of bumpy dark-green florets atop a pale yellow-green trimmed stalk. Good craft with stippled floret texture, clean outline, and clear light direction. Distinct from every sibling. — —
—
 tile_veg_broccoli
Board tile (still) player-facingtile_veg_*
 good — Clearly a broccoli crown: bumpy multi-tone green floret clusters with a chunky cream stalk, sitting centred on the grass pad. Slightly busy/dark at small sizes but still parses as broccoli. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot
Vector icon (still) player-facingvegetables
 good — Unmistakable carrot: tapered orange root with vertical highlight ribs and feathery green leaf fronds up top. Clean outline, warm consistent shading, fills the box well. Strong and distinct. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot/idle-autumn
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_veg_carrot
 good — Nine-frame loop with a subtle bob and gentle leaf-frond sway plus a tiny falling-leaf/spark accent drifting near the pad; motion is organic, not a rigid slide. Carrot stays centred on the brown autumn pad with a fallen-leaf dressing across frames, and the loop reads seamlessly. The two small white comma-shaped glyphs mid-strip are intentional spark/leaf overlay accents, not flicker artifacts. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot/idle-spring
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_veg_carrot
 good — Single static frame (correct for the spring fallback), clean pixel craft with a vivid orange ramp, green grass pad and a small pink flower for seasonal flavour. The carrot is well centred and the top fronds read clearly as carrot greens. No animation expected here and none needed. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot/idle-summer
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_veg_carrot
 good — Nine-frame loop with a gentle bob and lively leaf sway plus small yellow sparkle accents twinkling on the green pad; motion is subtle and organic, not sliding. The carrot keeps a brighter, glossier summer highlight and stays centred on the pad throughout. Reads as a clean seamless idle. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot/idle-winter
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_veg_carrot
 good — Single static frame (correct winter fallback) showing the carrot with blue-frosted fronds, a snow cap on the greens and a clean white snow pad. Pixel craft is solid and the subject identity is unmistakably the same carrot. Centred and game-ready. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot/trans-autumn-winter
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_veg_carrot
 good — Across 17 frames the dirt pad whitens to snow, the green fronds tint cool blue and accumulate a growing snow cap, and frost speckles appear on the carrot body; progression is smooth with no white-out, bloom or popping. The final frame lands almost exactly on the winter idle and the carrot identity is preserved throughout. Pad stays centred with no jumping. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot/trans-spring-summer
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_veg_carrot
 good — Seventeen frames show the spring blossom fading, the fronds becoming fuller and the carrot body gaining a brighter glossy summer highlight while the grass pad deepens; the change is gradual and the subject identity holds. No white-out, flicker or pad jump. A relatively understated transition since both endpoints are green-pad states, but it reads correctly. — —
—
 tile_veg_carrot/trans-summer-autumn
Board tile (animated) player-facingtile_veg_carrot
 good — Over 17 frames the lush green grass pad shifts to brown autumn dirt and orange fallen leaves accumulate around the base while the fronds dull from bright green; progression is smooth and the carrot stays consistent and centred. No white-out, bloom or popping. Lands cleanly on the autumn idle state. — —
